His final statement — claiming Iran's missile production 'score is 20' and unaffected by war — came hours before his death. Israel said Naini was the 'IRGC's main propagandist.' His death is the fourth senior Iranian official killed by Israel this week.
factCheckNote: IRGC confirmed Naini's death; IDF confirmed the strike

title: IDF launches Nowruz strikes on heart of Tehran
description: Israel launched waves of strikes on Tehran on Nowruz — the Persian New Year coinciding with Eid al-Fitr — saying it targeted 'infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran.' Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent described the capital as 'hushed,' with none of the customary festivities. Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei had not been seen publicly since succeeding his father.
factCheckNote: IDF social media announcement; Al Jazeera Tehran correspondent confirmed

title: Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery struck again by Iranian drones
description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery complex (730,000 bpd capacity) again, sparking fires. Emergency crews worked to contain the blaze. Kuwait summoned Iran's ambassador. The refinery had already been targeted in previous days, exacerbating the regional energy crisis.
factCheckNote: Kuwaiti authorities confirmed the strike and emergency response

title: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first public statement as Supreme Leader
description: New Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, issued his first public statement since succeeding his father Ali Khamenei: 'Iran's enemies must have their security taken away.' The statement, responding to Israel's targeting of senior officials, signaled Iran's intent to continue fighting. Khamenei had not been seen publicly for 21 days since assuming power.
factCheckNote: Statement reported by Al Jazeera, NPR, and other outlets

title: Lebanon death toll surpasses 1,000 — 1 million displaced
description: Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed 1,001 people killed and 2,584 wounded since Israel renewed widespread attacks on the country. At least 118 children are among the dead. Over one million Lebanese — one-sixth of the population — have been displaced. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun renewed calls for a truce, while France's envoy said disarming Hezbollah while Lebanon is being bombed is 'unreasonable.'
factCheckNote: Lebanon Health Ministry official figures

title: Brent crude at $110.28 — US weighing sanctions relief on 140M barrels Iranian oil
description: Brent crude futures rose 1.3% to $110.28/barrel as Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed the US may lift sanctions on ~140 million barrels of Iranian crude stranded on tankers. 'We will be using the Iranian barrels against the Iranians to keep the price down,' Bessent said. WTI rose 0.1% to $96.20. Saudi officials warned oil could hit $180 if disruptions persist through April.
factCheckNote: Bessent statement on Fox Business; CNBC oil price reporting

title: Iran fires 8th missile salvo since midnight — fragments 400m from Western Wall
description: Iran launched its eighth ballistic missile attack on Israel since midnight on Nowruz/Eid al-Fitr. Sirens activated across the southern Negev and northern Israel. An intercepted missile left fragments in the Jewish Quarter of Jerusalem's Old City, approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall. A separate impact was reported in Rehovot. No Israeli casualties were reported from this specific wave. The strikes coincided with two major religious holidays — Nowruz and Eid al-Fitr.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog and CBS News confirmed missile fragments in Jerusalem Old City area

title: IDF kills Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled in Beirut
description: The IDF announced the killing of Hussein Makled, head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters, in overnight airstrikes on Beirut. Israel simultaneously issued new evacuation orders for southern Lebanese villages ahead of expanded ground operations. Lebanon's overall death toll passed 1,001, with more than 1,049,328 displaced — nearly one in five Lebanese. The UN warned of catastrophic humanitarian consequences as Beirut's southern suburbs were struck repeatedly.
factCheckNote: IDF announced killing; Anadolu Agency and Al Jazeera confirmed

title: Iran's Gen. Shekarchi threatens global tourist destinations on Nowruz
description: Iranian military spokesman Gen. Abolfazl Shekarchi issued a sweeping threat on Nowruz: 'From now on, based on the information we have about you, even parks, recreational areas and tourist destinations anywhere in the world will no longer be safe for you.' The unprecedented global threat targeting civilian infrastructure was condemned internationally. Hours later, the IRGC spokesperson who claimed Iran was 'producing missiles even during war conditions' was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
factCheckNote: Statement reported by Military.com, Reuters, multiple outlets

title: Trump calls NATO 'COWARDS' for refusing Hormuz — 'NATO IS A PAPER TIGER'
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social calling NATO allies 'COWARDS' for not joining efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz: 'Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices... COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER!' He added: 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!' The post came after six allies (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan) offered limited Hormuz assistance only after a ceasefire — conditions Trump rejected. The outburst marked the sharpest US attack on NATO since the war began.
factCheckNote: Truth Social post confirmed by Bloomberg, CNN, Al Jazeera

title: IDF claims air supremacy over Iran — 70% of missile launchers destroyed, 3+ more weeks planned
description: The IDF announced it had achieved air supremacy over most of Iran's airspace after destroying more than 100 Iranian air defense systems and approximately 120 detection systems. The IDF claimed approximately 70% of Iran's estimated 500 ballistic missile launchers had been destroyed or disabled. Brig. Gen. Defrin stated the IDF plans at least three more weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industry through Passover (April 12). The US also confirmed deployment of an additional 2,500 Marines and the USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group to the Middle East, bringing total US troop presence to ~50,000.
factCheckNote: Israeli military claims — independently unverifiable; IDF has history of overstating damage

title: Trump signals 'winding down' of Iran war — calls NATO 'COWARDS' for refusing to help reopen Hormuz
description: President Trump sent conflicting signals on Day 21: posting on Truth Social that the US is 'getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts,' while simultaneously blasting NATO allies as 'COWARDS' for refusing to commit forces to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking to Naval Academy midshipmen, Trump stated: 'Their leaders are all gone. The next set of leaders are all gone. And the next set of leaders are mostly gone... We want to talk to them and there's nobody to talk to.' Trump ruled out ground troops, saying 'I'm not putting troops anywhere.' However, the Pentagon was simultaneously preparing detailed ground force plans including 82nd Airborne deployment scenarios and JSOC nuclear material extraction operations.
factCheckNote: Trump claims war is 'militarily won' — but Hegseth's own data shows strike frequency 'ebbed and flowed,' Hormuz remains effectively closed, and Iran continues missile attacks. IAEA disputes Netanyahu's claim that Iran 'can no longer enrich uranium.'

title: Iran fires IRBMs at Diego Garcia — demonstrates previously unknown 4,000km range capability
description: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the British Indian Ocean Territory — the longest-range Iranian strike ever attempted at approximately 4,000 km, double Iran's previously claimed 2,000 km range. The attack came hours after UK PM Starmer gave the US permission to use the base for strikes on Iranian missile sites. One missile failed mid-flight; a US Navy destroyer fired an SM-3 interceptor at the second. Neither missile struck the base. The attack marks a significant escalation, opening a new geographic front far from the Persian Gulf and demonstrating an IRBM capability Iran had not previously acknowledged.
factCheckNote: Reported by WSJ citing multiple US officials; no official US/UK/Iran confirmation as of publish time. Range claim (4,000km) would represent a major strategic revelation if confirmed.

title: Iran fires cluster missiles at central Israel — Rishon Lezion daycare hit, debris falls near Al-Aqsa
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at central Israel on Day 22. A cluster munition struck an empty daycare in Rishon Lezion — no casualties. Debris from an intercepted ballistic missile landed in Jerusalem's Old City near the Temple Mount and Al-Aqsa Mosque, damaging a road and wall in the Jewish Quarter. The IDF identified three rounds of missile fire before midnight and another barrage hours later.
factCheckNote: Rishon Lezion daycare damage confirmed by Israeli emergency services. Jerusalem debris confirmed by Israeli police and TOI liveblog.

title: Natanz nuclear facility struck by US-Israel — Iran reports no radioactive leakage
description: US and Israeli forces conducted airstrikes on the Natanz uranium enrichment facility on Day 22. Iranian state media agency Tasnim reported 'no radioactive leakage' from the site. Natanz was Iran's primary centrifuge enrichment complex. The strike follows earlier attacks on Fordow and Isfahan nuclear sites.
factCheckNote: Strike confirmed by Al Jazeera and TOI liveblogs. 'No radioactive leakage' claim from Tasnim (Iranian state media) — independent verification impossible due to internet blackout.

title: UK authorizes US to use Diego Garcia for strikes on Iranian Hormuz missile sites
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer formally authorized the United States to use Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK base in the British Indian Ocean Territory, for airstrikes against Iranian missile sites threatening the Strait of Hormuz. The decision represents a significant escalation in UK involvement. Iran's FM Araghchi warned that UK bases would become 'legitimate targets' as a result. The authorization came hours after Iran had already fired two IRBMs at Diego Garcia.
factCheckNote: Authorization confirmed by ITV News and GB News citing UK government statements.

title: Araghchi: Iran 'seeks to end the war permanently' — demands reparations, no US talks
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Iran seeks to 'end the war permanently' but set conditions: a decisive and final end to attacks plus war reparations. He dismissed US negotiations, saying there is 'no good experience in talking with the Americans.' Reports emerged simultaneously of a backchannel Iranian intelligence-CIA contact through a third country to discuss terms. The signals represent Iran's first significant diplomatic signaling since the war began.
factCheckNote: Araghchi 'end war permanently' statement reported by Al Arabiya — not independently confirmed by official Iranian statement as of press time.

title: IDF strikes Bandar Anzali naval base on Caspian Sea — corvette, 4 missile boats destroyed
description: Israeli forces struck Iran's Caspian Sea naval base at Bandar Anzali, destroying a corvette, four missile boats, auxiliary vessels, a shipyard, and a command center. This marks the first IDF strike on Iran's northern Caspian coast. CENTCOM's Admiral Brad Cooper separately confirmed that Iran's Navy 'is not sailing' and its naval capability has been effectively eliminated.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed Caspian strikes via Lt. Col. Shoshani. Times of Israel liveblog corroborated.

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 51 drones over Eastern Province — largest single Saudi intercept of the war
description: Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed the interception of 51 Iranian drones over the Eastern Province on March 21, coinciding with Nowruz and Eid al-Fitr. This is the largest single-day Saudi aerial interception since the conflict began. The Eastern Province hosts Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura refinery and Abqaiq processing complex. No infrastructure damage or casualties reported.
factCheckNote: Saudi Defense Ministry official statement confirmed by Anadolu Agency.

title: 22 nations issue joint statement condemning Hormuz blockade — US and China absent
description: A coalition of 22 countries including UAE, UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada, South Korea and others issued a joint statement condemning Iran's attacks on commercial shipping and the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The statement called for an immediate moratorium on attacks on civilian infrastructure. Notably, neither the US nor China signed. Iran dismissed the statement as 'interventionist.'
factCheckNote: Joint statement text confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog, India TV News, and GOV.UK.

title: CENTCOM: Iran's military capabilities reduced to 10% — missile rate down 90%
description: US CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper held a press briefing declaring Iran's military has been degraded to approximately 10% of pre-war capacity. He stated Iran's Navy is not sailing, tactical fighters are not flying, and missile/drone launch rates have fallen 90% and 83% respectively from Day 1. US-Israel strikes have destroyed 77% of tunnel entrances at 27 Iranian underground bases. A US strike also dropped multiple 5,000-pound bombs on an underground coastal weapons storage facility.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM official press briefing confirmed by ABC News, NPR, and CNN.

title: Trump: 'I think we've won' — declares Iran's military obliterated, signals war winding down
description: Speaking to reporters and on Truth Social, President Trump declared: 'I think we've won. We've knocked out their navy, their air force. We've knocked out their anti-aircraft. We've knocked out everything. We're roaming free.' He also stated the US is 'getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts.' On the Strait of Hormuz: 'The Hormuz Strait will have to be guarded and policed, as necessary, by other Nations who use it — The United States does not.' Simultaneously, 2,200–2,500 additional Marines departed for the region.
factCheckNote: Trump statements confirmed by Axios, CNBC, NBC News, Al Jazeera. Marine deployment simultaneously confirmed by NBC News and Military.com.

title: Israel Katz: attacks will increase significantly — directly contradicts Trump winding down
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz issued a statement declaring that US-Israel attacks on Iran will "increase significantly" this week, directly contradicting Trump's "winding down" post from hours earlier. Katz: "This week, the intensity of the attacks that the IDF and the U.S. military will carry out against the Iranian terrorist regime and against the infrastructures on which it relies will increase significantly, and the campaign led by U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will continue." The contradiction illustrated a widening strategic gap between US and Israeli war objectives on Day 22.
factCheckNote: Katz statement confirmed by US News/AP and TASS. Contradiction with Trump well-documented by multiple outlets.

title: Nowruz: Pezeshkian proposes "Islamic Assembly of Middle East" — Iran's first peace framework
description: On the Persian New Year (Nowruz), Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian delivered a major address proposing the creation of an "Islamic Assembly of the Middle East" as a regional security and cooperation structure, explicitly calling for foreign powers to leave the region. He rejected claims that Iran is the source of regional instability, blaming Israel instead. The proposal represents Iran's first concrete diplomatic framework offered since the war began, though analysts noted it implicitly demands US withdrawal as a precondition. Pezeshkian also referenced Iranian civilian casualties, including the strike on a kindergarten.
factCheckNote: Nowruz address confirmed by ANI and Tribune India citing Iranian state media. Consistent with prior Iranian diplomatic messaging.

title: IAEA confirms no radiation leak from Natanz 2nd strike — nuclear capabilities assessed "significantly set back"
description: The IAEA confirmed its monitoring equipment showed no off-site radiation increase following the second airstrike on Natanz. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization of Iran said nuclear material was safe and there was no radiation leak. The IDF denied involvement, with Israeli officials stating they had "no knowledge" of the Natanz strike, strongly suggesting it was a unilateral US strike using bunker-buster bombs. Independent nuclear experts assessed Iran's enrichment capacity was significantly degraded but warned that pre-positioned enriched uranium stockpiles may have survived, and Iran retains nuclear knowledge. A full reconstitution could take years; a partial restart could begin within months.
factCheckNote: IAEA statement confirmed by Washington Examiner and Al Jazeera. IDF denial reported by Jerusalem Post.

title: Araghchi on NBC: Iran demands "permanent end to war" — rejects all ceasefire proposals
description: Iranian FM Araghchi appeared on NBC's Meet the Press, flatly rejecting any ceasefire and demanding a "complete, comprehensive and lasting end to the war" with reparations. "We welcome any initiative that can fully end this war; we are ready to listen and consider. However, at present, while some countries are trying to find a solution, the United States does not appear ready to halt its aggression." He also denied any contact with US envoy Steve Witkoff since the war began: "My last contact with Witkoff was prior to his employer's decision to kill diplomacy with another illegal military attack on Iran." On Hormuz: "The strait is only closed to enemy ships."
factCheckNote: NBC Meet the Press appearance confirmed by India TV News, Social News XYZ, and NBC News.

title: Iranian IRBM direct hit on Arad — 74 injured, Israeli air defense fails for first time
description: An Iranian intermediate-range ballistic missile carrying a warhead 'weighing hundreds of kilograms' struck the center of Arad, between apartment buildings, injuring 74 people (7 serious, 15 moderate, 52 light) — the highest single-attack casualty count of the war. Critically, Israeli air defense systems were activated but failed to intercept the missile. IDF Spokesperson Effie Defrin acknowledged: 'The air defense systems were activated but did not intercept the missile.' The IDF opened a formal investigation into the failure. Netanyahu called the evening 'very difficult.' The attack came hours after Israeli Defense Minister Katz declared strikes on Iran would 'increase significantly' this week, and reveals continued Iranian capability to penetrate Israeli air defenses despite claims of 90% degradation.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, CBS News, IDF spokesperson statement. Casualty figures from MDA (Magen David Adom).

title: Iran strikes Dimona — first ballistic missile to hit Israel's nuclear city
description: A separate Iranian ballistic missile struck a one-story building in Dimona, a city of 36,000 adjacent to the Negev Nuclear Research Center — Israel's only nuclear facility. 47 people were injured including a 10-year-old boy in serious condition and a woman moderately wounded. The IAEA stated it received 'no indication of damage to the nuclear research center.' Israeli Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf said: 'The skies of Israel are unprotected' — suggesting Iran has pre-prepared targeting plans for Israel's nuclear assets. This is the first time in the war Iran has struck a city housing Israel's nuclear infrastructure. Combined with the Arad interception failure, the Day 22 evening strikes represent a significant escalation in Iran's ability to penetrate southern Israel's air defense coverage.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Euronews, Times of Israel, IAEA statement, Ghalibaf post confirmed by Haaretz.

title: IDF strikes Malek Ashtar University — Tehran nuclear weapons R&D facility destroyed
description: The IDF struck the Malek Ashtar University of Technology in Tehran, a facility used to develop nuclear weapons components and ballistic missiles under IRGC supervision. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir called the strike 'extensive' damage to a 'strategic' target sanctioned by the West for decades. The IDF also struck Iranian air defense batteries covering the Tehran area in the same operation. The Malek Ashtar complex was a key node in Iran's nuclear weaponization R&D chain, developing precision guidance systems, fusing mechanisms, and missile reentry vehicle components. This was among the highest-value targeting of Iran's nuclear weapons program in the war so far.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IDF statement, Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel. Malek Ashtar University long-sanctioned by US/EU.

title: US-Israeli strike kills child at Ahvaz entertainment complex — hospital damaged in Andimeshk
description: A US-Israeli airstrike struck the Ritaj entertainment complex in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province, killing one child and injuring both parents. The strike on a civilian entertainment venue drew international condemnation. Separately, a strike damaged the Imam Ali Hospital in Andimeshk, in the same province, forcing it to cease accepting new patients. An unexploded projectile struck a residential building in Aghajari with no casualties. Khuzestan Province's deputy governor confirmed the deaths. The strikes highlight ongoing risks to civilian infrastructure in Iran's western provinces.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Anadolu Agency reporting Iranian deputy governor statement. Consistent with ongoing strikes in Khuzestan.

title: Houthis threaten to close Bab el-Mandeb Strait — 'double chokepoint' scenario emerges
description: Houthi political bureau member Mohammed al-Bukhaiti and military official Abed al-Thawr declared the group is actively weighing closure of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, the Red Sea chokepoint through which 12% of global trade passes. The Houthis cited solidarity with Iran and declared 'Hour Zero' military readiness. Any closure would initially target US and Israeli-linked vessels. Combined with the already-closed Strait of Hormuz (Day 19), a Bab el-Mandeb closure would create an unprecedented 'double chokepoint' scenario — cutting off both the Persian Gulf route and the Mediterranean/Suez route simultaneously. Major shipping carriers Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM had already paused Trans-Suez sailings. NBC News assessed this could be 'the next choke point for the global economy.'
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Africanews, CGTN, NBC News. Houthi statement from named officials. Shipping suspensions separately confirmed.

title: Trump issues 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum — threatens to obliterate Iran's power plants
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social at 7:44 PM ET: 'If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!' The 48-hour deadline expires ~7:44 PM ET Monday, March 23. Trump also rejected ceasefire talks: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.' This came hours after Trump had signaled 'winding down' operations, creating confusion about US war aims. The ultimatum represented the first explicit US threat to target Iranian civilian energy infrastructure.
factCheckNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by Fox News, Bloomberg, CNN, NBC. Time of post confirmed at 7:44 PM ET. Deadline calculated as ~7:44 PM ET March 23.

title: IDF Chief Zamir: Campaign at 'halfway stage' — plans 3+ more weeks of operations
description: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir declared the military campaign against Iran is at its 'halfway stage.' The IDF plans at least 3 more weeks of systematic strikes. Key statistics cited: ~70% of Iran's 500 estimated ballistic missile launchers destroyed or disabled; ~80% of Iran's air defense systems destroyed. US CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper said 8,000+ targets struck, including 130 Iranian vessels destroyed — 'the largest elimination of a navy in a three-week period since World War II.' Zamir added that Iran's IRBM fire toward Diego Garcia shows 'Berlin, Paris, and Rome are all within range.' The statement directly contradicted Trump's 'winding down' narrative from March 20-21.
factCheckNote: Zamir statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. CENTCOM figure from Adm. Cooper statement. IDF statistics consistent with ongoing operations reporting.

title: Iran military issues counter-threat: US energy, IT, and desalination infrastructure will be targeted
description: In direct response to Trump's 48-hour power plant ultimatum, the Iranian military issued a formal counter-threat via Fars News Agency: if Iran's energy infrastructure is struck, 'all energy, information technology and desalination infrastructure belonging to the US and the regime in the region will be targeted.' The threat covers Gulf state desalination plants that produce virtually all drinking water for Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, and Kuwait. Loss of desalination capacity would constitute an existential threat to Gulf civilian populations. Trump's deadline expires March 23 evening — analysts assessed Iran was unlikely to comply with the Hormuz demand.
factCheckNote: Iranian military statement via Fars News Agency confirmed by CNN live updates and NBC News. Gulf desalination dependence is documented fact.

title: USS Boxer and 11th MEU deploy from San Diego — US builds ground-assault capability
description: USS Boxer (LHD-4), an amphibious assault ship, departed San Diego with the 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit carrying approximately 2,200 Marines. Combined with a second MEU also deploying, total additional US Marines headed to the Middle East is ~5,000. The US now has over 50,000 troops total in the region. The Pentagon confirmed no decision to deploy ground troops into Iran, but the amphibious deployment creates a viable coastal assault option and escalates pressure on Tehran. Analysts noted the deployment could also support a potential Hormuz mine-clearing operation.
factCheckNote: USS Boxer deployment confirmed by Military Times, Stars and Stripes, PBS News. Ground option framing confirmed by military.com reporting.

title: Israel strikes Malek Ashtar University weapons R&D complex in East Tehran
description: Israel launched strikes targeting the Malek Ashtar University of Technology's research and development complex in East Tehran. The IDF announced the facility was used to develop components for nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles, and stated: 'Israel will not allow the Iranian regime to acquire nuclear weapons.' Malek Ashtar is Iran's premier defense engineering institution, with known links to IRGC missile and UAV development programs. Multiple explosions reported across eastern Tehran. This follows the Day 22 strike on Natanz and signals continued Israeli targeting of Iran's weapons-development infrastructure.
factCheckNote: IDF announcement confirmed by Times of Israel, Al Jazeera Day 23, and CNN live updates.

title: Netanyahu visits Arad, urges Europe to 'join the fight against Iran'
description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu visited Arad — struck by Iranian ballistic missiles on Day 22 — and urged European leaders to join the military coalition against Iran. Netanyahu cited Iran's demonstrated ~4,000km IRBM range (from the Diego Garcia strike) as proof that European capitals are within reach: 'Berlin, Paris, Rome are all within range.' He stated: 'If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it. It's time to see the leaders of the rest of the countries join up.' He also called the Hormuz blockade 'blackmail of the entire world' and said it was 'a miracle' no one was killed in Arad. Defense Minister Katz separately ordered strikes on all Litani River bridges in Lebanon.
factCheckNote: Netanyahu's Arad visit and statement confirmed by Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, and Al Jazeera. Katz Litani order confirmed by Times of Israel.

title: Lebanon death toll reaches 1,001; Hezbollah fires rockets at Metula
description: Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health confirmed 1,001 people have been killed in Lebanon since the conflict expanded on March 2, with over 2,650 wounded and more than 1 million displaced — approximately 1 in 5 Lebanese. Simultaneously, Hezbollah fired six rockets at Metula in northern Israel; three crashed in Lebanese territory and three were intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Israel has struck over 500 Hezbollah targets and conducted 26 waves of airstrikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh suburb. The IDF ground operation launched March 16 continues south of the Litani River.
factCheckNote: Lebanon Health Ministry death toll of 1,001 confirmed by Times of Israel and Al Jazeera. Hezbollah rocket attack on Metula confirmed by IDF.

title: Iran: Hormuz will be 'completely closed indefinitely' if power plants struck
description: Iran's military operational command issued a formal counter-statement to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum: if Iran's power plants are struck, the Strait of Hormuz will be 'completely closed indefinitely.' The statement further warned that all US and Israeli energy, IT, and desalination infrastructure in the region would be targeted. With the Trump deadline expiring Monday March 23 at approximately 7:44 PM ET, Iran's response signaled zero compliance and maximum escalatory intent. Gulf state desalination plants supply 70-100% of drinking water in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain — making an attack on them an existential threat to Gulf civilian populations.
factCheckNote: Iranian military counter-threat confirmed by Bloomberg, Al Jazeera live blog, and NBC News. Gulf desalination dependency is documented fact (UN/World Bank).

title: Iran-CIA secret backchannel contact reported via third-country intelligence service
description: The New York Times reported that operatives from Iran's Ministry of Intelligence secretly contacted the CIA through a third-country intelligence service to explore terms for ending the conflict. The backchannel contact came as Iran's leadership structure remained in disarray from continued strikes on Tehran, Natanz, and other sites. No formal ceasefire talks are underway. Iran's publicly stated position remains: 'we never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation' (FM Araghchi on NBC). The private contact vs. public rejection signals Iran may be exploring exit options while maintaining a defiant posture.
factCheckNote: Reported by NYT citing US officials briefed on the outreach. Iran has publicly denied any ceasefire contact with the US. Classified nature makes independent verification impossible.

title: CENTCOM: 8,000+ Iranian targets struck; 130 vessels destroyed — 'largest navy elimination since WWII'
description: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper released a Day 22 video update stating US forces have struck over 8,000 Iranian military targets since February 28, including 130 Iranian vessels. Cooper described it as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' He stated the US has flown over 8,000 combat flights and maintains complete air superiority over Iran. Cooper also said Iran's ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz had been 'degraded.' The Pentagon separately confirmed its formal $200 billion supplemental funding request to Congress for the war.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM official statement via Stars and Stripes and C-SPAN video. The 8,000 target and 130 vessel figures are CENTCOM claims — independent verification of exact counts is impossible due to ongoing operations and restricted access.

title: Hezbollah rocket kills Israeli civilian at Misgav Am; IDF orders destruction of Litani bridges
description: A Hezbollah rocket struck Misgav Am in northern Israel, killing Ofer Moskovitz, 60. Hezbollah claimed the attack targeted Israeli army soldiers. The IDF opened an investigation, noting no sirens had sounded before the strike. Defense Minister Katz ordered the IDF to immediately destroy all bridges over the Litani River used by Hezbollah for supply and movement. The death brought Lebanon-front Israeli civilian fatalities to at least 3 since the conflict expansion began March 2.
factCheckNote: Death confirmed by Israeli emergency services. IDF investigating origin of the projectile.

title: Iran fires 8th missile salvo of Day 23 at Israel — 150-180 wounded across southern cities
description: Iran's IRGC fired its eighth wave of ballistic missiles at Israel on Day 23, targeting Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Beersheba, and Kiryat Gat. The combined day's attacks wounded approximately 150-180 people, with at least 11 in serious condition. Israeli air defenses intercepted the majority of incoming missiles but two direct hits caused structural damage in Arad. Iran framed the attacks as retaliation for the US-Israeli strike on Natanz nuclear facility the previous day.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israeli emergency services and Times of Israel liveblog. IAEA confirmed no radiation leak from Dimona area.

title: IRGC releases 'Say goodbye to electricity' map targeting Gulf energy infrastructure
description: Iran's IRGC released via Mehr News Agency a targeting map depicting power plants, energy facilities, and desalination infrastructure across UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain captioned 'Say goodbye to electricity.' Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf stated that all regional energy infrastructure would become legitimate targets if Iran's own power plants are struck per Trump's ultimatum. The threat was a direct counter to Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum, escalating the crisis to encompass potential attacks on Gulf states hosting US military assets.
factCheckNote: Map verified via Mehr News Agency and CBS liveblog. Parliamentary statement confirmed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iranian authorities taunt US/Israel/EU

title: Iran grants Japan Hormuz transit rights; developing IRGC vetting system for approved shipping
description: Iranian FM Araghchi announced Japan-related ships are approved to transit the Strait of Hormuz, joining China, India, and Pakistan on Iran's approved list. Iran simultaneously began developing a formal IRGC registration vetting system requiring vessels to pre-submit ownership, cargo, and destination data to IRGC naval command. At least one tanker reportedly paid $2 million for transit rights. Approximately 40,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships on either side of the strait.
factCheckNote: Araghchi statement confirmed by Japan Times and Al Jazeera.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran allows Japanese ships

title: EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials; Kallas holds diplomatic calls on Hormuz
description: EU High Representative Kaja Kallas announced new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations. Kallas held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea to coordinate pressure on Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. European nations continued to decline sending NATO warships to Hormuz, with Germany's Defense Minister Pistorius stating: 'What does Trump expect a handful of European frigates to do in the Strait of Hormuz that the powerful US Navy cannot do?'
factCheckNote: Kallas post on X confirmed. Pistorius statement confirmed by multiple European sources.

sources.name: Fortune — Europe rejects NATO Hormuz mission

title: Sen. Murkowski calls for congressional war authorization vote amid fourth week of conflict
description: Senator Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) stated she is considering pushing for a congressional war authorization vote if Trump decides to deploy US troops to Iran, saying: 'It raises it to a completely different level than what had been advertised to us as members of Congress. Congress deserves and should demand greater engagement with the administration on the plans.' Separately, Democratic senators Booker, Kaine, Murphy, Schiff, Baldwin, and Duckworth forced a second War Powers Act vote on March 18 — Senate Republicans blocked it again. Trump administration has not sought congressional authorization for the ongoing military operations.
factCheckNote: Murkowski statement confirmed by CNN. War Powers vote confirmed by Sen. Booker's office.

title: US drops GBU-72 bunker buster bombs on Iranian anti-ship missile bunkers near Hormuz; CENTCOM: 8,000+ targets struck
description: CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper announced US forces deployed multiple GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator 5,000-pound bunker buster bombs against hardened underground Iranian anti-ship missile facilities along the coastline near the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes targeted anti-ship cruise missile launchers, mobile missile systems, intelligence support sites, and ship-tracking radar relays. Cooper stated US forces have now struck over 8,000 Iranian targets total and destroyed 130 vessels — calling it 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' The GBU-72 is the US military's heaviest conventional air-dropped penetrating bomb, designed to defeat deeply buried hardened facilities.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM press release and Admiral Cooper video statement confirmed by Gulf News, Maritime Executive, and The Hill.

sources.name: Maritime Executive — US hits Iran antiship sites

title: Netanyahu visits Arad strike site; calls on world leaders to join the war; IDF chief: Hezbollah fight 'only just begun'
description: PM Netanyahu toured Arad, where Iranian ballistic missiles struck residential buildings overnight, wounding 115+. He stated: 'If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have given it,' citing the Arad attack, missiles fired toward Jerusalem holy sites, and the Diego Garcia ICBM attempt. Netanyahu called on world leaders to formally join the war against Iran: 'It's time for world leaders to join us.' IDF Chief of Staff Zamir declared the Hezbollah front is 'only just begun,' signaling weeks more of fighting. Ben Gurion Airport imposed flight restrictions effective Monday.
factCheckNote: Netanyahu statement confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel. Zamir quote confirmed by IDF spokesperson.

title: Iranian cluster munitions scatter across 7+ central Israel sites including Tel Aviv; 15 wounded
description: Cluster bomb fragments from Iranian ballistic missiles landed across at least 7 sites in central Israel on March 22, including Tel Aviv, wounding 15 people. Iran's use of cluster munitions — internationally condemned weapons that scatter indiscriminate submunitions over wide areas — marked an escalation in Iranian targeting tactics against civilian areas. Human rights organizations condemned the strikes. Separately, on March 23, additional cluster missile impacts were reported in central Israel with no new injuries. Iran also fired on Lebanese territory — possibly the first time in this war — with an Iranian ballistic missile apparently striking southern Lebanon, unclear if intentional or a miss.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed cluster warhead fragments found at multiple sites. Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post reporting confirmed.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Iran cluster missile barrage

title: IDF overnight strikes destroy 4 IRGC missile production and fuel facilities in Tehran; power outages follow
description: In overnight strikes following Day 23, Israeli forces struck four major Tehran targets: an IRGC missile component production compound, a missile parts storage site, a Defense Ministry ballistic missile fuel production facility, and a second missile component production site. Israel said the strikes 'significantly damaged Iran's ability to keep producing key ballistic missile components.' Power substation explosions hit western Tehran and Karaj, causing temporary electricity outages. Iran confirmed the strikes but downplayed damage.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iran International, VINnews, and Al Jazeera reporting. Iran state media acknowledged strikes on Tehran.

sources.name: Iran International — IDF Tehran overnight strikes
VINnews — IDF strikes Iranian weapons sites

title: Israel orders all Litani River bridges destroyed; Qasmiyeh coastal bridge struck and demolished
description: Defense Minister Katz ordered IDF to destroy all Litani River bridges used by Hezbollah and to demolish homes in front-line Lebanese border villages. Israeli aircraft destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge, a major coastal route linking Sidon with Tyre and southern Lebanon. Lebanese President Aoun warned this was 'a prelude to a ground invasion.' IDF said the operation cuts Hezbollah supply routes. Lebanon death toll now exceeds 1,024.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by AP/US News, Al Jazeera video, and The National reporting on bridge destruction.

sources.name: US News/AP — Israel orders Litani bridges destroyed
Al Jazeera — Strike on Qasmiyeh bridge

title: CENTCOM: US has struck 8,000+ Iranian targets; 130 naval vessels destroyed — largest navy elimination since WWII
description: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper announced the US has struck over 8,000 Iranian military targets since the war began and destroyed 130 Iranian naval vessels — 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' CENTCOM confirmed it dropped GBU-72 5,000-lb bunker buster bombs on an underground coastal anti-ship missile and mobile launcher storage facility along the Strait of Hormuz to neutralize Iran's ability to threaten Gulf shipping.
factCheckNote: Admiral Cooper statements confirmed by CBS News and CNN Day 23 coverage.

sources.name: CBS News live updates — CENTCOM milestone

title: Trump rejects Pope Leo XIV ceasefire appeal: 'I don't want a ceasefire'; says Iran military is 'dismantled'
description: After Pope Leo XIV appealed for peace during his Sunday Angelus address — calling on all parties to cease hostilities and pursue 'sincere dialogue and respect for the dignity of every human person' — President Trump publicly rejected the ceasefire call. Trump said: 'We can talk, but I don't want a ceasefire,' adding that halting operations would be 'premature' and that Iran's military had been 'dismantled.' Trump also revealed he is 'leaning toward' traveling to Israel in April to receive the Israel Prize at the Independence Day ceremony.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by National Catholic Register and Zenit. Pope Leo XIV Angelus confirmed by The Hill and ABC7.

sources.name: National Catholic Register — Trump rejects ceasefire
The Hill — Pope Leo XIV calls for peace

title: IDF overnight strikes destroy 4 IRGC ballistic missile production sites in Tehran; power outages reported
description: Israeli Air Force conducted a major overnight strike campaign against IRGC ballistic missile infrastructure across Tehran and Karaj. Four facilities were destroyed: an IRGC complex manufacturing ballistic missile components, a missile component storage facility, an Iranian Defense Ministry compound producing missile fuel, and a second ballistic missile components factory. IDF also struck multiple air defense systems protecting Tehran. Power outages were reported in western Tehran and Karaj following explosions at electrical substations.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iran International and Times of Israel March 23 liveblog. Power outages independently reported on social media from Tehran.

sources.name: Iran International — IDF strikes weapons sites Tehran overnight

title: Hezbollah rocket hits Nahariya — 6 wounded; IDF kills Radwan Force commander Barji in Lebanon
description: Hezbollah rocket fire struck the northern Israeli coastal city of Nahariya, wounding six people. Separately, an IDF drone strike in Majdal Selm, southern Lebanon killed Abu Khalil Barji, commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force special operations unit, along with two other operatives. IDF ground forces continued advancing in south Lebanon; 36th Division launched a new targeted operation expanding the forward defense area toward the Litani River.
factCheckNote: Both events confirmed by Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post liveblogs.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — IDF kills Radwan Force commander

title: Ben Gurion Airport restricted to 1 flight/hour, max 50 passengers — Trump Hormuz deadline expires in hours
description: Israeli Transportation Minister Miri Regev announced Ben Gurion Airport will be restricted to one outbound flight per hour with a maximum of 50 passengers, effective 5 PM March 23. The move reflects sustained security concerns following Iranian missile attacks on central Israel. Simultaneously, Trump's 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to fully open the Strait of Hormuz is set to expire at approximately 7:44 PM ET, raising fears of a major escalation if Iran does not comply.
factCheckNote: Airport restrictions confirmed by Haaretz and Times of Israel. Hormuz deadline timing confirmed by Axios (original ultimatum posted 7:44 PM ET March 21).

sources.name: Haaretz — Israel to limit flights at Ben Gurion Airport

title: IDF 'wide-scale wave' strikes IRGC HQ, Basij base, rocket factory across Tehran
description: The IDF launched what it called a 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting Iranian terror regime infrastructure in Tehran,' hitting IRGC police headquarters, the Basij militia base, a rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ in the pre-dawn hours. Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent reported that the size and volume of explosions were 'unprecedented,' especially on the eastern side of the city. Power outages were reported in parts of Tehran and Karaj. CENTCOM separately struck and destroyed Iran's Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant — used to manufacture gas turbine engines for IRGC drones.
factCheckNote: IDF formally announced the operation. Qom plant destruction confirmed by CENTCOM with before/after imagery released.

sources.name: CBC News — Israel launches new attacks on Tehran
Business Standard — CENTCOM destroys Qom turbine plant

title: Civilian casualties across Iran: Tabriz (6 killed), Khorramabad (1 child killed), Urmia (buildings flattened), Bandar Abbas IRIB station struck
description: Multiple Iranian cities suffered civilian casualties in the early morning: In Tabriz, airstrikes hit residential buildings killing 6 and injuring 6, confirmed by East Azerbaijan Province crisis management. In Khorramabad, Lorestan province, a child was killed in a residential strike with multiple wounded. In Urmia, northwest Iran, residential buildings were 'flattened,' with Red Crescent rescue teams searching rubble. In Bandar Abbas, a US-Israeli strike hit the IRIB state radio transmitter, killing 1 IRIB security guard and injuring another.
factCheckNote: Tabriz confirmed by crisis management official Majid Farshi. Khorramabad confirmed by Fars News Agency. All verified by Al Jazeera liveblog and RTÉ.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran
Anadolu Agency — Bandar Abbas broadcast center strike

title: Joint Iran-Hezbollah barrage hits northern Israel; missile fragment falls in Safed
description: Iran and Hezbollah launched a coordinated simultaneous barrage targeting northern Israel in the early morning. Al Jazeera's correspondent reported Israeli authorities believed it was a joint coordinated attack. Seven IDF soldiers were lightly wounded across northern Israel and south Lebanon. Part of an intercepted Iranian ballistic missile fell in the northern city of Safed (Tzfat), igniting a fire that firefighters brought under control. No civilian casualties from the shrapnel fall.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel liveblogs. Firefighters confirmed fire from missile fragment in Safed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera Iran War liveblog March 23

title: Trump announces 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes — claims 'productive' US-Iran talks; Iran flatly denies any negotiations
description: In a major diplomatic development, President Trump posted on Truth Social that the US and Iran had held 'very good and productive conversations regarding a complete and total resolution' of hostilities, and instructed the Pentagon to postpone all strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for 5 days. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi immediately denied any negotiations were underway, calling Trump's claim an attempt to 'mislead oil traders.' Iran's Fars news agency cited a government source saying there had been 'no direct or indirect contact.' Markets surged on Trump's announcement. Iran simultaneously threatened to mine the Persian Gulf and strike Gulf power plants if its coastline or islands are attacked.
factCheckNote: Trump's Truth Social post widely reported. Iranian denial confirmed by multiple state media sources. Market surge corroborated by Bloomberg and Reuters.

sources.name: NPR — Iran defiant as Trump delays striking power plants
Bloomberg — Trump delays Iran strikes for five days
Fox News — Trump postpones strikes on Iranian energy sites

title: Trump reveals deal terms at West Palm Beach: Iran to surrender nukes, enrichment, uranium stockpile — Hormuz 'jointly controlled'
description: Speaking to reporters at West Palm Beach airport, Trump revealed detailed terms of what he claimed were agreed-upon deal elements with Iran. He said Iran agreed to 'no nukes, no enrichment' and would surrender its enriched uranium stockpile. On the Strait of Hormuz, Trump said it would be managed 'by mutual agreement of both sides' and potentially 'jointly controlled.' He identified Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner as his negotiators and said the counterpart was a 'most respected' Iranian leader — not Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Iran's government denied all of it. Israeli officials told Haaretz they believe formal US-Iran talks in Pakistan could begin within days.
factCheckNote: Trump's claims of deal terms are unverified — Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly denied any negotiations or agreements. Trump's account of 'no nukes, no enrichment' agreement cannot be independently confirmed and Iran's state media called it disinformation designed to lower oil prices.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Trump says Iran agreed to no uranium enrichment
Axios — Trump suspends Iran strikes for Hormuz negotiations
Israel Hayom — Iran agrees to give up nuclear weapons

title: Kataib Hezbollah extends 5-day pause on US Embassy Baghdad attacks — Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims 27 attacks in 24 hours
description: Iraqi Shia militia Kataib Hezbollah extended its conditional pause on attacks against the US Embassy in Baghdad for an additional five days. The group simultaneously reported 27 separate attacks on US military installations across Iraq and the Gulf in the prior 24 hours — the highest daily tempo since the conflict began. Attacks involved drones, rockets, and tactical missiles. Eight overnight attacks targeted Camp Victoria near Baghdad International Airport. Three drones were intercepted near Erbil Airport; another crashed near Baghdad injuring four civilians.
factCheckNote: Embassy pause confirmed by The National/Reuters. Iraqi militia attack claims largely consistent with CENTCOM's pattern of reporting; specific strike numbers represent militia's own claims.

sources.name: The National — Iraqi pro-Iran militia extends pause in attacks on US Embassy
Kurdistan 24 — Militia drone attack on US bases in Erbil

title: IEA: conflict already worse than both 1970s oil shocks combined — 40+ energy facilities damaged in 9 countries
description: IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol issued his starkest warning yet, saying at least 40 energy facilities across 9 countries have been severely damaged in the conflict and calling it 'worse than the two oil shocks of the 1970s combined.' He said the global economy faces 'a major, major threat' and called opening the Strait of Hormuz 'the single most important solution,' warning 'no country will be immune to the effects of this crisis if it continues to go in this direction.' Brent crude briefly reached $119/barrel earlier in the week. Trump's 5-day pause announcement later in the day pulled prices back to ~$101, providing temporary market relief.
factCheckNote: IEA head Birol's statement confirmed via NBC News live updates and Al Jazeera. The 40+ facilities figure is IEA's own assessment.

sources.name: NBC News Iran War live updates
Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 24 explainer

title: IRGC attacks Prince Sultan Airbase (Saudi Arabia) and US 5th Fleet headquarters (Bahrain)
description: The IRGC confirmed strikes against Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia and US Naval Support Activity Bahrain — headquarters of the US 5th Fleet — using ballistic missiles and drones. Saudi Arabia confirmed two ballistic missiles were fired toward Riyadh: one intercepted, one fell in an uninhabited area south of the capital. The strikes represented Iran's escalation of the conflict into Gulf Cooperation Council territory and against US regional naval command infrastructure.
factCheckNote: IRGC confirmed the strikes. Saudi Arabia independently confirmed two incoming ballistic missiles. Al Jazeera Day 24 explainer and Reuters corroborate.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 24 explainer
Al Jazeera — Unprecedented strikes across Iran

title: Iran fires 6 missile barrages at Israel; Hezbollah launches simultaneous rockets — shrapnel across central Israel
description: Iran launched at least six separate ballistic missile barrages at Israel throughout Day 24, the highest single-day launch tempo yet recorded. Hezbollah coordinated simultaneous rocket salvos from Lebanon during two of the barrages. Sirens sounded across northern, southern, and central Israel. Despite largely successful interceptions, falling shrapnel struck multiple communities. The pattern continued Iran's operational strategy of overwhelming air defenses through distributed, timed attacks.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog documented each barrage in real-time. Haaretz confirmed shrapnel damage in central Israel locations.

title: IDF kills IRGC Quds Force member in Beirut; issues evacuation warnings for four southern suburbs
description: The IDF struck and killed an identified IRGC Quds Force member in Beirut — the first confirmed killing of an IRGC officer inside the Lebanese capital since the current conflict. The IDF subsequently issued evacuation warnings for four densely populated southern Beirut suburbs: Bourj el-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik, and Chiyah, signaling a further expansion of Israel's Beirut targeting scope beyond what was struck in prior weeks.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the strike and the evacuation warnings. Jerusalem Post live updates and Times of Israel liveblog corroborate.

title: USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group — 2,500 Marines depart San Diego for Middle East; total US force reaches ~50,000
description: USS Boxer (Wasp-class), USS Portland, and USS Comstock departed Naval Base San Diego carrying approximately 2,500 Marines from the 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit bound for the Middle East. Combined with the earlier USS Tripoli deployment (2,200 Marines) and existing ground and air forces, total US military personnel in the Middle East theater has reached approximately 50,000 — the largest single concentration since the 2003 Iraq War.
factCheckNote: Mercury News and Stars & Stripes independently confirmed the departure of the three-ship ARG. 50,000-troop figure from CENTCOM estimates cited in multiple outlets.

sources.name: Mercury News — Three San Diego warships with 2,500 Marines en route to Middle East
Stars and Stripes — Marines, USS Boxer deployment

title: Trump announces 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes — cites 'productive talks'; Iran denies all contact
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social that the US and Iran had engaged in 'very good and productive conversations' over the past two days toward a resolution. He announced he has 'instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period.' The announcement came hours before his own 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum was set to expire. Iran's FM Araghchi, President Pezeshkian, and a government source via Fars News all flatly denied any negotiations had occurred — Araghchi calling the claims 'an attempt to mislead oil traders.' Oil markets swung violently, with Brent crashing 14% intraday before partial recovery.
factCheckNote: Trump post confirmed across all major outlets. Iran denials confirmed across state media and Araghchi's X account. The dispute — whether talks occurred at all — remains unresolved. Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey are serving as intermediaries.

sources.name: NPR — Iran defiant as Trump delays striking power plants
Al Jazeera — Trump postpones military strikes on Iranian power plants
Axios — Trump suspends Iran strikes for Hormuz negotiations

title: IRGC 75th wave strikes Prince Sultan Airbase (Saudi Arabia) and US 5th Fleet HQ (Bahrain) with ballistic missiles; Saudi Arabia confirms 2 missiles fired toward Riyadh
description: The IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones against Prince Sultan Airbase (the primary US air hub in Saudi Arabia) and Naval Support Activity Bahrain (US 5th Fleet headquarters), continuing Operation True Promise 4. Saudi Arabia confirmed two ballistic missiles were fired toward Riyadh — one intercepted, one fell in an uninhabited area south of the capital. Air raid sirens and explosions were reported across Bahrain. The attack represented a direct expansion of Iranian strikes into GCC territory and against core US regional command infrastructure, even as Trump's 5-day energy strike pause remained in effect.
factCheckNote: IRGC confirmed the strikes. Saudi Arabia confirmed missile attacks toward Riyadh. Al Jazeera and CBS News independently reported.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 24 explainer
CBS News — Iran war live updates

title: Trump reveals deal terms: Iran must surrender uranium, end enrichment, accept Hormuz joint control; Iran calls claims 'disinformation'
description: President Trump told reporters that Iran had verbally agreed to 'no nukes, no enrichment' and would surrender its enriched uranium stockpile. He described Hormuz being 'jointly managed' as part of any deal. Envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff were named as conducting the talks. Iran's government dismissed these claims — Araghchi said Hormuz is 'not closed,' Pezeshkian posted on Telegram calling Trump's statements 'attempts to reduce energy prices and buy time.' US officials separately told media they preferred engaging with Iran's parliament speaker Qalibaf, not Araghchi. The deal framing remains entirely unconfirmed by Iran.
factCheckNote: Trump claims of Iranian agreement are contested — Iran denied all contact. Third-party intermediaries (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) are believed to be relaying messages. Independent confirmation of specific 'agreements' is absent.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Trump: Iran agreed to no uranium enrichment
Israel Hayom — Trump: Iran agrees to give up nuclear weapons

title: IEA chief Birol: Iran war is worst oil supply disruption in modern history — larger than combined 1973 and 1979 shocks
description: IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol issued an extraordinary warning that the global economy faces a 'major, major threat,' characterizing the current Hormuz crisis as the 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market' — surpassing the combined impact of the 1973 Arab oil embargo and 1979 Iranian Revolution, which together removed approximately 10 million barrels per day. The IEA's March 2026 Oil Market Report confirmed at least 40 energy facilities across 9 countries have been severely damaged. IEA member nations are drawing down the 400 million barrel emergency reserve release ordered on March 11, the largest in IEA history. Brent crude peaked at /barrel on Day 24 before Trump's 5-day pause pulled it below .
factCheckNote: Birol statement confirmed by Bloomberg and CNN. IEA March 2026 OMR confirms 40+ facilities damaged. Historical oil crisis comparisons verified against IEA and EIA data.

sources.name: CNN Business — Oil energy shock worse than 1970s

title: IDF overnight strikes hit IRGC HQ, Intelligence Ministry, Khatam-al Anbiya command in Tehran
description: The Israeli Air Force conducted another major overnight strike package against Tehran on Day 25, hitting the IRGC main security headquarters, an army training base housing anti-aircraft missile storage, a Defense Ministry weapons production and storage facility, an IRGC Air Force weapons production site, the Iranian Intelligence Ministry headquarters, and the Khatam-al Anbiya military emergency command center. The IDF said it had now destroyed or disabled approximately 330 of Iran's ~470 ballistic missile launchers and was planning at least three more weeks of sustained operations.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post reporting citing official IDF statements.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF hits arms production sites, intel HQs in overnight Tehran strikes
Jerusalem Post — IDF strikes several targets in Tehran including intelligence HQ

title: Iran resumes near-hourly missile barrages after 11-hour quiet; 100kg warhead hits Tel Aviv residential area
description: After an 11-hour quiet period on Monday, Iran relaunched near-hourly ballistic missile barrages overnight into Tuesday. A 100-kilogram warhead struck a residential area in central Tel Aviv, mangling an apartment building facade and crushing vehicles; four people sustained light injuries. In a separate strike, an intercepted missile fragment struck a mobile home in an unrecognized Bedouin community in the Negev, injuring four including two infants. The IDF confirmed at least eight distinct barrages across Israel throughout the day. Iran has now fired over 1,800 missiles and drones at Israel since Day 1.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Haaretz, Jerusalem Post, and NPR on-scene reporting.

sources.name: Haaretz — Iran Relaunches Hourly Missile Attacks at Israel After 11 Hours of Quiet
NPR — Iran fires more missiles at Israel

title: Lebanon expels Iran's incoming ambassador — declares him persona non grata in major diplomatic rupture
description: Lebanese Foreign Minister Youssef Raggi declared Iran's incoming ambassador Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata and ordered him to leave by March 29 — a watershed diplomatic rupture between Lebanon and Iran. Lebanon also recalled its own ambassador to Tehran and reimposed visa restrictions on Iranian travelers. The government explicitly cited the IRGC's direct command of Hezbollah military operations on Lebanese soil as a violation of diplomatic norms. The decision underscored the devastating human cost the war has imposed on Lebanon: over 1,000 dead, 2,800+ wounded, and nearly one million people displaced — roughly 20% of the country's population.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, The National, and Times of Israel.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Lebanon declares Iranian ambassador persona non grata
The National — Lebanon declares incoming Iranian ambassador persona non grata

title: Iran appoints hardline IRGC veteran Zolghadr to replace assassinated security chief Larijani
description: Iran appointed Mohammad-Bagher Zolghadr as secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani, who was killed in an Israeli airstrike. Approved by Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and formally appointed by President Pezeshkian, Zolghadr is a hardline former IRGC brigadier general known for suppression operations during his tenure as deputy interior minister under Ahmadinejad. Security analysts noted his appointment signals Iran's intent to maintain a military-first posture and predicted Israel would target him as a continuation of its leadership-decapitation campaign.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg and The National.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Iran Appoints IRGC Veteran Zolghadr as Security Chief
The National — Iran appoints new security chief Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr

title: Araghchi privately signals readiness for deal with Witkoff/Kushner; Iran parliament speaker publicly denies all talks
description: Iran International reported that Foreign Minister Araghchi privately signaled readiness for a deal in a call with US envoys Witkoff and Kushner, citing approval from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei to 'close the issue quickly' if Iran's conditions were met. Simultaneously, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly stated 'no negotiations have been held with the US,' calling Trump's claims an attempt to 'manipulate the financial and oil markets.' The IRGC publicly called Trump a 'deceitful American president.' US mediators indicated an Islamabad meeting between Ghalibaf's team and Witkoff/Kushner was being arranged for this week. Significant gaps remain; Israel was excluded from these contacts, prompting concern in Jerusalem.
factCheckNote: Private Araghchi signal reported by Iran International; Iran's public denials are simultaneous. Both can be true — Iran publicly denying while backchannel talks proceed. Islamabad meeting not yet confirmed.

sources.name: Iran International — Iran foreign minister signaled readiness for deal
Al Jazeera — Where do US-Iran negotiations leave Israel?
Axios — Trump suspends Iran strikes for Hormuz negotiations

title: Iran continues attacking Gulf States — Saudi Arabia intercepts 20 drones, Kuwait activates 7+ times, UAE missile intercept injures Indian national
description: Iran maintained its multi-front Gulf assault on Day 25. Saudi Arabia intercepted approximately 20 Iranian drones targeting its Eastern Province, home to major oil and energy infrastructure. Kuwaiti air defenses activated at least seven times in a single night. UAE air defense intercepted a ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi; one Indian national sustained minor injuries from falling debris. Bahrain has now intercepted a cumulative total of 153 missiles and 301 drones since the war began. Gulf State civilian deaths stand at 21. Iran's sustained campaign against Gulf neighbors — all of whom are home to US military bases — represents a deliberate strategy of regional destabilization.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 25 liveblog and NBC News live updates.

sources.name: Al Jazeera Day 25 — whats happening on day 25
NBC News live updates Iran war March 24

title: IDF captures 2 elite Hezbollah Radwan Force operatives; Katz announces all Litani River bridges destroyed
description: Golani Brigade troops captured two operatives from Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force in southern Lebanon after spotting them preparing to fire anti-tank missiles at Israeli forces. Forces also discovered and destroyed a weapons-laden tunnel. Israeli Defense Minister Katz simultaneously announced that the IDF has destroyed all bridges over the Litani River used by Hezbollah to resupply its forces — a significant infrastructure milestone in the Lebanon campaign. Separately, Katz announced the implementation of 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' for villages used as Hezbollah firing positions. The Radwan Force is Hezbollah's best-equipped rapid-reaction unit; its operatives' capture provides significant intelligence value.
factCheckNote: Radwan capture confirmed by Times of Israel. Litani bridges announcement confirmed by IDF/Katz statement.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF troops capture two Radwan Force operatives

title: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper: US destroyed 19 Iranian ships, 1 submarine, 2,000+ targets, 9,000 sorties — planning weeks-long Hormuz reopening operation
description: CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper delivered a comprehensive operational assessment: the US has now conducted 9,000 combat sorties, struck approximately 2,000 targets, destroyed 19 Iranian naval vessels and 1 submarine, and confirmed Iran has attacked civilian targets more than 300 times. Cooper stated the Strait of Hormuz is 'physically open' but Iran is continuing to fire on vessels attempting transit. He announced the US is planning a 'weeks-long operation' to force full reopening of the strait. The IDF separately confirmed it is preparing at least 3 more weeks of operations against thousands of remaining targets across Iran.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by C-SPAN CENTCOM press conference and CBS News live updates.

sources.name: C-SPAN — CENTCOM Update on Operation Epic Fury
CBS News — Trump calls off Hormuz ultimatum

title: US strikes senior Iran-backed militia commander Saad Dawai in Iraq Anbar Province; militias offer $100K bounty for US forces locations
description: The US military conducted a strike in Iraq's Anbar province targeting the headquarters of an Iran-backed armed group and its senior commander Saad Dawai. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq has now claimed over 290 attacks on US forces since Operation Epic Fury began — one of the highest-tempo proxy campaigns in the war. In a significant escalatory signal, the IRI announced a reward of 150 million Iraqi dinars (approximately $100,000) for information about the locations of American forces in Iraq, representing a direct escalation from military attacks to targeted bounty operations against US personnel.
factCheckNote: Strike confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 25 reporting and Long War Journal. Casualty status of Saad Dawai unconfirmed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera Day 25 — whats happening on day 25
Long War Journal — Iranian-backed militias continue attacks on US forces

title: IDF strikes Isfahan explosives production facility — 120+ bombs across dozens of Iranian targets overnight
description: The Israeli Air Force conducted a major overnight strike wave targeting Iran's 'production industries,' dropping over 120 bombs across dozens of targets. The strikes hit Iran's main explosives production facility in the Isfahan area, ballistic missile launch sites, weapons production facilities, and air defense systems. The Isfahan explosives factory had been partially restored since a prior strike in June 2025 and was functioning again before being struck. The IDF framed the campaign as systematically dismantling Iran's industrial capacity to manufacture and replenish munitions.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the strikes on the Isfahan explosives facility. Reported by Times of Israel and Iran International.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Israeli Air Force bombed key Iranian explosives facility in Isfahan
Iran International — Israel strikes missile facility in Isfahan

title: Hezbollah rocket kills Israeli woman at Mahanayim Junction — Iranian cluster warhead missile wounds 6 in Petah Tikva
description: Two significant Hezbollah/Iran strikes hit Israeli civilians in the evening of Day 25. A Hezbollah rocket killed a woman in her 30s at the Mahanayim Junction in northern Israel — the first Israeli civilian death by Hezbollah rocket fire in several days. Two others were wounded, including one man with serious head shrapnel injuries. Separately, Iran fired a ballistic missile with a cluster warhead at the Tel Aviv metropolitan area; bomblets dispersed over Petah Tikva and Bnei Brak, wounding at least 6 by shrapnel. Iran's continued use of cluster munitions against densely populated civilian areas drew condemnation from human rights organizations.
factCheckNote: Hezbollah death confirmed by MDA, IDF, Jerusalem Post. Cluster warhead injuries confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Al Jazeera.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Woman declared dead after Hezbollah rocket attack
Jerusalem Post — One killed, two wounded by Hezbollah rocket fire in northern Israel
Jerusalem Post — At least six injured after shrapnel from Iranian cluster missile

title: US airstrike kills PMF commander Saad Dawai + 14 fighters at Habbaniyah — deadliest single Iraq strike of the conflict
description: US airstrikes hit the PMF operations headquarters at Habbaniyah base, Anbar province, during a command meeting. PMF confirmed the death of Anbar operations commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji and 14 named fighters; at least 30 were wounded. The US also reportedly struck the residence of senior PMF leader Falih al-Fayadh in Mosul (he was not present at the time). This is the deadliest single US strike on Iraqi militia forces since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq had recently claimed over 290 total attacks on US forces and announced a $100,000 bounty for the locations of American military personnel in Iraq.
factCheckNote: PMF confirmed deaths and names. Confirmed by The National, Kurdistan 24, The Defense Post, Cyprus Mail.

sources.name: The National — Air strike kills 15 PMF fighters in deadliest attack since start of Iran war
Kurdistan 24 — PMF Anbar Operations Commander and 14 Fighters Killed in US Strike

title: US-Israeli strikes hit Bushehr nuclear plant vicinity + new IDF wave targets IRGC HQ and energy infrastructure across Iran
description: A US-Israeli airstrike struck a structure 350 metres from Iran’s Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, confirmed by the IAEA; no radiation release or reactor damage reported. Iran condemned the strike as an attack by the “American-Israeli enemy.” In a separate large-scale IDF strike wave hours later, Israel targeted the IRGC’s main central security headquarters in Tehran (confirmed destroyed), as well as energy facilities in Isfahan, Khorramshahr, Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, and Sirjan. IDF struck 50+ targets in this wave; two gas facilities and a pipeline were also reported hit. Iran’s state media confirmed large explosions across multiple cities.
factCheckNote: Bushehr strike confirmed by IAEA. IRGC HQ strike confirmed by IDF, JNS, and Alma Research Center.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Iran says US and Israel attacked vicinity of Bushehr nuclear plant
JNS — IDF launches large-scale strikes hitting main IRGC security headquarters
Alma Research Center — Day 25 daily report

title: Trump declares “war has been won” — 5-day pause on Iranian power plant strikes announced; Pakistan mediating US-Iran talks
description: President Trump announced a 5-day pause on US-Israeli strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure, citing “very good and productive conversations” with Iran. Posting on Truth Social in all-caps, he declared “the war has been won” and claimed Iran offered “a very significant prize” related to the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said Kushner, Witkoff, Rubio, and Vance are all participating. The US passed a 15-point peace framework to Iran through Pakistani intermediaries with a 24-hour response window. Iran’s Foreign Ministry, IRGC, and parliament speaker publicly denied direct talks while Iranian FM Araghchi privately signaled deal readiness in a back-channel call with Witkoff. Israel — not part of the talks — fears a premature ceasefire before it achieves its war goals; Israeli military officials told NPR they need “several more weeks.”
factCheckNote: Trump’s claim of active direct talks disputed by Iran. Indirect contacts via Pakistan/Turkey/Egypt confirmed. Iran’s claim of “no negotiations” partially disputed by Araghchi-Witkoff back-channel reporting (Iran International).

sources.name: NPR — Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a prize in talks Iran has denied having
CNN — Live updates: Trump says Vance and Rubio participating in talks with Iran
Bloomberg — Pakistan Seeks to Mediate Peace Talks With US, Iran in Islamabad
NPR — Israel said to fear premature ceasefire, needs several more weeks

title: Pentagon orders 82nd Airborne deployment to Middle East — Iran fires missiles at Eilat and Jerusalem
description: The Pentagon ordered the 82nd Airborne Division (2,000–3,000 troops) from Fort Bragg to deploy to the Middle East, with Maj. Gen. Tegtmeier and HQ staff included; two Marine Expeditionary Units (~5,000 troops) also arriving. No decision yet on ground invasion of Iran. Simultaneously, Iran fired separate missile barrages at Eilat in southern Israel (13th attack in 24 hours, assessed intercepted) and at the Jerusalem region (sirens activated, no injuries confirmed). The Israeli reserve mobilization was expanded to 400,000 soldiers; the Defense Minister stated the goal of occupying southern Lebanon up to the Litani River.
factCheckNote: 82nd Airborne deployment confirmed by Bloomberg, Military Times, and The Intercept. Iranian missile attacks confirmed by IDF and Times of Israel.

sources.name: Bloomberg — US Set to Deploy 2,000 Troops From 82nd Airborne to Middle East
Military Times — US expected to send thousands of soldiers to Middle East

title: Iranian cluster munitions hit Bnei Brak — 12 injured; ballistic missile penetrates Tel Aviv air defenses
description: Iranian cluster bomb munitions struck Bnei Brak, injuring 12 including 6 children. Additional impacts in Petah Tikva and Rosh Ha'ayin. Separately, a ballistic missile with a several-hundred-kg warhead struck northern Tel Aviv after Israeli air defenses failed to intercept, destroying apartment blocks and injuring 4. The IAF is investigating the interception failure. Iran fired at least 8 separate missile waves at Israel throughout March 24.
factCheckNote: Cluster munition strike confirmed by Times of Israel and JPost. Tel Aviv interception failure confirmed by IDF.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Cluster munition impacts in Bnei Brak
Al Jazeera — Building collapses after Iranian missile strike on Israel

title: Lebanon expels Iranian ambassador — declares persona non grata amid Hezbollah backlash
description: Lebanese FM Youssef Raggi declared Iranian ambassador-designate Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata, ordering departure by March 29. Lebanon recalled its own ambassador from Tehran. Hezbollah condemned the decision as 'reckless and reprehensible.' Total Lebanese casualties since March 2: 1,072 killed, 2,966 injured, 1.5M displaced.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Washington Post, and FDD.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Lebanon declares Iranian ambassador persona non grata
Washington Post — Lebanon orders Iran's ambassador out

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah fuel stations, intelligence HQ, and Litani crossings; 3 Quds Force killed in Beirut
description: IDF struck Hezbollah-linked Al-Amana fuel stations, Intelligence Directorate HQ in Beirut, a Radwan Force command center, and all crossings along the Litani River used for weapons transfers. IDF destroyed a terror tunnel near Mount Dov. Three IRGC Quds Force members killed in targeted assassinations in Beirut overnight. Defense Minister Katz announced a permanent 'security zone' in southern Lebanon to the Litani River.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by JPost and Al Jazeera.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — IDF strikes Hezbollah fuel stations
Al Jazeera — Israel kills three in Beirut

title: Philippines declares national energy emergency; Kuwait airport drone-struck; AWS Bahrain disrupted again
description: Philippines became the first country to declare a national energy emergency due to the Iran war — it imports 98% of its oil from the Gulf and fuel prices have risen ~200%. Iranian drones struck Kuwait International Airport fuel depot (4th attack). AWS Bahrain cloud region disrupted again. German President Steinmeier called the war a 'breach of international law.'
