IDF confirmed 3,000+ targets hit and 330/470 missile launchers destroyed — Iran's daily launch rate dropped 90%.
factCheckNote: Philippines emergency confirmed by Rappler. Kuwait strike confirmed by Gulf News. IDF stats confirmed by Time and JNS.

sources.name: Rappler — Marcos declares national energy emergency
Gulf News — Iranian drone strikes hit fuel depot at Kuwait airport
Time — Iran strikes Israel as war rages

title: IDF destroys Iran's submarine R&D center; Bushehr 'worst-case' — Rosatom evacuating; Iran missile near Israel's largest power plant
description: Israeli Air Force destroyed Iran's sole submarine development facility (Underwater Research Center) in Isfahan and two cruise missile production sites in central Tehran. Rosatom chief declared Bushehr nuclear plant situation a 'worst-case scenario' after second strike in two weeks, began third evacuation phase with ~480 Russian staff remaining. Iran fired ballistic missile near Orot Rabin power plant in Hadera — Israel's largest (2,590 MW, ~20% of national supply) — no damage reported.
factCheckNote: Submarine facility destruction confirmed by IDF. Rosatom evacuation confirmed by Al Arabiya and DevDiscourse. Orot Rabin near-miss confirmed by IEC and Times of Israel.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Rosatom worst-case scenario at Bushehr

title: US delivers 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan — Iran publicly denies all talks
description: US delivered a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran through Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir. Plan covers sanctions relief, nuclear cooperation, IAEA monitoring, missile limits, and Hormuz access. Iran's military spokesman mocked US: 'Have your internal conflicts reached the point where you are negotiating with yourselves?' Parliamentary speaker Qalibaf dismissed reports as 'fake news.' An Iranian source told CNN Tehran willing to listen to 'suitable' proposals but demands Vance lead, not Witkoff/Kushner.
factCheckNote: Plan delivery confirmed by Bloomberg, Time, CNBC, and Fox News. Iran denial confirmed by multiple outlets. Pakistan mediation confirmed by WaPo.

sources.name: Bloomberg — US drafts 15-point plan to end Iran war
Al Jazeera — What are each side's demands?

title: Israel announces formal occupation of south Lebanon to Litani River; 9 killed in airstrikes; 400,000 reservists authorized
description: Defense Minister Katz formally announced occupation of southern Lebanon up to Litani River (~30km north), creating 'security zone' covering ~10% of Lebanon. Finance Minister Smotrich called for annexation. Hezbollah declared it 'existential threat.' Israeli strikes killed 9 in south Lebanon (4 in Adloun, 2 in Mieh Mieh refugee camp, 3 elsewhere). Knesset extended emergency to April 14; government authorized IDF to draft 400,000 reservists. Hezbollah anti-tank attack wounded 1 IDF officer.
factCheckNote: Litani occupation confirmed by JPost and Japan Times. Lebanon casualties confirmed by France 24 and Lebanese Health Ministry. Reservist authorization confirmed by ToI.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Israel announces territorial seizure to Litani
France 24 — Israeli strikes on south Lebanon leave nine dead

title: Oil drops ~6% on ceasefire hopes; Senate fails war powers vote 3rd time; Hengaw: 6,530 killed in Iran; Japan releases oil reserves
description: Brent crude fell ~6% to ~$99/bbl on ceasefire plan news, dropping below $100 for first time since Day 22. WTI fell to ~$88.50. Saudi Arabia intercepted 32+ Iranian drones and 1 ballistic missile targeting Ras Tanura/Abqaiq oil facilities. Southern Tehran strike killed 12, wounded 28. Habbaniyah military clinic in Iraq struck — 7 killed, 13 wounded. Senate war powers vote failed 47-53 for 3rd time. Hengaw 7th report: 6,530 killed in Iran (640 civilians). Japan announced 30-day oil reserve release — largest ever. Pentagon signed munitions surge deals with BAE/Lockheed/Honeywell.
factCheckNote: Oil price drop confirmed by Fortune and CNBC. Hengaw report confirmed via hengaw.net. Saudi intercepts confirmed by Al Jazeera. Senate vote confirmed by NBC News.

sources.name: Hengaw — 7th report on Iran war casualties

title: IRGC Wave 80: Four missile salvos in 40 minutes hit Tel Aviv metro area and US Gulf bases simultaneously
description: The IRGC launched Operation True Promise 4 (Wave 80) — four successive salvos of Kheibar Shekan, Emad, and Sejjil ballistic missiles plus attack drones targeting the Tel Aviv metropolitan area within a 40-minute window. The attack also targeted US military installations in Kuwait (Ali al-Salem, Arifjan), Jordan (al-Azraq), and Bahrain (Sheikh Isa). Multiple interceptions were carried out; debris from one intercepted missile caused a vehicle fire in central Tel Aviv. 6 Israelis lightly injured at four sites. The IDF conducted an investigation into two separate air defense failures (Dimona/Arad) and found no link between incidents. For the first time, an Iranian ballistic missile was intercepted over Lebanese airspace.
factCheckNote: IRGC Wave 80 confirmed by PressTV, Al Jazeera liveblog, and Times of Israel. US base targeting confirmed by PressTV. Lebanon intercept described as first-ever by Times of Israel.

title: Hezbollah anti-tank commander killed in drone strike; Israel destroys 5th and final Litani River bridge
description: An Israeli drone killed Hassan Kamal Halawi, commander of Hezbollah's anti-tank missile unit, in a strike on his vehicle in Qaaqaait al-Jisr, southern Lebanon. A separate IDF airstrike destroyed the Qaaqaait al-Jisr Bridge over the Litani River — the 5th Litani crossing to be blown up, now completing Israel's isolation of south Lebanon north of the river. Defense Minister Katz confirmed all five Litani bridges have been destroyed. Approximately 150,000 civilians remain trapped south of the Litani. Hezbollah maintaining ~100 rocket launches per day at northern Israel.
factCheckNote: Halawi killing confirmed by Naharnet and Al Jazeera. 5th bridge destruction confirmed by Israel Hayom quoting Defense Minister Katz.

sources.name: Naharnet — Israeli drone kills commander Qaaqaait al-Jisr
Israel Hayom — IDF strikes Lebanon bridges

title: Iran formally rejects US 15-point peace plan; issues 5 counter-conditions including Hormuz authority demand
description: Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace proposal (delivered via Pakistan) through Foreign Minister Araghchi, issuing five counter-conditions: (1) halt to all US-Israeli military operations and targeted assassinations; (2) binding guarantee that war will not resume; (3) payment of full war reparations to Iran; (4) end to attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iraqi militia groups; (5) international recognition of Iran's 'natural, legal right' to control the Strait of Hormuz. Araghchi stated: 'No negotiations will be held prior to that.' Iran simultaneously denied any direct talks with the US, calling Trump's negotiation claims false. Iran also demanded that JD Vance personally replace Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner in any talks — a demand the White House rejected.
factCheckNote: 5 counter-conditions confirmed by PressTV state news, Anadolu Agency, NPR, and CNBC. Vance demand confirmed by Daily Beast. White House rejection confirmed by NBC News.

sources.name: Press TV — Iran rejects US proposal, 5 conditions
NPR — Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war

title: Philippines declares national energy emergency; Saudi Arabia and UAE moving toward war entry; US A-10s strike Iraq PMF
description: Philippine President Marcos declared a national State of Energy Emergency — the first country globally to do so — as fuel prices more than doubled and strategic reserves dwindled. Japan simultaneously announced its largest-ever 30-day oil reserve release (part of IEA's 400M barrel coordinated effort). The Wall Street Journal reported Saudi Arabia and UAE are moving closer to entering the war on the US-Israel side; Saudi Arabia has already authorized use of military bases. Iran threatened to seize Bahraini and UAE coastlines if the US 'makes any mistake.' US A-10 aircraft struck PMF targets in Anbar Province, Iraq, killing 7 and wounding 10; Iraq's National Security Council authorized PMF to respond under 'self-defense.' Iran named Mohammed Bagher Zolghadr as new SNSC secretary, replacing Ali Larijani (killed March 17).
factCheckNote: Philippines energy emergency confirmed by Al Jazeera and Newsweek. Saudi/UAE report confirmed by WSJ via Alma Research. A-10 strikes confirmed by Alma Research.

sources.name: Alma Research — March 25 daily report (Saudi/UAE, A-10 strikes)

title: CENTCOM strikes 10,000th Iranian target; Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drones; Hezbollah anti-tank commander Halawi killed
description: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed the US has now struck over 10,000 military targets inside Iran, with 92% of Iran's largest naval vessels destroyed and the pace of Iranian attacks down 90% since Day 1. B-52 bombers are delivering up to 70,000 pounds of munitions per mission. In a parallel operation, Iranian drones struck a fuel storage tank at Kuwait International Airport, igniting a fire with no casualties; Kuwait's National Guard intercepted 6 additional drones. Six Hezbollah-linked individuals were arrested in Kuwait for an alleged assassination plot. IDF announced the killing of Hassan Kamal Halawi, commander of Hezbollah's southern front anti-tank unit, in an Israeli drone strike in Qaaqaait al-Jisr, Nabatieh. Hezbollah rockets wounded one IDF reservist and lightly injured a battalion commander near the Lebanon border. An Iranian ballistic missile was intercepted over Lebanese airspace for the first time in the conflict — a new trajectory approach analysts say aims to bypass southern Israeli air defenses.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM 10,000-target claim confirmed by Admiral Cooper video statement. Kuwait airport fire confirmed by Al Arabiya and Kuwait civil aviation authority. Halawi killing confirmed by IDF and Lebanese civil defense. Lebanon intercept confirmed by IDF and OSINT analysts.

sources.name: VINnews — B-52 bombers delivering 70,000 lbs of munitions per mission

title: Saudi Arabia grants US King Fahd Air Base access; UAE moves toward war entry; Houthi missile hits Israeli tanker in Red Sea
description: Saudi Arabia agreed to grant the US military access to King Fahd Air Base, reversing its earlier refusal, according to reporting confirmed by Bloomberg and i24. The UAE is discussing troop deployment and is encouraging the US to not agree to a ceasefire before Iran's military capabilities are further degraded. Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan warned 'Saudi Arabia's patience with Iranian attacks is not unlimited.' Separately, Houthi spokesman Yahya Saree claimed a ballistic missile directly struck the Israeli-owned tanker Scarlet Ray (Liberia-flagged) in the northern Red Sea near Yanbu — confirmed by UKMTO; vessel continued voyage with no crew injuries. Guterres declared the war 'out of control' and appointed veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as UN personal envoy for Iran-Israel conflict resolution. Two competing UNSC draft resolutions on Hormuz are in blue — one from Bahrain/GCC condemning Iranian obstruction, one from Russia that avoids naming any party.
factCheckNote: Saudi/UAE war entry reporting confirmed by Bloomberg, i24, WSJ. Houthi tanker claim confirmed by UKMTO. Guterres statement confirmed by UN News. UNSC draft resolutions confirmed by Security Council Report.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Saudis, UAE take some steps toward joining Iran war
UN News — Guterres declares war out of control, appoints personal envoy
Anadolu Agency — Houthis claim ballistic missile strike on Israeli tanker

title: IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed; Iran attacks UAE killing 2; IDF soldier Greenberg killed in Lebanon
description: In a major targeted killing, Israel struck IRGC Navy headquarters in Bandar Abbas, killing IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri — the architect of the Strait of Hormuz blockade — at approximately 3 a.m. local time. Defense Minister Katz: 'The man directly responsible for the terrorist operation of mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz was blown up and eliminated. The IDF will hunt you down and eliminate you one by one.' Tangsiri commanded the IRGC Navy since 2018. Iran has not officially confirmed his death. In parallel, Iran launched ballistic missile attacks against UAE targets, with 2 people killed in Abu Dhabi by falling debris from an intercepted missile. Saudi Arabia intercepted a drone in the Eastern Province. Kuwait and Bahrain also reported attacks. In Lebanon, Staff Sergeant Ori Greenberg, 21, of Golani Brigade's reconnaissance unit was killed in a gunfight with Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon — the 3rd IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since the war began. IDF also confirmed all 6 Litani River bridges used by Hezbollah have now been destroyed, trapping approximately 150,000 civilians in the south.
factCheckNote: Tangsiri killing confirmed by IDF/Defense Minister Katz; Washington Post, Al Jazeera reporting. UAE attack/casualties confirmed by Times of Israel Day 27 liveblog and Al Jazeera. Greenberg killing confirmed by Haaretz, IDF statement.

title: IRGC Wave 81 'True Promise 4' strikes 70+ Israeli targets; Pentagon preparing 'final blow' options including Kharg Island seizure; Iraq authorizes military response to US strikes
description: The IRGC launched Wave 81 ('True Promise 4'), firing Emad, Qiam, Khorramshahr-4, and Qadr ballistic missiles at Haifa, Dimona area, and Tel Aviv. IRGC claimed 70+ Israeli targets struck; 12 injured in Bnei Brak from cluster munition debris. Iran also fired multiple additional missile salvos at central Israel with sirens near Jerusalem. Axios reported the Pentagon is finalizing 'final blow' options for Iran, with Kharg Island seizure on the table. Iran responded by fortifying Kharg Island with mines, MANPADs, and additional troops; Iranian parliamentary speaker Ghalibaf warned of 'occupation attempt.' One MEU is set to arrive this week, and 82nd Airborne plus 2 MEUs would give the US approximately 6,000–8,000 ground troops near Iran. In Iraq, the Ministerial Council for National Security formally authorized the PMF (Popular Mobilization Forces) to 'confront and respond to military attacks from US aircraft and drones using available means in accordance with the right to self-defense' — the first formal Iraqi government authorization for military counter-response to US strikes. Trump on Truth Social responding to Iran's rejection of the 15-point plan: 'WRONG!!! They better get serious soon, before it is too late, because once that happens, there is NO TURNING BACK, and it won't be pretty!'
factCheckNote: IRGC Wave 81 confirmed by PressTV, Times of Israel liveblog. Pentagon 'final blow' options reported by Axios. Kharg Island fortification confirmed by CNN. Iraq authorization confirmed by Al Jazeera. Trump Truth Social post confirmed by NBC News live blog.

title: IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed in Bandar Abbas strike; IDF Sgt. Volansky killed in Lebanon Merkava attack; Iran fires Wave 82 at Israel
description: Israel killed IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri — the architect of the Strait of Hormuz blockade — in a strike on the IRGC naval HQ in Bandar Abbas at approximately 03:30 local time. Two senior aides were also killed: Behnam Rezaei (Navy Intel Director) and Gen. Masib Bakhtiari (First Fleet Commander). Defense Minister Katz: 'The man directly responsible for the Hormuz chokehold was blown up and eliminated. The IDF will hunt you down one by one.' CENTCOM's Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed the killing: 'Strikes on the Iranian navy would continue.' Hegseth at the Cabinet meeting later said Iran 'now has no navy, no navy leader.' Iran launched IRGC Wave 82 (True Promise 4) — ballistic missiles and drones at US-Israeli targets, with two salvos against central Israel in the morning. Sgt. Aviaad Elchanan Volansky (21, 7th Armored Brigade) was killed when a Hezbollah anti-tank missile penetrated his Merkava tank in southern Lebanon — 4th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon. Trophy APS intercepted the first missile; the second penetrated. Four other soldiers wounded. Iran also struck UAE — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris. IDF struck Mashhad (14th Air Force base — most northeastern target yet), Tabriz airport (two F-5/F-4 jets destroyed), Shiraz command center, and ballistic missile sites in Kermanshah/Dezful (~70 munitions). Hezbollah rocket killed 1 in Nahariya, wounded 14. Kuwait announced arrest of 6-member Hezbollah assassination cell targeting state leaders. Trump Cabinet meeting: declared Iran 'begging to make a deal,' revealed Iran allowed 8-10 tankers through Hormuz as a 'present,' said US would 'keep blowing them away unimpeded.' WSJ reported Trump told aides to 'wrap up' conflict 'in coming weeks.' Brent crude spiked to $105.85/bbl on war escalation following Iran's firm rejection of 15-point peace plan. AAA gas $3.981/gal nationally — $0.019 from $4.00 milestone. Hormuz blockade Day 24.
factCheckNote: Tangsiri death confirmed by IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, WaPo, Euronews. UAE deaths confirmed by Al Arabiya and The National. Volansky death confirmed by IDF. Wave 82 confirmed by NewsFirst, Times of Israel liveblog. Oil prices confirmed by Fortune, FX Leaders. Hezbollah Nahariya rocket attack confirmed by Times of Israel MDA report.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iranian naval commander Alireza Tangsiri killed in attack, Israel says
CNBC — Strait of Hormuz: Israel kills IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri
CNN — Iran war live: Trump casts doubt on prospect of an Iran deal

title: Trump extends energy pause to April 6 (Truth Social); Kallas: Russia giving Iran intel to kill Americans at G7; Brent surges to  — S&P -1.74% worst day of war; Pakistan on-record confirms mediator
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social extending the pause on striking Iranian energy plants by 10 more days to April 6, 2026 at 8 PM ET, citing an Iranian government request. "Talks are ongoing and going very well." The extension came after Iran denied ongoing negotiations — causing Brent crude to surge from ~ to .99–/bbl and WTI to ~.48 before the announcement brought some stability. The S&P 500 fell 1.74%, the worst single-day drop of the war. At the G7 foreign ministers meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas disclosed: "We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans." UK Defence Secretary John Healey confirmed with British intelligence backing. France also confirmed the cooperation. US special envoy Steve Witkoff diverged sharply, saying Russia denied the claim and the US would take them "at their word." Pakistani FM Ishaq Dar made the first on-record confirmation that Pakistan is actively mediating indirect US-Iran talks: "In reality, US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan." Iran FM Araghchi maintained there are no negotiations — only message exchanges. Turkish and Egyptian officials also confirmed supporting the mediation effort. G7 foreign ministers, meeting in France, described the Iran war as "a catastrophe" for the global economy but acknowledged little ability to force an end to it. Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) announced she is drafting a formal AUMF (Authorization for Use of Military Force) for the Iran war, saying the administration won't answer questions about war scope or end goals.
factCheckNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by NPR, Bloomberg, NBC News. Kallas statement confirmed by CBS News, Bloomberg, Euronews. Pakistan confirmation confirmed by NPR/OPB, Times of Israel liveblog. Market data confirmed by NBC News, Al Jazeera economic tracking.

CBS News — Russia helping Iran with intel to target and kill Americans
Bloomberg — Trump Extends Pause of Iran Energy Strikes to April 6

title: IRGC Wave 83 targets four US Gulf bases and Israeli cities — Al-Dhafra, Al-Udeiri, Ali Al-Salem, Sheikh Isa, Ashdod, Modi'in
description: Iran's IRGC launched Wave 83 of Operation True Promise 4, the most geographically dispersed strike of the conflict. Targets included Al-Dhafra Air Base (UAE), Al-Udeiri Air Base (Saudi Arabia), Ali Al-Salem Air Base (Kuwait), and Sheikh Isa Air Base (Bahrain) — hitting all four major US military hubs in the Gulf simultaneously. The IRGC also targeted an oil depot and storage tanks in Ashdod, Israel, and a military personnel site in Modi'in. A Patriot missile system maintenance and repair hangar at Sheikh Isa was specifically targeted. The IRGC claimed missiles carried messages thanking Spain, Pakistan, India, and Germany for their solidarity against the war.
factCheckNote: IRGC statement confirmed by Tribune India, ANI News, GlobalSecurity.org/PressTV. Multiple Gulf defense ministries acknowledged attacks.

sources.name: Tribune India — Iran launches 83rd wave of retaliatory strikes

title: IAF destroys Yazd naval missile and sea mine production facility — Iran's most central naval arms site
description: The Israeli Air Force struck Iran's most central facility for developing, assembling, and storing advanced naval missiles and sea mines in Yazd. The site produced missiles for launch from submarines, helicopters, and cruise platforms, and manufactured sea mines deployed in the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The IDF described it as a significant blow to the production capabilities of Iran's naval forces. The strike was guided by Military Intelligence and Naval Intelligence Branch. CENTCOM simultaneously confirmed 92% of Iran's large naval ships have been eliminated under Operation Epic Fury.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel liveblog, The Media Line.

title: IAF 60-jet wave destroys Tehran weapon manufacturing complexes and IRGC facilities
description: Israel deployed approximately 60 IAF fighter jets dropping over 150 munitions on weapon manufacturing sites in Tehran and surrounding areas. Targets included dozens of weapon manufacturing facilities, a military base storing anti-aircraft systems, a production site for key ballistic missile components, a battery production site, and an IRGC weapons production facility. Iranian soldiers identified at a Tehran building as preparing missile fire were targeted and killed in the strike. IDF also completed a simultaneous wave of extensive strikes in Isfahan targeting infrastructure. The operation accelerated Israel's stated goal of degrading Iran's missile production capabilities before any potential ceasefire.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel Day 28 liveblog, Jerusalem Post.

title: Pentagon weighs 10,000 more troops to Middle East — 82nd Airborne and armor
description: The Pentagon is actively planning the deployment of up to 10,000 additional US ground troops to the Middle East, including infantry and armored units from the 82nd Airborne Division. This would supplement the 5,000+ Marines and paratroopers already en route. The deployment is being considered in the context of escalatory scenarios, including the Kharg Island seizure option. WSJ had separately reported that Trump told aides he wants to wrap up the conflict in coming weeks, tied to his May 14-15 China trip deadline.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Stars and Stripes, Jerusalem Post. WSJ Kharg/troop reporting also confirmed.

title: Rubio spars with G7 allies in France — Germany calls war a catastrophe, 35 nations join Hormuz reopening talks
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with G7 foreign ministers at Vaux-de-Cernay Abbey outside Paris. Germany Defense Minister Boris Pistorius called the war a catastrophe for the world's economies. France's top military official said allies were surprised by an American ally who doesn't even bother to inform us when it decides to engage in military operations. Rubio defended the war, saying countries should be grateful the US is willing to confront the threat. On the separate accusation by Kallas that Russia is giving Iran intel to kill Americans, Rubio downplayed the claim, saying Russia denied it and the US takes them at their word. Separately, 35 nations joined a France-hosted military planning conference on how to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after hostilities end. Brent crude: .99/bbl (+4.1%). WTI: .48/bbl (+4.6%). S&P 500 fell 1.74% on March 26 — worst day of the war.
factCheckNote: G7 meeting confirmed by Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, Washington Post. Oil prices confirmed by NBC News, FX Leaders. S&P confirmed by NBC News.

title: Kuwait dual port strike — Shuwaikh and Mubarak Al-Kabeer hit at dawn
description: Iranian drones and cruise missiles struck both of Kuwait's major commercial ports in a pre-dawn attack on Day 28. Shuwaikh Port, Kuwait's main commercial hub near Kuwait City, sustained confirmed infrastructure damage. Mubarak Al-Kabeer Port was struck by a combined drone and cruise missile attack. Kuwait Ports Authority confirmed material damage at both sites. No casualties were reported. The dual-port strike is the most significant single attack on Kuwaiti commercial infrastructure since the conflict began, signaling Iran's willingness to target Gulf state logistics.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Kuwait Ports Authority, Al-Arabiya, Gulf News, The National, Anadolu Agency.

title: IDF strikes Arak reactor (2nd), Ardakan yellowcake (first ever), Isfahan steel plants — major nuclear escalation
description: Israel conducted a significant escalation in Day 28, simultaneously striking three major Iranian nuclear and industrial facilities. The Khondab Heavy Water Research Reactor in Arak was struck for the second time in this war; IAEA confirmed no nuclear material was present and no radiological risk. Most significantly, Israel struck the Ardakan yellowcake uranium extraction facility in Yazd Province for the first time — IDF described it as 'the only one of its kind in Iran,' a critical step in uranium processing. Two steel factories were also struck: Mobarakeh Steel (Isfahan Province) and Khuzestan Steel, killing 1 and wounding 15. Iranian FM Araghchi alleged Israel claimed to have coordinated the strikes with the US, a claim the US did not confirm. The IRGC subsequently issued an 'beyond eye for eye' retaliation threat and evacuation warning for US/Israeli-linked industrial workers across the Gulf.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IDF statement, IAEA (no radiation risk at Arak), Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel. 1 killed, 15 injured at Isfahan steel plants confirmed by Iranian state media.

title: Iran turns back 3 Chinese ships at Hormuz — Brent $112.57, WTI crosses $100 for first time
description: The IRGC Navy turned back three vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island. The blocked ships included CSCL Indian Ocean and CSCL Arctic Ocean (COSCO/China) and the Marshall Islands-flagged Lotus Rising. Iran's IRGC confirmed the strait remains shut to unauthorized vessels, directly contradicting Trump's public claim it was open. Ship-tracking data confirmed all three vessels U-turned in the strait. Brent crude spiked to $112.57/barrel — the highest level since 2022 — while WTI briefly crossed $100/barrel for the first time since this conflict began. Macquarie issued a warning that oil could reach $200/barrel if Hormuz remains closed through June.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg, CNBC, FDD analysis, Newsweek. Ship-tracking data corroborates IRGC turn-back claim. Brent $112.57 confirmed by Bloomberg/CNN settlement data.

title: Araghchi denies all negotiations, vows heavy price — IRGC issues 'beyond eye for eye' threat and industrial evacuation warning
description: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi explicitly denied that any negotiations with Israel or the US had taken place, contradicting both Pakistani diplomatic confirmation and Trump's optimistic framing. 'No negotiations have happened with the enemy until now, and we do not plan on any negotiations,' he stated, noting that passing messages through mediators is not negotiations. He vowed Iran would exact a 'heavy price for Israeli crimes' following strikes on the Arak reactor, Ardakan yellowcake facility, and Isfahan steel plants. Separately, IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Seyed Majid Moosavi publicly warned workers at US- and Israeli-linked industrial sites across the Gulf to evacuate immediately, stating Iran's retaliation would go 'beyond a simple proportional response — beyond eye for eye.' Iran also officially accepted a UN request for humanitarian aid and agricultural shipping to pass safely through Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Araghchi denial confirmed by Al Jazeera, The Hill, PBS. IRGC evacuation warning confirmed by Press TV, Euronews. UN humanitarian passage confirmed by Iranian UN Mission statement.

title: IDF second 'extensive' Beirut wave — 2 soldiers killed in south Lebanon ground clashes
description: Israeli Air Force launched a second 'extensive' wave of strikes against Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut's Dahiyeh district, with advance evacuation warnings for Bourj el-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik, and Chiyah. Earlier in the day, two IDF soldiers were killed in ground fighting in southern Lebanon villages of Bayada and Shamaa where Hezbollah engaged IDF forces at close quarters using light and medium weapons. Cumulative Lebanon death toll reached 1,142. Separately, Dan Accadia Resort hotel in Herzliya, central Israel, sustained damage from Iranian ballistic missile fragments — possibly cluster submunitions — with no injuries.
factCheckNote: Second Beirut wave confirmed by IDF statement, Jerusalem Post. 2 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon confirmed by IDF. Lebanon death toll per Lebanese Health Ministry. Herzliya hotel damage confirmed by Israeli rescue services.

title: Vance confronts Netanyahu on regime-change promises, confirmed as lead Iran negotiator — German FM says direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan 'prepared'
description: VP JD Vance was formally designated by Trump as lead Iran negotiator, overseeing the 15-point deal negotiations with Witkoff and Kushner. Before this announcement, Vance had confronted Netanyahu in a 'difficult' phone call, challenging him for overselling the likelihood of Iranian regime change before the war started. 'Before the war, Bibi really sold it to the president as being easy, as regime change being a lot likelier than it was,' a senior US official told Axios. Separately, German FM Johann Wadephul confirmed on record that the US and Iran have been in indirect contact and a direct meeting was 'prepared' and expected 'very soon in Pakistan.' Wadephul called it 'first signs of hope and trust.' Turkey and Egypt also confirmed they have been passing messages between Tehran and Washington. Rubio told the G7 the war would continue 2-4 more weeks.
factCheckNote: Vance appointment and Netanyahu confrontation confirmed by Axios, Times of Israel, CBS News. German FM Wadephul Pakistan direct meeting confirmed by Al Arabiya, Times of Israel liveblog. Rubio 2-4 weeks timeline confirmed by Axios, Bloomberg, NPR.

title: IRGC Wave 84 — strikes Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj), claims US aerial refueling fleet destroyed
description: Iran launched Wave 84 of Operation True Promise 4 — the final IRGC strike wave of Day 28 — targeting Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj) in Saudi Arabia. The IRGC Aerospace Force and IRGC Navy conducted a joint solid- and liquid-fueled missile and drone attack, claiming to have destroyed US aerial refueling assets including wide-body support aircraft and tankers. IRGC stated it destroyed anti-missile defense systems at the base. Wave 84 was dedicated to martyrs of IRIB (Iranian state broadcasting). US forces at Al-Kharj include units supporting Operation Epic Fury air operations.
factCheckNote: IRGC claim of US refueling fleet destruction not confirmed by CENTCOM or Pentagon as of publication. IRGC casualty/damage claims routinely overstated.

title: Iranian cluster warhead kills 1 civilian in central Israel — Day 28 ends with 6 missile salvos
description: Day 28 of the conflict ended with Iran firing six separate ballistic missile salvos at Israel. In the fifth salvo, an Iranian ballistic missile carrying a cluster bomb warhead struck a residential area in central Israel. One man in his 60s was killed when a cluster submunition struck near a residential building while he was outside a shelter. Two men in their 50s were lightly injured in the same strike. Fire and Rescue teams responded to six separate bomblet impact sites. The attack brought the total confirmed Israeli civilian deaths in the conflict to 19. The cluster warhead attack reflects Iran’s escalating use of indiscriminate munitions targeting Israeli population centers, which Human Rights Watch had previously documented at Ramat Gan (March 17) and Kafr Qasim.
factCheckNote: One civilian killed confirmed by Israeli emergency services and Times of Israel liveblog. Cluster munition use confirmed by Fire and Rescue multi-site response. Six salvo count confirmed by IDF.

title: Houthis fire first missile at Israel — war spreads to 5th front as Yemen joins conflict
description: Day 29 opened with a dramatic escalation: Yemen's Houthi movement fired its first ballistic missile at Israel since the start of the 2026 Iran War. Houthi military spokesman Brigadier General Yahya Saree announced on Al-Masirah TV the launch of a 'barrage of ballistic missiles' targeting 'sensitive Israeli military sites.' The IDF confirmed the missile was intercepted. The Houthis had previously abstained at Tehran's request, but reversed course on Day 29 — joining Iran, Hezbollah, Iraqi militias, and direct Iranian forces as the fifth front against Israel. Analysts warned a Houthi move to also close the Bab al-Mandeb strait could create a second major shipping blockade.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the launch and interception. Houthi spokesman Yahya Saree claimed responsibility on Al-Masirah TV. Reported across Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Washington Post, Euronews.

Haaretz — Houthis Join Iran War Firing First Missile at Israel

title: Iran drones strike Kuwait Airport (radar damaged), Salalah Port Oman, UAE industrial zone — widening Gulf attacks
description: Iran launched coordinated drone and missile attacks on three Gulf states in the pre-dawn hours of Day 29. In Kuwait, drones struck Kuwait International Airport and targeted two commercial ports (Shuwaikh and Mubarak Al-Kabeer), significantly damaging the airport's radar system. Kuwait's air defense intercepted 7 incoming projectiles but the airport radar took a direct hit. In Oman, two Iranian drones struck the Port of Salalah — a major regional container hub — injuring one foreign worker and damaging a crane. The IRGC claimed the Oman strike targeted a US logistics vessel. In the UAE, Iran fired cruise missiles and drones at an industrial zone, triggering fires and injuring 5 people. The triple-state attack reflects Iran's continued use of Gulf infrastructure pressure as a strategic tool.
factCheckNote: Kuwait civil aviation confirmed radar damage via KUNA. Oman government confirmed Salalah port attack via Oman Observer. UAE defense ministry confirmed industrial zone attack. All three reported across regional media.

title: Trump calls NATO 'paper tiger,' demands allies send warships to Hormuz
description: President Trump sharply escalated his criticism of NATO on Day 29, calling the alliance a 'paper tiger' on Truth Social. Trump wrote that NATO countries 'didn't want to join the fight to stop a Nuclear Powered Iran' and now complained about high oil prices while refusing to help open the Strait of Hormuz. Trump warned he would scale back US military commitments to NATO members that refused to contribute warships to Hormuz operations. He also stated the US had 'thousands of targets left to strike' in Iran — pushing back on reports the war was near conclusion. Secretary of State Rubio, speaking at the G7 meeting in France, simultaneously stated the war would end in 'weeks, not months' and required 'no ground troops.' The conflicting signals from Trump and Rubio created confusion among allies about US end-state objectives.
factCheckNote: Trump Truth Social posts confirmed. Rubio G7 statements confirmed by multiple outlets including Times Live, The Hill, and AP.

sources.name: The Hill — Trump calls NATO a 'paper tiger'
The Hill — Rubio says Iran war to last weeks not months

title: Pakistan hosts emergency Islamabad summit — Saudi, Turkey, Egypt foreign ministers converge for Iran peace talks
description: Pakistan hosted a significant diplomatic summit on Day 29 as the foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt arrived in Islamabad for emergency talks on ending the Iran War. Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar confirmed the meeting. The summit represents the most substantial collective diplomatic effort by Muslim-majority nations to achieve a ceasefire. Iranian President Pezeshkian spoke with Pakistan PM Sharif ahead of the talks, affirming Iran's conditions: recognition of its legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm international guarantees against future aggression. Iran had previously submitted a 5-point counterproposal through Pakistani intermediaries after formally rejecting the US 15-point ceasefire framework. Germany's FM Wadephul said a direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan was being 'prepared for very soon.'
factCheckNote: Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar confirmed the summit. Pezeshkian call with Pakistan PM Sharif confirmed via Pezeshkian's X post. German FM Wadephul statement confirmed by Al Arabiya.

title: Israel kills 2 journalists in south Lebanon — Al Manar, Al Mayadeen correspondents targeted in Jezzine; Bourj Hammoud strike kills 1
description: Israel struck and killed two journalists in the Jezzine area of south Lebanon on the morning of Day 29. Ali Shoeib, a correspondent for Hezbollah-linked Al Manar TV, and Fatima Ftouni, a reporter for Al Mayadeen, were both killed in the strike. The IDF said Shoeib was an IRGC intelligence operative 'operating under the guise of a journalist.' Lebanese President Joseph Aoun condemned the killings as a violation of press freedom and international law. A separate Israeli airstrike struck an apartment building in Bourj Hammoud, northeastern Beirut, killing at least one person and wounding four others. Smoke was visible over southern Beirut suburbs from overnight Israeli strikes on Dahiyeh. The killings drew international condemnation from press freedom organizations. Lebanon's total conflict death toll now stands at 1,145+.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the Jezzine strike. Al Manar and Al Mayadeen confirmed the deaths of their correspondents. Lebanese President Aoun's condemnation confirmed by Al Jazeera and regional media.

Al Jazeera — Iran war live: journalists killed in Lebanon

title: Houthis fire first ballistic missile at Israel — 5th active front opens
description: Yemen's Houthi movement entered the 2026 Iran-Israel war on Day 29 by firing a barrage of ballistic missiles at Israel — the first Houthi attack on Israel since the war began February 28. Houthi Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree announced on Al Masirah TV that the operation targeted 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel, with Beersheba the primary target area near the Negev Nuclear Research Center. The IDF confirmed missile interception with no casualties or significant damage. Senior Houthi official Mohammed Mansour warned that closing the Bab al-Mandeb Strait is 'among our options.' The Houthi entry opens a fifth simultaneous active front: US-Israel direct strikes on Iran, Hezbollah in Lebanon, Iraqi militias, and now Houthis in Yemen. A Bab al-Mandeb closure would threaten a second major global shipping chokepoint — roughly 30% of Israel's imports and  trillion in annual global trade use the route.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed interception. Houthi spokesman Yahya Saree claimed responsibility on Al Masirah TV. Confirmed by Al Jazeera, NBC News, PBS, CNBC, Military.com.

title: Vance: US 'taking care of business,' will be out 'soon'; Rubio: war ends in 'weeks not months'
description: Vice President JD Vance signaled the war is nearing conclusion in an afternoon podcast interview: 'We are taking care of business, we are going to be out of there soon, and gas prices will come back down.' He said the US has 'accomplished the vast majority of our military projects' and the war will continue only 'a little while longer' to ensure Iran is 'neutered for a very, very long time.' Secretary of State Rubio told reporters after a G7 meeting in Fiuggi, France that the war would conclude 'in a matter of weeks, not months,' with the goal of destroying Iran's capacity to manufacture missiles and drones. Iran has reportedly indicated it prefers Vance to lead any US negotiating delegation in potential Islamabad talks. The signals come as an Islamabad peace summit convenes with Saudi, Turkish, and Egyptian foreign ministers in Pakistan, and as Iranian President Pezeshkian told Pakistan PM Sharif 'trust is needed' for talks to succeed. A Fox News poll simultaneously showed 64% of Americans disapprove of Trump's handling of the Iran war.
factCheckNote: Vance interview with Benny Johnson confirmed. Rubio remarks at G7 Fiuggi, France confirmed by State Department transcript and CNBC. Pezeshkian-Sharif call confirmed by Iranian state media.

sources.name: Haaretz — Vance: We're Taking Care of Business

title: Lebanon toll reaches 1,189 — 9 paramedics killed; anti-war protests in Israel; 3rd journalist confirmed
description: Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 47 killed and 112 wounded in the past 24 hours, bringing the cumulative toll since March 2 to at least 1,189 confirmed killed including 124 children. WHO confirmed 9 paramedics were killed and 7 wounded in five separate Israeli strikes on emergency responders in southern Lebanon on Day 29, with 51 total medical workers killed in Lebanon since March 2. A third journalist killed in the Jezzine strike was confirmed: Mohammed Ftouni, brother of Al Mayadeen reporter Fatima Ftouni, was a freelance video journalist traveling in the same marked media vehicle. In Israel, approximately 1,200 protesters gathered at Tel Aviv's Habima Square demanding an end to the war; 18 were arrested (13 in Tel Aviv, 5 in Haifa). This was the first anti-war rally where center-right opposition groups joined left-wing organizers — a signal of widening Israeli public skepticism.
factCheckNote: Lebanese Health Ministry figures confirmed by Al Jazeera. WHO paramedic death toll confirmed by WHO statement. Mohammed Ftouni death confirmed by CPJ and Washington Post. Tel Aviv protest confirmed by Haaretz and CNN.

The missile was intercepted by IDF air defenses; sirens sounded across Beersheba and surrounding Negev communities. Falling interception debris injured 11 people in Eshtaol, central Israel. Hours later, Houthis launched a second cruise missile at Israel, also intercepted. Houthi spokesman Brig. Gen. title: Iran fires cluster munitions over Tel Aviv — 1 killed, 8 impact sites
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles carrying cluster munitions at Tel Aviv overnight March 28-29. CNN journalists on the ground witnessed cluster munitions exploding over the city. Eight impact sites were reported across Tel Aviv including at a university. A 60-year-old male was killed and two others injured. Separately, IDF launched a 'widespread wave of strikes' against Iranian military infrastructure in the heart of Tehran overnight — targeting ballistic missile production facilities, storage sites, command centers, and the HQ of an Iranian naval weapons and ship producer. Explosions were heard across Tehran and smoke visible from the north.
factCheckNote: Tel Aviv cluster munitions and casualty confirmed by Times of Israel and CNN. IDF Tehran strike wave confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel liveblogs.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live Day 30

title: IDF soldier killed in south Lebanon; IRGC threatens Gulf universities; WHO: 9 paramedics killed
description: IDF Sgt. Moshe Yitzchak HaCohen Katz, 22, of the 890th Paratroopers Battalion, was killed after Hezbollah fired rockets in south Lebanon (Bayada/Shamaa). Three soldiers were moderately wounded. Katz was a US-born lone soldier from New Haven, CT. Separately, Iran's IRGC declared US and Israeli-affiliated universities in the Middle East 'legitimate targets' — including Texas A&M Qatar, Northwestern Qatar, and NYU Abu Dhabi — demanding the US condemn reported strikes on Iranian universities by March 30 noon. WHO confirmed 9 paramedics were killed and 7 wounded in 5 separate Israeli strikes in Lebanon; 51 medical workers have been killed in Lebanon since March 2, and 4 hospitals plus 50+ health centers forced to close.
factCheckNote: IDF soldier death confirmed by IDF and Jerusalem Post. IRGC university threat confirmed by NBC News. WHO paramedic count confirmed by WHO statement and Haaretz.

Jerusalem Post — IDF soldier killed in south Lebanon
NBC News — Iran war live blog

title: Islamabad summit: Saudi, Turkish, Egyptian FMs meet Pakistan — Iran allows Pakistani ships through Hormuz
description: Foreign ministers from Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt arrived in Islamabad for a 2-day emergency peace summit hosted by Pakistan. As a major diplomatic breakthrough, Iran agreed to allow 20 ships per day under the Pakistani flag through the Strait of Hormuz — a first partial opening of the blockade. Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar confirmed the arrangement. Germany's FM Wadephul said a direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan is 'prepared for very soon.' China's Wang Yi praised Pakistan's mediation. Pakistan PM Sharif confirmed a 1-hour call with Iranian President Pezeshkian. The summit represents the most significant diplomatic push yet to end the war.
factCheckNote: Summit confirmed by Al Jazeera, US News, and Express Tribune. Pakistani-flagged ship arrangement confirmed by Pakistan FM. German FM statement confirmed by Arab News.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live Day 30 (Islamabad summit)
Express Tribune — Pakistan hosts critical Iran peace talks
Al Jazeera — As war on Iran enters second month, Yemen's Houthis open new front

title: Day 30 summary: War enters second month — 1,963+ killed in Iran, interceptor shortage deepens
description: The conflict enters its second month with no end in sight despite accelerating diplomacy. Key figures: Iran 1,963+ killed (24,910+ injured); Lebanon 1,189+ killed; Israel 26 killed; US 13 killed, 315+ wounded. Vance confirmed as lead US peace negotiator alongside Witkoff and Kushner; Rubio says war ends in 'weeks not months.' Trump extended April 6 deadline on Iranian power grid strikes to allow Islamabad talks to proceed. Iran rejected a 15-point US peace proposal but allowed limited Hormuz passage for Pakistani ships. RUSI study warns US/Israel running critically low on missile interceptors — Arrow 2/3 and THAAD stocks deemed 'unsustainable' at current interception rates. Oil markets: Brent $107.81/bbl (+35% from pre-war), US gas at $3.98/gal national average.
factCheckNote: Death tolls from Al Jazeera tracker and Lebanon Health Ministry. Interceptor shortage from RUSI/Haaretz. Vance role confirmed by Axios. Oil/gas prices from Fortune/AAA.

sources.name: NPR — Iran war at one month
Al Jazeera — As war on Iran enters second month, Yemen's Houthis open new front
Haaretz — Israel and U.S. Running Low on Interceptors

title: IRGC strikes Bahrain's Alba and UAE's Emirates Global Aluminium — first industrial sector attack on GCC
description: Iran's IRGC launched a combined missile and drone attack on two of the Gulf's largest aluminium producers: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) and Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) at Abu Dhabi's Khalifa Economic Zone. The IRGC claimed both facilities are 'affiliated with US military and aerospace sectors.' EGA suffered significant damage. Alba confirmed two employees were lightly wounded. The strikes mark Iran's first deliberate targeting of Gulf industrial/manufacturing infrastructure directly linked to Western defense supply chains.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, Arab News, and New Arab. Alba corporate statement confirmed employee injuries.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran's IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities
CNBC — Bahrain aluminium giant says Iranian attack targeted its facility

title: Imagery confirms E-3G AWACS totally destroyed at Prince Sultan — first US strategic aircraft loss of conflict
description: Open-source imagery published March 29 confirmed the total destruction of USAF E-3G Sentry AWACS (tail 81-0005) at Prince Sultan Air Base, struck in the March 27 IRGC attack. The fuselage was split in two and the radar dome displaced. One KC-135 also completely destroyed; three others severely damaged. This represents the first loss of a US strategic command-and-control aircraft in combat since the Cold War. The E-3G provides airborne warning and control capability; the US AWACS fleet is now reduced from 16 to 15 active aircraft. Total US casualties at Prince Sultan: 15+ wounded (5 seriously).
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Air & Space Forces Magazine and The War Zone via open-source imagery. 15 US wounded per Haaretz/WaPo.

sources.name: Air & Space Forces Magazine — Key E-3 AWACS Damaged in Iranian Attack on Saudi Air Base
The War Zone — Images Purportedly Show E-3 Sentry Totally Destroyed

title: WaPo: Pentagon war-gaming Iran ground operations including Kharg Island raid — Rubio vs. Hegseth rift deepens
description: The Washington Post reported that the Pentagon is actively preparing ground operation plans for Iran, including potential raids on Kharg Island (Iran's main oil export facility) and Larak Island, and possible deployment of the 82nd Airborne Division. White House Press Secretary Leavitt acknowledged the planning: 'It's the job of the Pentagon to make preparations to give the Commander in Chief maximum optionality. It does not mean the president has made a decision.' Secretary Rubio publicly opposes ground troops; Secretary Hegseth supports the option. IDF Spokesperson Nadav Shoshani separately announced Israel is 'days away from completing all top-priority targets' in Iran and said US-Israel forces are 'well-coordinated, shoulder-to-shoulder.' Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned 'Iranian forces are ready to fire and punish any US soldiers who set foot on Iranian soil.'
factCheckNote: WaPo report confirmed via multiple outlets. White House quote per WaPo and CNN. IDF Spokesperson per Al Jazeera. Ghalibaf per Al Jazeera liveblog.

sources.name: Washington Post — Pentagon prepares for weeks of ground operations in Iran
Al Jazeera — Pentagon readies 'for weeks of US ground operations' in Iran

title: Iranian missile strikes Beersheba — 11 injured; fragment ignites ADAMA chemical plant in Ne'ot Hovav
description: An Iranian ballistic missile struck Beersheba in southern Israel, injuring 11 people with shrapnel and shock-wave flying objects, with 20 more treated for acute anxiety. A missile fragment simultaneously struck the ADAMA Makhteshim agricultural chemical plant in the nearby Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone, igniting a fire and triggering fears of a hazardous chemical leak. Israeli Home Front Command ordered residents to shelter indoors; Highway 40 closed. Three hazmat teams deployed. Environmental Protection Minister Idit Silman later issued an all-clear: no risk to the public. ADAMA (Syngenta Group subsidiary) manufactures pesticides, herbicides, and fungicides at the facility.
factCheckNote: MDA confirmed 11 injuries. ADAMA confirmed its plant was hit. Environment Ministry issued formal all-clear. All per Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post Day 30 liveblogs.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Iranian missile hit on Ne'ot Hovav factory
Ynet — Iran targets southern Israel: Be'er Sheva and Neot Hovav

title: Pope Leo XIV Palm Sunday: rejects war, says Jesus 'does not listen to prayers of those who wage war'
description: Pope Leo XIV addressed an estimated 80,000 pilgrims in St. Peter's Square for Palm Sunday Mass, delivering his 12th direct appeal since the war began for an end to the Iran-Israel conflict. He stated: 'This is our God: Jesus, King of Peace, who rejects war, whom no one can use to justify war. He does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war, but rejects them.' The address was widely interpreted as a direct condemnation of both US-Israeli offensive operations and Iranian retaliatory strikes. The Vatican has now called for peace at least 12 times since February 28.
factCheckNote: Vatican News and NPR confirmed the exact quote from the Palm Sunday homily, March 29, 2026.

sources.name: NPR — Pope Leo rejects claims God justifies war on Palm Sunday
Vatican News — Pope Leo XIV Palm Sunday

title: US-Israeli strike kills 5 at Bandar Khamir port — IRGC naval staging area near Strait of Hormuz
description: A combined US-Israeli airstrike struck the port quay of Bandar Khamir in Hormozgan Province, Iran, killing five people and injuring four others. Iran's IRNA news agency cited local officials condemning the strike. Two vessels and a vehicle were damaged. Bandar Khamir lies approximately 25 kilometers from the Strait of Hormuz and has served as a staging area for IRGC naval operations and small-boat harassment missions targeting regional shipping. The strike marks continued targeting of Iran's Hormuz-area infrastructure.
factCheckNote: IRNA confirmed 5 killed. Al Bawaba cited IRNA. Corroborated by Al Jazeera Day 30 liveblog.

sources.name: Al Bawaba — Strikes hit Iran's Bandar Khamir city, 5 killed

title: IDF evening Tehran wave — power outages across capital; 2 killed near Shaft
description: The IDF conducted a wide-scale evening wave of airstrikes across Tehran and surrounding areas, deploying dozens of fighter jets and dropping over 120 bombs on ballistic missile R&D complexes, weapons production sites, missile storage facilities, and air defense systems. Shrapnel from a strike hit a high-voltage power tower in Alborz province, causing widespread power outages across northeastern, western, central, and eastern Tehran. Civilian casualties: 3 wounded in Saadat Abad, 9 wounded in western Tehran, 2 killed and 5 wounded near Shaft city in Gilan Province.
factCheckNote: Power outages confirmed by Iranian state media. Casualties reported by Iranian Red Crescent and local officials. IDF confirmed strike wave.

title: IAEA confirms Khondab (IR-40) heavy water reactor destroyed — 'no longer operational'
description: The IAEA officially confirmed that Iran's Khondab heavy water research reactor (IR-40) near Arak, Markazi Province, 'sustained severe damage and is no longer operational.' The Agency noted no declared nuclear material was present. The IR-40 was a central element of Iran's heavy water program subject to JCPOA redesign agreements in 2015. This marked the confirmed destruction of a second significant nuclear facility after Fordow was struck earlier in the conflict.
factCheckNote: IAEA official confirmation at 20:28 UTC March 29. IDF also confirmed bombing.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF confirms bombing Iran's heavy water reactor at Arak
Türkiye Today — US-Israeli strikes hit Iran's Khondab facility at Arak

title: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon explosion — Indonesian battalion
description: A UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and one was critically injured after an explosion struck a UN peacekeeping position near Adchit Al-Qusayr in southern Lebanon. The position belonged to the Indonesian battalion. UNIFIL stated: 'Deliberate attacks on peacekeepers are grave violations of international humanitarian law and may amount to war crimes.' Investigation launched; origin not attributed. The death raised the toll of international forces killed in Lebanon to its highest since 2006.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by UNIFIL official statement and multiple international media outlets.

sources.name: RTE.ie — UNIFIL peacekeeper killed in Lebanon
Times of Israel — UNIFIL says one peacekeeper killed, one critically injured

title: Trump claims Iran agreed to 'most of' 15-point US demands; considers Kharg Island seizure
description: Aboard Air Force One, President Trump told reporters Iran had agreed to 'most of' the 15-point US demands conveyed through Pakistan, saying 'I do see a deal in Iran, yeah. Could be soon.' In a concurrent Financial Times interview, Trump stated he is still considering seizing Kharg Island — Iran's primary oil export hub handling ~90% of Iranian crude. Iran's FM Araghchi immediately contradicted Trump, calling the 15-point demands 'unreasonable.' Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf rejected the plan entirely, stating Iranian forces were 'waiting to set American soldiers on fire' if they entered Iran.
factCheckNote: Trump's claim that Iran 'agreed to most points' directly contradicted by Iran's Araghchi and Ghalibaf on same day.

title: Islamabad summit — Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt align on hosting US-Iran talks
description: Foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt met in Islamabad in the most significant regional diplomatic push since the war began. Pakistan announced it will host direct US-Iran talks 'in coming days.' FM Dar: 'Pakistan is very honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides.' All four nations expressed full support. China conveyed support for Pakistan's mediation role. Direct Rubio-Araghchi talks reportedly possible within 48-72 hours. Iran allowed 20 Pakistani-flagged ships through Hormuz as a goodwill gesture ahead of the summit.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple outlets including Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NPR. Pakistan's FM Dar confirmed on X and in press conference.

title: Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck — Iran confirms, fire contained
description: A US-Israeli airstrike hit a production unit at the Tabriz Petrochemical Company in northwestern Iran at approximately midnight Tehran time. IRNA reported the strike. Emergency teams rapidly contained the fire. The company CEO confirmed the strike live on Iranian state TV, stating no hazardous substances were released. Oil prices surged toward $115/barrel on reports of strikes on energy infrastructure. Kuwait's National Guard intercepted five drones in a simultaneous wave.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IRNA (Iran's official news agency), Anadolu Agency, Xinhua, and multiple other outlets.

Targets included IRGC ballistic missile R&D complexes, weapons production facilities, air defense systems, and ballistic missile storage and launch sites. IDF stated ~40 Tehran weapons facilities have been struck over two days. description: An Iranian drone struck a service building at a major power station and water desalination plant in Kuwait, killing one Indian worker and causing significant material damage. Kuwait's Ministry of Electricity officially confirmed the fatality. Kuwait's National Guard said it downed five drones in the attack wave. The Indian Embassy in Kuwait confirmed the death, stating that eight Indian nationals have now been killed across the wider West Asia conflict.

description: A coordinated barrage from Iran and Lebanon struck Haifa. An Iranian cluster munition hit five locations in Haifa and neighboring towns. Ten missiles were fired from Lebanon, most intercepted. A direct hit was confirmed on a tanker at the Bazan (ORL) oil refinery, igniting a fire and damaging a gasoline storage tank. Power lines hit by shrapnel caused localized outages. Bazan refinery shares fell 2.5%. This was the seventh Iranian wave since midnight. One person lightly wounded in nearby Shfaram.

title: IDF Alpinist Unit — first documented cross-border raid from Syrian Hermon to Lebanon
description: IDF's Alpinist Unit (mountain commandos) under the 810th Brigade / 210th Division conducted a rare cross-border operation, crossing on foot through deep snow from the Syrian Hermon to the Mount Dov area in southern Lebanon. Described as the first time the IDF has documented such an operation. Forces gathered intelligence and located Hezbollah infrastructure, uncovering bunkers and an extensive weapons cache including artillery, mortars, rocket launchers, explosives, and landmines.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IDF spokesperson and reported by Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, JNS.

title: Trump claims 'regime change achieved' — threatens to seize Kharg Island and 'take the oil'
description: In a Financial Times interview published March 30, Trump declared 'regime change' has been achieved in Iran and threatened to seize Kharg Island (Iran's main oil export terminal, handling ~90% of exports). He said: 'Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't. We have a lot of options.' He also threatened to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants and oil wells unless a deal is reached soon. He simultaneously posted on Truth Social that the US is 'in serious discussions with A NEW, AND MORE REASONABLE, REGIME.' Iran's government denied accepting any US framework. Pezeshkian's government warned that Iran's economy could 'collapse within 3-4 weeks' without a ceasefire.
factCheckNote: Trump's claim that Iran agreed to 'most of' the US 15 demands is disputed — Iran's FM Araghchi publicly denied accepting any US framework and called exchanges through mediators 'not negotiations.' Trump's claim that 'regime change has been achieved' is disputed by analysts — the Islamic Republic has not fallen and Mojtaba Khamenei continues to issue statements as Supreme Leader.

title: Spain closes airspace to US military — Trump threatens trade war with NATO ally
description: Spain's Defense Minister Margarita Robles formally closed Spanish airspace to all US military aircraft conducting operations related to the Iran war — the first NATO ally to take such a step. 'Neither the bases are authorised, nor is the use of Spanish airspace authorised for any actions related to the war in Iran.' The US had already relocated 15 aircraft from Rota and Morón bases after Spain's earlier ban on base use. Trump responded by threatening to 'cut off all dealings with Spain,' calling Spain 'terrible' and instructing Treasury Secretary Bessent to impose economic measures — potentially triggering the first major intra-NATO economic confrontation of the conflict.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Spanish Defense Ministry statement, Euronews, and Times of Israel.

Times of Israel — Trump threatens to cut all trade ties with Spain over Iran war refusal

description: Sergeant Liran Ben Zion, 19, from Holon, was killed when a Hezbollah anti-tank missile struck his Merkava tank during operations in southern Lebanon. An officer was seriously wounded in the same attack. Two additional anti-tank missiles were fired at responding troops with no additional casualties. The IDF simultaneously reported its forces reached the Litani River, marking a new advance in the ground operation. Ben Zion is the 7th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since the start of operations on March 2.
factCheckNote: Death confirmed by IDF announcement and Times of Israel liveblog.

title: 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two separate incidents — deadliest day for UN forces
description: March 30 became the deadliest day for UNIFIL forces in the conflict. In the first incident (early morning), an Indonesian peacekeeper was killed and another critically injured at Adchit Al-Qusayr from indirect fire. In the second incident, two more peacekeepers were killed when an explosion destroyed their vehicle near Bani Hayyan; a third peacekeeper was severely injured. The origin of both attacks remains under UNIFIL investigation. France called an emergency UN Security Council meeting. UN Secretary-General Guterres said the attacks 'may amount to war crimes.' Total UNIFIL killed in this conflict: at least 4.
factCheckNote: Both incidents confirmed by UNIFIL statements and Al Jazeera. Investigation ongoing.

HRW verified three confirmed attacks killing at least 4 Israeli civilians: 2 men in Yehud (March 9), a man and woman in Ramat Gan (March 18). Investigators reviewed 50+ videos and photographic evidence showing missiles releasing 21-80 submunitions each. HRW called Iran's actions 'unlawful' and said they 'may amount to war crimes' under the Convention on Cluster Munitions and international humanitarian law.

On ABC Good Morning America the same day, Rubio described finding Iranians willing to speak 'in ways that previous people in charge have not spoken to us.' title: FT: Hegseth broker attempted defense ETF investment before war — Pentagon denies 'entirely false'
description: Financial Times reports Hegseth's Morgan Stanley broker tried to invest multimillion dollars in BlackRock iShares Defense Industrials ETF before the war. Fund was too new so investment did not execute. Pentagon's Sean Parnell calls it 'entirely false and fabricated.'
factCheckNote: FT reports with evidence; Pentagon denies. Under investigation.

sources.name: CNBC — Hegseth defence investments Iran war
Al Jazeera — Pentagon denies Hegseth defence investment

title: IRGC drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC 'Al-Salmi' at Dubai port — 2M barrels, fire contained
description: Iranian attack drone struck the Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi while at anchor 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai. Tanker carrying ~2M barrels crude (1.2M Saudi, 800K Kuwaiti) bound for China. Fire contained by 04:30 local, no spill, 24 crew safe. 25th commercial vessel attacked since Hormuz closure.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, Gulf News, Khaleej Times.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Drone attack on Kuwaiti tanker
Bloomberg — Iran strikes Kuwait oil tanker

title: Saudi intercepts 10 drones + 8 ballistic missiles in overnight Iranian attack wave
description: Saudi air defenses intercepted 10 drones and 8 ballistic missiles overnight. Four missiles targeting Riyadh intercepted. Drone debris fell on 6 houses in Al-Kharj Governorate. Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain also reported interceptions.
factCheckNote: CNN and WaPo confirmed.

title: Overnight US-Israeli strikes hit Tabriz petrochemical, Yazd, Bandar Abbas, Parchin, Kish Island
description: Overnight strike wave expanded target list beyond Tehran/Karaj/Shiraz/Qom/Abadan. Tabriz petrochemical unit hit (fire contained per Fars). Strikes reported in Yazd, Bandar Abbas, Parchin, and Kish Island. Targets included ballistic missile component development and UAV engine production facilities.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and CNN.

title: Iranian cluster munition warhead strikes Bnei Brak, Ramat Gan, Petah Tikva — 12 wounded
description: Iranian missile with cluster munition warhead (~24 bomblets) penetrated defenses and dispersed submunitions over central Israel. 23-year-old man moderately injured, 11 lightly wounded including 6 children. Cars ablaze, workshop fire in Petah Tikva. HRW condemns use of cluster munitions.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Ynet, Times of Israel. HRW documented cluster munition use.

sources.name: Ynet — Iranian cluster missile hits central Israel
HRW — Iran Unlawful Cluster Munition Strikes

title: Iran Parliament approves Strait of Hormuz toll plan — bans US/Israeli vessels
description: Iranian parliament approved tolling plan for Hormuz: rial-denominated fees, ban on US/Israeli ships, ban on vessels from countries with unilateral sanctions against Iran. Speaker Ghalibaf warned he has intelligence of US plan to seize an Iranian island with regional ally support. Revenue potential ~$600-800M/month.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by BNN Bloomberg and India TV News.

sources.name: BNN Bloomberg — Iran Hormuz toll plan

title: 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon — first UNIFIL fatalities
description: Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two separate incidents within 24 hours (March 29-30). One killed at Adchit al-Qusayr, two killed near Bani Hayyan when vehicle exploded. First UNIFIL fatalities of this conflict. France seeks emergency UNSC meeting.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by France 24, Al Jazeera, UN News, CBC.

title: Rubio: war 'weeks not months' — Hormuz will reopen 'one way or another'
description: Secretary of State Rubio told Al Jazeera war objectives achievable in weeks, confirmed communication with Iran through intermediaries. On regime change: 'Would we be heartbroken? Absolutely not.' Called Iran's Hormuz tolling 'illegal, unacceptable, dangerous.' Criticized NATO allies refusing US base access.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera interview. State Dept transcript published.

title: Knesset approves record $45.8B defense budget; AAA gas hits $4.02/gal
description: Israel's Knesset approved NIS 143B ($45.8B) defense budget — $9.48B increase over 2025, with NIS 30B wartime funding. Total state budget NIS 850.6B ($271B), largest ever. US gas prices cross $4.00 at $4.02/gal per AAA — first time since August 2022. Brent $112.90, up 41% from pre-war.
factCheckNote: Budget: Breaking Defense, ToI. Gas: AAA Newsroom. Oil: CNBC.

title: US bunker-buster strikes devastate Isfahan arsenals; B-52s fly over Iran
description: US forces struck Isfahan with 2,000-lb bunker-buster bombs targeting ammunition arsenals, underground 'missile city,' and weapons depots. Massive fireballs visible. Pentagon announced B-52 bombers now flying 'overland missions' over Iran for first time — signaling air defense destruction. Previously only stealth B-2/B-1 used.
factCheckNote: Euronews, Kurdistan24, GV Wire confirmed strikes and B-52 deployment.

title: Iran drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC at Dubai port; UAE intercepts 48-projectile barrage
description: Iranian drone struck Al-Salmi VLCC (2M barrels crude) at Dubai port anchorage — fire extinguished, no spill, 24 crew safe. UAE intercepted 8 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles, 36 drones in same period. Cumulative UAE interceptions: 433 BMs, 19 CMs, 1,977 drones.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg confirmed tanker attack. LiveUAMap confirmed UAE interceptions.

title: Hezbollah fires 45 rockets at Haifa; IDF kills 8 in Lebanon; NATO intercepts 4th missile over Turkey
description: Hezbollah launched ~45 rockets at Haifa/Galilee (3 lightly injured). IDF struck launchers and killed 8 across Tyre, Sidon, and Bint Jbeil districts (32 wounded). NATO intercepted 4th Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey via SM-3. Iranian missile hit Petah Tikva workshop.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Al Jazeera, UPI confirmed all events.

sources.name: UPI — NATO intercepts missile over Turkey

title: Hegseth: 'Decisive days ahead'; China-Pakistan unveil 5-point ceasefire plan
description: Pentagon briefing: Hegseth said 'upcoming days will be decisive,' did not rule out ground forces, revealed secret Middle East trip. Gen. Caine announced B-52s over Iran. China and Pakistan unveiled 5-point plan: immediate ceasefire, peace talks, end non-military strikes, restore Hormuz, UN framework. Pezeshkian told EU he's ready to stop fighting with guarantees.
factCheckNote: CBS News, Middle East Eye, Euronews confirmed. Stars and Stripes covered Pentagon briefing.

sources.name: Middle East Eye — China-Pakistan peace plan

title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad; UNSC emergency session on UNIFIL deaths
description: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped near Baghdad's Palestine Hotel by Kataib Hezbollah-linked gunmen. One kidnapper arrested; Kittleson not recovered. UNSC convened emergency session on 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon. France's FM Barrot: attacks on UNIFIL 'unacceptable and unjustifiable.'
factCheckNote: Washington Post, Al Jazeera, Euronews confirmed both events.

title: Brent settles $118.35 (May expiry) — best month since 1988; gas crosses $4; stocks rally
description: Brent May contract settled $118.35 (+48.2% pre-war) on expiry — best monthly gain since 1988 inception. June front-month ~$108. WTI $101.38. AAA gas crossed $4.00 to $4.018/gal nationally — first since Aug 2022. Dow rallied +1,125 pts on peace hopes. March worst month for S&P 500 since 2022.
factCheckNote: CNBC, Fortune, ConsumerAffairs, AAA confirmed prices.

title: IDF strikes Tofiq Daru chemical factory — claims Iran weaponizing fentanyl
description: IDF struck Tofiq Daru Company facility in Tehran area, claiming it supplied fentanyl to Iran's SPND for chemical weapons R&D. IDF says fentanyl is 50x stronger than heroin and was being weaponized. Strike 'impaired Iran's chemical weapons production capabilities.' Not independently verified.
factCheckNote: IDF claim about chemical weapons not independently verified. Iran has denied chemical weapons programs.

sources.name: Israel National News — IDF strikes chemical facility

title: Araghchi on Al Jazeera: 'zero trust' with US, denies negotiations underway
description: Iran FM Araghchi confirmed receiving messages from US envoy Witkoff but denied negotiations are underway. Said 'trust level is at zero.' Denied sending response to US 15-point proposal. Seeks 'complete end to war' not ceasefire. On Hormuz: 'Only for ships of those at war with us is this strait closed.'
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera interview broadcast live. Contradicts US claims of progress in talks.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran FM confirms contact, denies talks
Anadolu — Iran seeks complete end to war

title: White House: Trump to address nation on Iran — April 2, 9 PM ET
description: White House press secretary Leavitt announced Trump will deliver prime-time address at 9 PM ET Wednesday (April 2) with 'an important update on Iran.' First major televised address on the conflict. Comes after Trump said US could leave in '2-3 weeks.'
factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple outlets: CNBC, Axios, Bloomberg, The Hill.

sources.name: The Hill — Leavitt: Trump to address the nation

title: IDF dual airstrike kills Hezbollah commander in Beirut — 7 dead, 24 wounded
description: IDF carried out two airstrikes in Beirut: one on Khalde highway, one in Jnah area, targeting senior Hezbollah commander and another senior operative. Lebanese Health Ministry: 7 killed, 24 wounded. Hezbollah confirmed one of its commanders was killed.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, PBS, Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF Beirut strikes

title: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan struck for second time by US-Israeli forces
description: US-Israeli forces bombed Iran's largest steel production facility, the Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan, for the second time. Fars news confirmed. Massive fireballs near Isfahan mountains overnight geolocated by NBC News.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Euronews, Fars news agency confirmed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera Liveblog — Isfahan steel struck

title: IDF wave of strikes on Tehran infrastructure — Bandar Abbas, Bushehr, Malard, Zanjan hit
description: IDF completed another wave of strikes on Iranian government infrastructure. Targets: Shahid Haqqani Port (Bandar Abbas), meteorological facility (Bushehr), residential complex in Malard (W. Tehran), Grand Hosseinieh of Zanjan mosque complex (admin/cultural buildings destroyed).
factCheckNote: JNS, Times of Israel liveblog confirmed IDF statement.

sources.name: JNS — IDF strikes on Tehran infrastructure

title: IRGC fires 3 missile waves at Israel — 16+ wounded, 11-year-old girl critical in Bnei Brak
description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards fired three waves of ballistic missiles at Israel within one hour. Cluster munition warheads struck Bnei Brak area. 16+ wounded: 11-year-old girl critical (Sheba), boy seriously injured, woman and child at Schneider Children's Medical Center. Fourth wave intercepted later.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, CGTN, CNN confirmed.

sources.name: CGTN — Iran fires missiles at Israel

title: Iranian drone strikes Kuwait airport fuel depots — massive fire, no casualties
description: Iranian drone struck KAFCO fuel tanks at Kuwait International Airport, igniting a large fire. Airport remains closed since earlier attacks. No casualties — material damage only. Radar systems and Terminal 1 previously damaged.

AGBI — Iran drone sets airport fuel tanks ablaze

title: Iranian cruise missile hits oil tanker Aqua 1 off Qatar — 21 crew evacuated
description: Three cruise missiles fired from Iran at Qatar. Qatar intercepted 2 of 3. Third hit the Aqua 1, a QatarEnergy fuel oil tanker, 17nm north of Ras Laffan. Hull damage above waterline, no environmental impact. 21-member crew evacuated safely.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Al Arabiya, Peninsula Qatar, BusinessToday confirmed.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Iranian missile hit oil tanker in Qatari waters

sources.name: The National — Kuwait and Bahrain report fires after Iranian attacks

title: Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah joint missile attack on southern Israel
description: Yemen's Houthis launched a 'fresh wave' of ballistic missile attacks targeting 'vital sites' in southern Israel, jointly with Iran and Hezbollah. IDF downed Houthi projectile targeting the south.

sources.name: CGTN — Houthis claim missile wave on Israel

description: Iran FM Araghchi confirmed Iran prepared for 'at least six months' of war and 'completely ready' for ground attack. Confirmed Russian assistance: 'helping us in many different directions.' Demands Lebanon in ceasefire, reparations, sovereignty guarantees.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Three key takeaways from Araghchi interview

title: Trump: no deal needed, war ending in 2-3 weeks — WSJ: willing to leave without reopening Hormuz
description: Trump said Iran doesn't need a deal for him to end war. US will leave 'maybe two weeks, maybe three' when Iran is 'put into the Stone Ages.' WSJ reported Trump willing to end war without reopening Hormuz. Leavitt: private talks 'going well.' Hegseth: 'upcoming days decisive.'

description: Pope Leo XIV urged an Easter end to war: 'I hope Trump is looking for an off-ramp.' Vatican Secretary of State Parolin said the war 'does not seem to meet conditions' for a just war. First American pope increasingly vocal against the conflict.
