sources.name: Manila Times — Pope Leo XIV Easter ceasefire call
Washington Post — Pope urges Easter end to war

title: Trump confirms primetime address 9PM ET; 'absolutely' considering leaving NATO
description: Trump to deliver first major primetime address on Iran war. Told Reuters he is 'absolutely' considering withdrawing from NATO over allies' refusal to join. Told allies to 'get your own oil' and 'start learning how to fight for yourself.' White House official: will reaffirm war ending in 2-3 weeks.
factCheckNote: NPR, NBC News, Washington Post confirmed.

sources.name: NPR — Trump to address nation on Iran war
NBC News — Trump considering pulling out of NATO

title: Iran internal split: Pezeshkian signals ceasefire; Mojtaba Khamenei vows continued resistance
description: Iran President Pezeshkian told European Council President Costa: 'Iran has the necessary will to end this war' if guaranteed no further attacks. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei contradicted: 'Hormuz leverage must continue. Demand is continuation of effective defense.' IRGC made no mention of ending the war. Power struggle between elected president and supreme leader visible.
factCheckNote: CNBC, Al Jazeera, France 24 confirmed both positions.

sources.name: CNBC — Trump says Iran asked for ceasefire
France 24 — Mojtaba Khamenei vows continued support

title: Oil crashes: Brent to ~$102, WTI below $100 as war premium deflates
description: Oil prices dropped sharply on Trump exit signals. Brent fell from ~$108 to ~$102/bbl. WTI dipped below $100 to ~$99.46. Goldman estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical premium. May Brent expired at record $118.35 monthly gain. IEA chief Birol warned 'April will be much worse than March' and is mulling second strategic reserves release.
factCheckNote: CNBC, Fortune, Bloomberg confirmed price movements.

sources.name: CNBC — IEA warns April worse than March

title: Britain hosting 35-nation virtual meeting on Hormuz reopening; Trump says 'no longer concerned'
description: Britain announced it will host a virtual meeting with representatives from 35 nations tomorrow (April 2) to assess diplomatic options to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump shifted stance — said US is 'no longer concerned' with the blockade, telling countries that rely on it to 'keep it open' themselves. 20,000 seafarers stranded in active war zone.
factCheckNote: CNN live updates, Al Jazeera confirmed.

sources.name: CNN — Live updates: Iran war April 1
Al Jazeera — After Hormuz opens, turmoil would last months

title: Former Iran FM Kamal Kharazi seriously injured, wife killed in Tehran airstrike
description: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister and senior adviser to the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, was seriously injured in an Israeli airstrike in Tehran. His wife was killed. Kharazi was reportedly overseeing diplomatic back-channel engagement with Pakistan for a possible Vance-Iran meeting. The strike is seen as targeting Iran's diplomatic apparatus.
factCheckNote: Al Arabiya and multiple regional outlets confirmed.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Former Iran FM seriously injured, wife killed

title: Iran President Pezeshkian publishes open letter to American people
description: Pezeshkian released an open letter to US citizens stating 'the Iranian people harbor no enmity toward other nations.' Questioned whether war serves 'America First,' rejected unconditional surrender, called Iran's actions 'legitimate self-defense.' Bloomberg published full text. Markets interpreted as strongest ceasefire signal yet from Tehran.
factCheckNote: Full text published by Bloomberg and Dawn. Al Jazeera, US News confirmed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Pezeshkian asks US public
Bloomberg — Full text of Pezeshkian letter

title: EU extends Iran sanctions — 262 individuals, 53 entities; DOE issues 10M barrel SPR exchange RFP
description: EU extended Iran sanctions including travel bans and asset freezes on 262 individuals and 53 entities (through April 2027). Separately, US DOE issued emergency RFP for 10M additional barrels from SPR exchange, on top of 172M already committed from IEA's 400M barrel release.
factCheckNote: JURIST confirmed EU sanctions. DOE press release confirmed SPR exchange.

title: Massive bunker-buster strikes near Isfahan — largest secondary explosions of war
description: Heavy bunker-buster bombs dropped on mountainous area near Isfahan causing massive secondary explosions — described as some of the largest since the war began. Isfahan steel plant struck for second time that week. Al-Ahwaz steel plant in Ahvaz also hit.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed

title: IDF eliminates IRGC Quds Force engineering chief Mahdi Vafaei in Mahallat
description: Israeli Air Force killed Mahdi Vafaei, head of engineering branch of Quds Force's Lebanon Corps, responsible for Hezbollah's tunnel and underground infrastructure for 20 years. Led dozens of underground projects in Lebanon and Syria.
factCheckNote: Ynet News, Times of Israel, JNS confirmed

title: Trump delivers prime-time address: war ending in 2-3 weeks, sets April 6 Hormuz deadline
description: Trump claimed 'Operation Epic Fury's core strategic objectives are nearing completion.' Said war could end in 2-3 weeks. Set April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz or face destruction of energy infrastructure including Kharg Island. Claimed Iran asked for ceasefire — Iran denied this as 'false and baseless.' Criticized France as 'VERY UNHELPFUL.'
factCheckNote: Trump's ceasefire claim denied by Iran. PolitiFact rated 'regime change' claim Mostly False.

title: Netanyahu delivers Passover Eve 'Ten Plagues' speech — claims Iran's nuclear capability destroyed
description: Netanyahu claimed Israel has 'inflicted ten plagues on Iran's axis of evil,' listing blows to Hamas, Hezbollah, Assad, West Bank groups, Houthis, plus five blows to Iran directly. Said Iran's trillion-dollar investment 'has gone down the drain.' Vowed campaign 'is not yet over.'
factCheckNote: Nuclear destruction claims disputed by arms control experts and IAEA.

title: Iran fires 4 overnight missile salvos at Israel — cluster warheads wound 4 in Bnei Brak including 2 babies
description: Iran launched 4 ballistic missile salvos since midnight, some with cluster warheads. Bomblet struck Bnei Brak wounding 4 (including 2 babies). Fragments damaged sewage pipes causing flooding. Impact sites also in Tel Aviv and city center. Follows Passover eve barrage that wounded 14+.
factCheckNote: Multiple Israeli media outlets and MDA confirmed casualties.

title: Hezbollah fires 50+ rockets at northern Israel on Passover morning — 6 wounded
description: Hezbollah launched 50+ rockets from Lebanon targeting northern Israel during Passover celebrations. Kiryat Shmona struck (2 lightly wounded), plus injuries in Nahariya (3) and Kibbutz Kabri (1). Rocket hit building in Shfaram. IDF responded killing 40+ Hezbollah operatives.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed barrage size and casualty reports.

title: Oil surges 8% — Brent $109.16, WTI $108.36 on Trump escalation vow
description: Brent crude surged to $109.16/bbl (+8.0%) and WTI to $108.36/bbl (+8.2%) reversing April 1 dip to ~$101. Trump's primetime vow to hit Iran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 weeks dashed peace hopes. Asia-Pacific markets fell: Nikkei -1.4%, Kospi -2.82%.
factCheckNote: CNBC confirmed price data.

title: UK hosts 35-nation Hormuz conference — US absent
description: UK FM Yvette Cooper chairs virtual summit with 35 nations (France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan, etc.) to discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz. US notably absent. Goal: assess diplomatic measures to restore freedom of navigation once fighting stops. Military planners to follow.
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed UK invitation and US absence.

title: IRGC threatens 18 US tech companies — Apple, Google, Meta, Nvidia among targets
description: IRGC listed 18 US tech companies as targets including Apple, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing. Ordered employee evacuations from facilities. Unprecedented targeting of corporate infrastructure. Markets rattled.
factCheckNote: CNBC and Time confirmed IRGC statement.

title: China-Pakistan announce 5-point peace plan — first major power ceasefire initiative
description: After FM Dar-Wang Yi Beijing meeting, joint 5-point plan released: (1) immediate ceasefire + humanitarian access, (2) no force/threats during talks, (3) stop infrastructure attacks, (4) restore Hormuz shipping, (5) comprehensive peace framework under UN Charter. Pakistan serving as key US-Iran mediator.
factCheckNote: Axios confirmed plan details through multiple sources.

title: US-Israeli airstrike destroys Pasteur Institute of Iran — century-old medical research center
description: US-Israeli airstrike severely damaged the Pasteur Institute of Iran in Tehran, a century-old medical research center (founded 1920) that serves as Iran's leading center for vaccine development and infectious disease research. Iran Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security.' 316 healthcare facilities damaged since Feb 28.
factCheckNote: Common Dreams, Al Jazeera, and Middle East Eye all confirmed the strike.

title: Isfahan Mobarakeh Steel Complex fully shut down — recovery 6-12 months
description: Iran's largest steel producer, Mobarakeh Steel Company in Isfahan, has been completely shut down after repeated strikes. All steelmaking furnaces damaged. Khuzestan Steel in Ahvaz also struck. Recovery estimated 6 months to 1 year. Iran calls it 'economic terrorism.'
factCheckNote: Al Arabiya confirmed via company official statement.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Steel plants out of action

title: UK-led 40-nation Hormuz conference concludes — largest multilateral effort yet
description: UK FM Cooper chaired virtual conference with 40 nations (expanded from planned 35). France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan participating. US notably absent. Coalition signed statement demanding Iran stop blocking the strait and pledged follow-up military planning.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Washington Times confirmed 40-nation attendance and US absence.

title: Iran-Oman finalize draft for new Hormuz navigation regime
description: Iran nearly finalized a draft protocol for a new Strait of Hormuz navigation regime in joint talks with Oman. Signals Iran preparing post-war framework for Hormuz transit. Markets partially recovered on the news.
factCheckNote: CNBC and Tasnim News confirmed the draft protocol negotiations.

title: Guterres warns world 'on edge of wider war' — dispatches envoy Arnault
description: UN Secretary-General warned the world is 'on the edge of a wider war.' Directly addressed US and Israel: 'It is high time to stop the war.' Announced dispatch of Personal Envoy Jean Arnault to the region.
factCheckNote: UN News confirmed statement and Arnault dispatch.

sources.name: UN News — Guterres wider war warning

title: US Embassy Baghdad warns of imminent militia attacks within 24-48 hours
description: US Embassy in Baghdad issued urgent security alert warning that Iranian-backed Iraqi militia groups may attack central Baghdad within 24-48 hours. US citizens advised to leave immediately. Airspace closed, commercial flights not operating.
factCheckNote: Official US Embassy security alert confirmed by Al Arabiya and Business Today.

title: Hegseth fires Army Chief Gen. Randy George — replaced by military aide LaNeve
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George effective immediately. Gen. Christopher LaNeve (formerly Hegseth's military aide) named acting Army chief. Major military leadership upheaval during active combat operations in Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq. Bipartisan criticism from Congress.
factCheckNote: Breaking Defense, Washington Post, NPR all confirmed the firing and replacement.

sources.name: Breaking Defense — Hegseth fires Army chief

title: Evening Iranian missile salvo — Petah Tikva industrial zone struck, cluster bomblets hit playground
description: Iran fired ~30 missiles at Israel in late-day salvo. One struck industrial buildings in Petah Tikva; cluster warhead bomblets spread to a nearby playground. 5 wounded. An 11-year-old girl critically hurt by cluster bomb shrapnel. HRW condemned Iran's repeated use of inherently indiscriminate cluster munitions.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel confirmed with images. HRW documented cluster munition use.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Petah Tikva industrial buildings damaged

title: Macron calls military Hormuz reopening 'unrealistic' — markets recover from morning selloff
description: French President Macron said military reopening of Hormuz is 'unrealistic,' calling for ceasefire first. Criticized Trump: 'When you want to be serious, you don't say the opposite every day.' Markets partially recovered: Dow closed -0.13%, S&P +0.11%, Nasdaq +0.18% after intraday drops of 1-2% on Iran-Oman Hormuz navigation draft news.
factCheckNote: Bloomberg and France24 confirmed Macron statements. Market close data from CNBC.

title: GCC backs UN Security Council resolution authorizing force to reopen Hormuz — vote Friday
description: Gulf Cooperation Council nations backed a draft UNSC resolution authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Bahrain (current UNSC president) proposed the resolution. Russia and China expected to veto. Vote scheduled for Friday April 4.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel and The National confirmed resolution details and scheduled vote.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Gulf nations back UN Hormuz resolution
The National — GCC urges UNSC action

title: Dated Brent physical crude hits $141.36 — highest since 2008; WTI surges 12.9% to $113.03
description: Dated Brent physical spot price surged to $141.36/barrel, highest since the 2008 financial crisis. WTI crude settled at $113.03 after a 12.9% single-session surge — biggest daily gain since conflict began. IEA warned April supply crunch will be 'much worse than March.'
factCheckNote: CNBC confirmed Dated Brent physical at $141.36 and WTI settlement at $113.03.

title: IDF 162nd Division advances deeper into southern Lebanon — 40 Hezbollah fighters killed
description: IDF 162nd Division advanced to a new section of the Litani River, killing at least 40 Hezbollah fighters. Lebanese Armed Forces withdrew from more border villages. IDF ordered demolition of homes and bridges south of the Litani. 7 civilians killed in related strikes.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Jerusalem Post confirmed IDF operations and casualty figures.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israeli strikes kill 7 in southern Lebanon

title: Rubio says US can see 'finish line' on Iran war — confirms diplomatic exchanges
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Fox News Hannity: 'We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming.' Confirmed messages are being exchanged between Iran and the US with potential for a 'direct meeting.' Signaled US will reexamine NATO relations post-war.
factCheckNote: Breitbart and GMA News confirmed Rubio quotes from Hannity interview.

title: Trump threatens Iran's bridges and power plants — 'hasn't even started destroying what's left'
description: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'US military hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!' Previously postponed power grid strikes to April 6 amid talks. Represents next phase of infrastructure targeting. April 6 8PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz remains.

sources.name: Al-Monitor — Trump threatens bridges and power plants

title: Israeli strikes shut down Iran's two largest steel plants — restart 6-12 months

title: Iranian drones strike Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery for third time — fires contained
description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery (~466,000 bpd capacity), sparking fires in operational units. No injuries. Third attack on the refinery since the war began. Emergency teams contained the fires.

sources.name: The National — Kuwait races to contain damage

title: US/Israeli strikes destroy Pasteur Institute in Tehran — century-old medical research center
description: US and Israeli forces struck and heavily damaged the Pasteur Institute of Iran in Tehran, a century-old medical research facility founded in 1920 to combat infectious diseases. Part of broader infrastructure targeting campaign.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Al-Monitor confirmed Pasteur Institute strike.

title: Hezbollah fires ~130 rockets at northern Israel on Passover — 4 lightly injured
description: Hezbollah launched approximately 130 rockets at northern Israel on Passover, targeting Kiryat Ata area east of Haifa. Four lightly injured. Over 30 rockets fired in a single barrage.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed rocket barrage.

title: Iranian cluster munition missiles strike Kiryat Ata — 1 wounded
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles carrying cluster bomb warheads at northern Israel. One man lightly wounded in Kiryat Ata. IDF assessed the cluster warhead payload represents escalating Iranian weapons use.
factCheckNote: Haaretz confirmed cluster munitions and casualty.

title: Kuwait desalination plant hit by missile — Kuwait blames Iran, IRGC denies
description: Missile struck a Kuwaiti water desalination plant. Kuwait blamed Iran; IRGC denied responsibility. Escalates attacks on civilian infrastructure in Gulf states.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Kuwait desalination plant hit

title: Iran claims shootdown of US F-15E near Qeshm Island — CENTCOM denies all aircraft safe
description: IRGC claimed shootdown of US fighter jet near Qeshm Island in Strait of Hormuz. US CENTCOM denied, saying all aircraft accounted for. Iranian media initially claimed F-35 but debris shows F-15E wreckage.
factCheckNote: US CENTCOM denies. Debris photos show F-15E, not F-35 as initially claimed.

title: Netanyahu: 'Crushing the terrorist regime in Iran' in full US coordination
description: Netanyahu from military facility: 'Together with our American friends, we continue to crush the terrorist regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders, bombing bridges, bombing infrastructure. This regime is weaker than ever.'
factCheckNote: Times of Israel and Al Jazeera confirmed Netanyahu remarks.

title: Trump: Israel will 'do what I tell them' — asserts full control over war
description: Trump stated Israel will 'do what I tell them' and will stop attacking Iran 'when I stop,' asserting full US control. Reiterated threats to strike bridges and power plants.
factCheckNote: CNBC and Times of Israel confirmed Trump statements.

title: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other generals during wartime
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth ousted Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George, Gen. David Hodne, and Maj. Gen. William Green. Gen. Christopher LaNeve becomes acting Army chief. Hegseth: 'Our adversary thinks there are 15 ways we could come at them with boots on the ground.'
factCheckNote: CBS News, Washington Post, CNN confirmed the firings.

sources.name: CBS News — Hegseth ousts Army Chief of Staff

title: US strikes collapse B1 Bridge (Tehran-Karaj) — tallest bridge in Middle East destroyed
description: Two waves of US precision strikes collapsed the B1 bridge in Alborz Province, the tallest bridge in the Middle East connecting Tehran to Karaj. Purpose: sever drone and missile supply routes. 8 killed, 95 wounded. Trump warned 'Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!'
factCheckNote: NPR, Fox News, The War Zone, Euronews, i24News all confirmed bridge collapse.

title: Drone strike hits Red Crescent warehouse in Bushehr Province — aid supplies destroyed
description: Drone strike hit a Red Crescent aid warehouse in Choghadak, Bushehr province at ~05:30 local time. Two containers, two buses, and emergency vehicles destroyed. Three IFRC personnel killed since war began. Attribution unconfirmed.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Kurdistan24, and Xinhua confirmed the attack.

title: IDF strikes 50 Iranian ballistic missile sites during Passover — 140 bombs dropped
description: Israeli Air Force carried out 70+ strikes on 50 targets of Iran's ballistic missile array in western and central Iran during Passover. ~140 bombs dropped. IDF released video of strikes targeting launchers responsible for recent barrages.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel and JNS confirmed with IDF video.

title: Iranian missiles/drones halt UAE Habshan gas facility — 12 injured from interceptor debris
description: Interceptor debris from Iranian missiles caused fire at Habshan gas facility (Abu Dhabi's main gas complex, ADNOC). Operations halted. UAE engaged 18 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles, 47 drones. 12 injured in Ajban area from falling debris.
factCheckNote: Bloomberg and The National confirmed.

title: Iran strikes Bahrain Batelco/AWS data centers — banking and airline systems disrupted
description: IRGC struck Batelco HQ in Hamala, Bahrain hosting Amazon Web Services data center infrastructure. Power outages affected banking and airline systems. Also struck aluminum industries. First strike on US tech giant infrastructure.
factCheckNote: Free Press Journal and Arabian Business confirmed.

title: IDF kills Iranian ballistic missile commander Makram Atimi in Kermanshah airstrike
description: IDF eliminated Makram Atimi, commander of a central Iranian ballistic missile unit, in precise airstrike in Kermanshah. Atimi was responsible for dozens of missile launches toward Israel. Several battalion commanders also killed.
factCheckNote: Jerusalem Post and Xinhua confirmed.

title: Pentagon confirms US F-15E shot down over Iran — one crew rescued, one missing
description: Pentagon confirmed F-15E Strike Eagle shot down by Iranian air defenses over central Iran. US special forces rescued one crew member on Iranian territory. Second remains missing — Iran offered bounty for capture. Confirmed as 494th FS, RAF Lakenheath by The Aviationist.
factCheckNote: NBC News, CBS News, Axios, CNN, The Aviationist, Washington Post all confirmed. Debris photos show F-15E, not F-35 as Iran initially claimed.

title: IDF strikes Bekaa Valley bridges — 8 Litani crossings now destroyed, Hezbollah isolated
description: IDF struck Sohmor and Mashghara bridges over Litani River in western Bekaa Valley. Total destroyed bridges over Litani reaches 8, effectively isolating western Bekaa from rest of Lebanon, cutting Hezbollah reinforcement routes.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Alma Center confirmed.

title: Iranian missiles crater near Petah Tikva school — bomblets scatter across 3 cities
description: Iran launched 5 separate missile salvos at Israel. ~30 missiles crossed since Wednesday morning. Unintercepted missile cratered near Petah Tikva school. Cluster bomblets hit homes/roads/vehicles in Ramat Gan, Givat Shmuel, Petah Tikva. 3 lightly wounded.
factCheckNote: Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel confirmed.

title: Trump requests record $1.5 trillion defense budget — 44% increase driven by Iran war
description: Trump formally requested $1.5T Pentagon budget ($1.15T base + $350B reconciliation), 44% increase. Includes $50B supplemental for missile/interceptor replacement. Largest defense budget request in US history.
factCheckNote: Washington Post and CNBC confirmed.

title: 100+ legal scholars condemn US strikes on Iran as possible war crimes
description: Over 100 international law scholars (Yale, NYU, ex-HRW chief) signed Just Security open letter declaring US strikes may constitute war crimes. Cited Minab school strike (175+ killed), Hegseth's dismantling of civilian protection structures.
factCheckNote: Published via Just Security, covered by Al Jazeera and Common Dreams.

title: Zarif publishes peace plan in Foreign Affairs — Iran signals diplomatic opening
description: Former FM Zarif proposed: enrichment below 3.67%, regional enrichment facility with China/Russia, reopen Hormuz, nonaggression pact, lift all sanctions. Analysts note Zarif couldn't publish without tacit leadership approval — signals internal debate in Tehran.
factCheckNote: Arab News and Al-Monitor confirmed publication.

title: UK convenes 40+ countries to discuss Hormuz reopening — without US participation
description: UK convened 40+ countries including France, Germany, Italy, Canada, Japan, UAE. Joint statement demanding Iran stop blocking strait. Working-level meetings to follow. UNSC vote on GCC Hormuz resolution delayed to April 5.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, NPR, Foreign Policy confirmed.

title: Second US aircraft lost — A-10 Warthog downed during F-15E SAR operations in Iran
description: An A-10 Thunderbolt II was shot down by Iranian air defenses while providing close air support during search-and-rescue for the missing F-15E WSO. Pilot ejected and was recovered by US SOF. Two Black Hawk helicopters also hit by small arms fire but returned to base. F-15E WSO still missing — Iran offers bounty.
factCheckNote: NPR, Air & Space Forces Magazine, Breaking Defense confirmed.

title: Israeli public support for war drops to 45.5% — Haaretz poll
description: Haaretz poll shows Israeli support for the Iran war dropped from 63% to 45.5%, the lowest since Feb 28. Growing public skepticism about objectives, exit strategy, and mounting costs. Netanyahu faces increasing domestic pressure as Knesset opposition gains strength.
factCheckNote: Haaretz published full poll data with methodology.

title: EU extends Iran sanctions, Kallas-Wang Yi call on war — Meloni breaks with Trump
description: EU extended Iran sanctions to April 2027 (262 individuals, 53 entities), designated 19 new targets. Kallas held call with China's Wang Yi on Iran/global security. Italy's Meloni publicly broke with Trump — refusing offensive operations, traveling to Saudi Arabia for energy deals.
factCheckNote: JURIST, The Spectator confirmed.

title: Iranian missile barrage hits central/southern Israel — 15 impact sites, cluster munitions
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles at central and southern Israel with 15 reported impact sites across Tel Aviv, Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, Rosh HaAyin, and Givatayim. Cluster munitions caused widespread damage. Fire at Neot Hovav industrial zone in Negev. 1 lightly wounded in Bnei Brak.
factCheckNote: Haaretz, Times of Israel, JPost, Middle East Eye all confirmed.

title: Israeli strikes on Beirut Dahiyeh + Sohmor-Mashghara bridge destroyed
description: IDF struck Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut's southern suburbs (2 explosions). Separately destroyed Sohmor-Mashghara bridge in eastern Lebanon — 6th+ Litani crossing cut, further isolating Bekaa Valley.
factCheckNote: BSSN News, WION, Al Jazeera confirmed.

title: Islamic Resistance in Iraq launches 19 attacks on US bases in single day — major escalation
description: Iran-backed Islamic Resistance in Iraq carried out 19 drone and missile attacks on US bases across Iraq in 24 hours, targeting PMF HQ in al-Qaim and 34th Brigade HQ in Mosul. Represents significant escalation on Iraq front.
factCheckNote: ANI News, GlobalSecurity confirmed.

title: US intel: Iran rapidly repairing bunkers, retains half of missile launchers and massive drone fleet
description: US intelligence reports Iran using bulldozers to dig out and repair underground missile bunkers within hours of being struck. Deploying decoys to confuse BDA. CNN: Iran retains roughly half its missile launchers and thousands of one-way attack drones despite 36 days of strikes.
factCheckNote: WION, CNN confirmed via US intelligence sources.

title: Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf signals Bab el-Mandeb threat — potential second blockade
description: Iran's Parliament Speaker posted on X asking 'What share of global oil, LNG, wheat, rice, and fertiliser transits Bab el-Mandeb?' — signaling Iran could expand maritime blockade beyond Hormuz via Houthi allies in Yemen.
factCheckNote: Tribune India, Sunday Guardian confirmed X post.

title: UNSC Hormuz vote delayed to April 5 — faces triple veto from Russia, China, France
description: UN Security Council delayed Hormuz vote to April 5 (Good Friday). Bahrain-drafted resolution scaled back to 'defensive measures' after Russia, China, France objected. Likely triple veto expected.
factCheckNote: France24 confirmed.

title: Pope Leo XIV Good Friday ceasefire appeal — calls Israeli President Herzog
description: Pope Leo XIV called for end to war on Good Friday: God 'does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war.' Phoned Israeli President Herzog urging 'all paths of dialogue.' Guterres told US and Israel it is 'high time to stop the war.'
factCheckNote: Washington Post, US News, UN News confirmed.

title: 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers wounded at El Adeisse, Lebanon
description: Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers wounded in explosion at El Adeisse position in southern Lebanon, two seriously. Follows three Indonesian peacekeepers killed in previous weeks. Mounting toll on UN forces.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed.

title: US-Israeli strikes hit Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone — 6 plants struck
description: US-Israeli strikes hit Iran's second-largest petrochemical complex in Khuzestan Province. Fajr 1 and 2, Rejal, Amir Kabir, Abou Ali, and Bandar-e Imam Khomeini facilities struck. 5 injuries, all personnel evacuated.
factCheckNote: Tasnim News, The National, Arab News confirmed.

title: IDF Navy strikes Ramada Hotel in Beirut — 5 IRGC Quds Force commanders killed
description: IDF Navy struck the Ramada Hotel in Raouche, Beirut, killing 5 senior IRGC Quds Force commanders: Majid Hosseini, Ali Reza Biazar, Ahmad Rasouli, Hossein Ahmadlou, and Abu Mohammad Ali.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel and YNet confirmed.

title: Projectile strikes Bushehr Nuclear Plant — 1 killed, IAEA condemns (4th attack)
description: A projectile hit the perimeter of Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, killing one security guard. IAEA Director Grossi expressed 'deep concern.' No radiation increase. Iran FM Araghchi warned of 'radioactive fallout on GCC capitals.'
factCheckNote: IAEA, Al Jazeera, NucNet confirmed.

title: IDF 70+ strikes across Iran; Hezbollah recaptures Khiam and Qantara
description: IDF carried out 70+ strikes on Iran targeting missile sites, UAV storage, air defenses, weapons R&D. Netanyahu claims 70% of Iran's steel production destroyed. Hezbollah recaptured northern Khiam and re-entered Qantara; 48 IDF wounded, 40 Hezbollah killed.
factCheckNote: Tribune India, JPost confirmed.

title: Trump 48-hour ultimatum: 'all Hell' by April 6; Iran rejects ceasefire
description: Trump warned Iran has 48 hours to reopen Hormuz or face 'all hell.' Iran rejected US ceasefire proposal — response 'given on battlefield.' Graham 'completely convinced' of overwhelming force. UNSC vote delayed again. Indirect Vance-Ghalibaf negotiations stalled.
factCheckNote: Axios, CBS News, Al Jazeera confirmed.

title: Iran opens Hormuz for essential goods; Oracle Dubai HQ hit; EU windfall tax push
description: Iran approved limited passage for essential/humanitarian goods through Hormuz (oil blockade remains). Oracle HQ in Dubai hit by drone/debris. 5 EU nations demand windfall profit tax. Russia condemns Bushehr strike. Israeli Supreme Court orders police to allow anti-war protests.
factCheckNote: Xinhua, Euronews, TASS, CNBC confirmed.

title: Day 36 evening: F-15E search ongoing; Lebanon toll 1,422; Tel Aviv protests restricted; OPEC+ tomorrow
description: Search continues for missing US airman in Iran. Lebanon death toll updated to 1,422 killed. IDF restricted Tel Aviv anti-war protest to 150 people before Supreme Court ordered access. US casualties updated to 15 killed, 365 wounded. Iraq toll at 109. OPEC+ ministerial meeting set for April 5. Trump 48-hour ultimatum expires April 6.
factCheckNote: NBC, Haaretz, Al Jazeera, CNN, Axios confirmed.

title: Day 37: Bushehr struck (4th), Mahshahr petrochem 5 killed, cluster munitions near Kirya HQ, Kuwait oil complex droned
description: Bushehr Nuclear Plant struck for 4th time — 1 IAEA guard killed, Russia evacuates 198 more Rosatom staff. Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone hit (5 killed, 170 wounded). Iranian cluster munitions struck near IDF Kirya HQ in Tel Aviv — no injuries. Israeli strikes on Beirut Dahiyeh and Tyre (2 girls killed, 40 wounded, hospital damaged). Eastern Lebanon Sohmor-Mashghara bridge destroyed. Kuwait Shuwaikh oil complex hit by drone — fire contained, no injuries. Iran rejects Trump 48-hour ultimatum as 'helpless, nervous, stupid.' Ceasefire talks at standstill. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.10.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, UN News, JPost, GMA News, Middle East Eye confirmed.

title: Day 37 PM: F-15E WSO RESCUED via commando raid in Iran; IDF 272 strikes/184 killed; anti-war protests dispersed; April 6 deadline TOMORROW
description: CRITICAL: Missing F-15E WSO rescued via commando raid deep inside Iran near Isfahan — evaded capture 24h, CIA ran deception campaign. US destroyed 2 stranded C-130s at makeshift airbase. Iran claims it destroyed C-130 and 2 Black Hawks; US confirms 1 Black Hawk struck but exited airspace. 5 Iranians killed in rescue firefight. IDF conducted 272 strikes across 14 Iranian provinces causing 184 casualties — Beheshti University medical research institute nearly destroyed. Tel Aviv anti-war protests violently dispersed at Habima Square, 17 arrested, missile alert during dispersal. Kuwait power/water plants + KPC HQ hit by drones. Trump's 48h Hormuz ultimatum expires TOMORROW April 6. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.10.
factCheckNote: CNN, Axios, NBC News, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency confirmed.

title: Day 37 EVE: Neot Hovav chemical plant hit again; Bahrain BAPCO/UAE engaged; Pope Leo Easter plea; Rubio arrests Soleimani family; OPEC+ meets
description: Iranian ballistic missile struck Neot Hovav chemical industrial zone (repeat strike) — hazmat leak, 1 lightly wounded, 3 hazmat teams deployed. Iranian drone hit Bahrain BAPCO storage tank — fire contained. UAE engaged 9 ballistic missiles, 1 cruise missile, 50 drones from Iran — targeting aluminum industries. Pope Leo XIV delivered Easter Urbi et Orbi: 'Let those who have weapons lay them down!' — announced April 11 peace vigil. Rubio revoked green cards and arrested Soleimani family members in US. Netanyahu video: 'After we destroyed 70% of steel production, today we attacked petrochemical factories.' OPEC+ meeting today deciding May production. Trump deadline expires TOMORROW April 6 8PM ET. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.10.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Gulf News, Xinhua, Khaleej Times, Al-Monitor, NCR, RedState confirmed.

title: Day 37 PM: Haifa building struck (3 missing); Lebanon 14 killed; Houthis target Ben Gurion; Oman-Iran Hormuz talks; Trump 'Power Plant Day' Easter rant
description: HAIFA: Iranian ballistic missile direct hit on residential building — 4 injured (1 critical, 82yo), 3-4 missing under rubble including child. Warhead may not have detonated. LEBANON: Israeli strikes kill 14 across Beirut/south, wound 39 (strike 100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital). HOUTHIS: Claimed joint IRGC-Hezbollah attack on Ben Gurion Airport — IDF says intercepted. HORMUZ: Oman-Iran deputy FM talks exchanging transit proposals. 3 Omani ships outside approved corridor. TRUMP: Easter profanity-laden rant — 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day and Bridge Day.' Set April 7 8PM ET deadline. Revealed US armed Iranian protesters via Kurds. IRAN: Gen. Aliabadi rejects ultimatum as 'helpless, nervous, stupid' — warns 'gates of hell.' IRGC claims C-130 + 2 Black Hawk kills (unconfirmed). Markets closed Easter. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.10.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, NPR, Axios, The Hill, Republic World, Middle East Eye confirmed.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Haifa missile strike

title: Day 37 EVE: F-15E rescue confirmed; Pope Leo Easter peace plea; Rosatom evacuates 198 from Bushehr; Rubio revokes green cards; gas $4.11; April 7 deadline TOMORROW
description: F-15E RESCUE: Second crew member (USAF colonel/WSO) confirmed rescued from mountain crevice after 48h evasion — Delta Force/SEAL Team Six op. Trump: 'WE GOT HIM!' POPE LEO: Easter message: 'Let those with weapons lay them down!' — announced April 11 peace vigil. BUSHEHR: Rosatom evacuated 198 more staff — only ~50 volunteers remain. IAEA: 1 plant worker killed by projectile. RUBIO: Revoked green cards of Iranian general's relatives, ordered arrests. GAS: AAA national average rises to $4.11/gal (+29.2%). HAIFA: Overnight rescue continues — 3-4 still missing under rubble. HORMUZ: Blockade Day 34 — April 7 8PM ET deadline approaches. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Markets closed Easter.
factCheckNote: NBC News, Axios, Anadolu Agency, Al-Monitor, RedState, AAA confirmed.

sources.name: Axios — Iran F-15 crew member rescued
NBC News — Pope Leo Easter message

title: Day 38 AM: Haifa rescue — 2 dead, 2 missing; cluster bombs hit Petah Tikva & Haifa; 11 killed in Lebanon; Hezbollah warship claim; Masnaa crossing closed
description: HAIFA: Overnight rescue confirms 2 dead, 2 still missing under rubble from Iranian missile strike on residential building. 24 wounded. CLUSTER BOMBS: Iran fires cluster bomb missiles at Petah Tikva (woman seriously hurt) and Haifa (4 lightly injured incl. 2 girls). LEBANON: Israeli strikes kill 7 in Kfar Hatta (incl. 4yo girl, soldier), 4 killed near Beirut hospital in Jnah (39 wounded). MASNAA: Israel forces closure of Lebanon-Syria border crossing. NAVAL: Hezbollah claims cruise missile hit on Israeli warship 68nm off coast — IDF denies. IRGC: Declares 'new Persian Gulf order' — Hormuz will 'never return.' TRUMP: Extended Hormuz deadline to Tuesday 8PM ET (3rd extension). Iran FM Baghaei rejects as 'incitement to war crimes.' OPEC+: 206k bpd output increase for May. Brent $110.82/WTI $112.00. Gas $4.10. April 7 deadline TOMORROW.
factCheckNote: JPost, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, MTV Lebanon, Arab News, NBC News, Enerdata confirmed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Tehran rejects Trump's Tuesday deadline
Jerusalem Post — Haifa rescue finds two dead

title: Day 38 PM: IRGC Intel Chief Khademi killed; South Pars petrochemical destroyed; Sharif University struck; cluster bombs kill 2 in Ramat Gan; Haifa 4 dead; 45-day ceasefire proposal; Qatar LNG attempts Hormuz
description: ASSASSINATION: Israel kills IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi + Quds Force Unit 840 commander Asghar Bagheri in dawn Tehran strike — IRGC's No. 2 eliminated. SOUTH PARS: Israeli strikes destroy Asaluyeh petrochemical complex — 5 killed, 170 injured, 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports now offline. SHARIF: US-Israeli strike hits Sharif University of Technology Electronics Research Center — gas infrastructure damaged. CLUSTER BOMBS: Iranian cluster munitions hit Ramat Gan/Bnei Brak/Givatayim — 2 killed (elderly couple), 28+ bomblet impact sites, heavy building damage. HAIFA: All 4 missing confirmed dead after 18-hour rescue (elderly couple 80s, man 40s, woman 35). CEASEFIRE: Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey submit 45-day ceasefire proposal (Islamabad Accord) — Iran won't reopen Hormuz under temporary deal. HORMUZ: Qatar LNG tankers attempt first exit since war; Iraqi tanker transits under new exemption. IRGC claims attack on USS Tripoli (unverified). Brent $110.82/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.104. April 7 deadline TOMORROW.
factCheckNote: AP/KSAT, Middle East Eye, PressTV, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Israel Hayom, Axios, CBC, CNBC, Bloomberg confirmed.

sources.name: AP/KSAT — Airstrikes on Iran kill more than 25
Axios — US, Iran mediators discuss 45-day ceasefire
Haaretz — Iran Fires Cluster Munitions at Tel Aviv Area

title: Day 38 Evening: Trump threatens to take out all of Iran tomorrow night; Araghchi refuses talks; Pope Leo calls for peace; EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials
description: TRUMP PRESSER: White House press conference — "The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night." Extended Hormuz deadline to Tue Apr 7 8pm ET. Threatened "Power Plant Day and Bridge Day." Called Iran 10-point counterproposal "significant" but "not good enough." ARAGHCHI: CBS Face the Nation — "We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans... We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes." Phone call with Lavrov Apr 5. IRAN RESPONSE: Presidential spokesperson calls Trump threats "sheer desperation and anger." Hormuz opens only when "all damage compensated through new legal regime." POPE LEO XIV EASTER: "Let those who have weapons lay them down!" Hoped Trump finds "off-ramp." EU: Kallas announces sanctions on 19 Iranian officials; Aspides naval mission scale-up. OSINT: Planet Labs indefinite satellite imagery blackout on Iran at US request (retroactive to Mar 9). OIL: Brent 09.90 (-0.8%); WTI 11.81 (+0.2%). Gas .10. OPEC+ 206k bpd May hike confirmed (symbolic). Deadline TOMORROW 8pm ET.
factCheckNote: NPR, CNBC, Fox News, CBS News, NBC News, PBS confirmed.

sources.name: NPR — Trump press conference on Iran
NBC News — Pope Leo Easter message

title: Day 38 Night: Zelensky reveals Russia-Iran intel sharing; Velayati threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure; Vance overnight ceasefire talks; oil surges to $111.25
description: RUSSIA-IRAN INTEL: Zelensky reveals Russia provided Iran satellite intelligence on 50-53 Israeli energy grid targets — Orot Rabin power station primary target. Russia told Iran Israel is 'energy island.' BAB AL-MANDEB: Senior Iranian adviser Velayati threatens 'Resistance front views Bab al-Mandeb as it does Hormuz' — if both straits closed, 25% of global energy blocked. JPMorgan: oil could hit $120-$150. CEASEFIRE: VP Vance in overnight talks with Pakistan FM Munir + Iranian FM Araghchi on Islamabad Accord two-phase deal. Reports suggest deal 'could be today.' OIL: Brent surges to $111.25 (+39.3%); WTI $110.13 (+45.8%). Gas $4.119. Alma Research Center: 455 Iranian attack waves since war began; daily avg 7.7/day. US casualties: 13 KIA, 365 WIA. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed. HRANA: 3,540 killed. April 7 DEADLINE 8PM ET.
factCheckNote: Kyiv Post, JPost, Al Jazeera, Breitbart, CNBC, Fortune confirmed.

sources.name: Kyiv Post — Russia provided Iran intelligence on Israeli targets
Al Jazeera — Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure
CNBC — US, Iran receive peace proposal

title: Day 39 Morning: DEADLINE DAY — Hegseth vows most intense strikes; Iran rejects ceasefire; Mojtaba Khamenei missing 30 days; oil volatile
description: DEADLINE DAY: Trump's 8pm ET (midnight GMT) Hormuz ultimatum expires TONIGHT. Threatens 'complete demolition' of power plants and bridges. HEGSETH: Tuesday will see 'most intense day of strikes on Iran since start of war.' Monday already highest volume. 15,000+ enemy targets hit total. IRAN REJECTS CEASEFIRE: 'We won't merely accept a ceasefire. We only accept an end of the war with guarantees.' 45-day proposal from Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey rejected. All diplomatic channels now exhausted — Pakistan track collapsed Apr 3. MOJTABA KHAMENEI: Missing 30 days since Mar 8 election. No photo/video/audio. IRGC believed making decisions. Pezeshkian can't get meeting. CIA searching. SAUDI DEFENSE: Riyadh intercepted 7 Iranian ballistic missiles + 10 drones (Apr 5). LEBANON: 1,461 killed, 4,430 wounded. Hezbollah cmdr killed by naval strike. CASUALTIES: HRANA 3,540+ killed (1,616 civilians, 244 children). Iran Health Min 2,076+. US: 13 KIA, 365 WIA. OIL: Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Gas ~$4.12. JPMorgan: $120-$150 if Bab al-Mandeb closes.
factCheckNote: PBS, Al Jazeera, NPR, Bloomberg, NBC News, Axios confirmed.

title: Day 39 Midday: IDF completes wide-scale strike wave; 33+ killed in Tehran residential areas; Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed; IRGC intel chief Khademi killed; Kharg Island hit; Istanbul consulate attack; Iran calls human chains
description: IDF STRIKE WAVE: Completed 'wide-scale wave targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' — weapons storage, missile production, air defense systems. Explosions in Tehran/Karaj, power outages. RESIDENTIAL STRIKES: 33+ killed across greater Tehran — Baharestan (13 dead incl 6 children under 10), Alborz (18 dead incl 2 infants, 24 injured), Shahriar (9 dead), Pardis (6 bodies recovered). SYNAGOGUE: Rafi-Nia Synagogue in central Tehran 'completely destroyed' — one of most important Khorasan Jewish gathering places. KHADEMI KILLED: IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi (IRGC No. 2) + Quds Force cmdr Asghar Bagheri killed in Israeli precision strike. KHARG ISLAND: New explosions reported hours before Trump deadline. Iranian state media confirms attacks. ISTANBUL: 3 gunmen attacked Israeli consulate — 1 killed, 2 captured; 2 police wounded. Consulate was empty. HUMAN CHAINS: Iran calls civilians to form human chains around power plants. IRGC: 'will deprive Americans of regional oil/gas for years.' IRAN MISSILES: New barrage at southern Israel intercepted. King Fahd Causeway (Saudi-Bahrain) temporarily closed. Houthis claim attacks on 3 ships + 2 US frigates. Brent $110/WTI $113. Gas $4.12. DEADLINE: 8pm ET tonight.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Fox News, Military.com, Middle East Eye, Gulf News, AP confirmed.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF completes wave of strikes
Al Jazeera — Synagogue destroyed, residential strikes
Fox News — IRGC intel chief killed

title: Day 39 Afternoon: US confirms 50+ strikes on Kharg Island; IDF targets 10 bridges/railways; Shiraz petrochemical hit; Makram Atimi killed; Trump: 'A whole civilization will die tonight'; Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues' Passover address; Pezeshkian: 14M ready to die; Hegseth impeachment push; Damascus intercepts; Gas $4.14
description: KHARG ISLAND: US confirmed 50+ military targets struck on Kharg Island — military bunkers, storage, air defense. Oil infra NOT targeted per officials. IDF BRIDGES: Wide-scale operation striking 10 bridges/railways — Kashan (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan bridge, Qom, Karaj, Qazvin. IDF warned Iranians to avoid trains until 21:00. SHIRAZ: IDF struck 'one of last remaining' nitric acid petrochemical compounds used for explosives/ballistic missiles. ATIMI KILLED: IDF eliminated Makram Atimi, central ballistic missile unit commander, in Kermanshah strike. Several battalion commanders also killed. Mobile launcher truck destroyed in Tabriz. TRUMP: At White House presser: 'A whole civilization will die tonight' — threatened every bridge decimated, every power plant destroyed. 'Not at all' concerned about war crimes. NETANYAHU: Passover address compared strikes to 'Ten Plagues.' Claimed 70% of Iran's steel production destroyed. PEZESHKIAN: 14M Iranians ready to sacrifice lives. HEGSETH: Impeachment articles from House Democrats; fired Army CoS Randy George. DAMASCUS: Explosions from Israeli intercepts of Iranian missiles over Syria. PETAH TIKVAH: 34-year-old woman seriously injured by interceptor debris. GUTERRES: Warned attacking civilian infrastructure unlawful. OPEC+: +206K bpd May. Gas $4.14. Brent ~$111/WTI ~$113-115. DEADLINE: 8PM ET TONIGHT.
factCheckNote: NBC News, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, CNN, i24NEWS, Fox News, Axios confirmed.

sources.name: NBC News — US strikes Kharg Island

title: Day 39 Evening: Russia/China veto Hormuz resolution; Iran severs US diplomacy; IRGC hits Saudi Jubail; King Fahd Causeway closed; Mojtaba Khamenei intel leak; Iran 10-point plan rejected; Human chains at power plants; Kittleson freed; Qatar condemns Iran
description: UNSC VETO: Russia and China vetoed Bahrain-sponsored resolution to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Vote 11-2. Watered-down version already removed offensive action language. IRAN CUTS TIES: Tehran officially suspended all diplomatic/indirect channels with US after Trump 'civilization will die' threat. Pakistan back-channel reportedly active. JUBAIL ATTACK: IRGC launched 11 ballistic missiles and 18 drones at Saudi Eastern Province. All intercepted but debris struck SABIC petrochemical complex at Jubail Industrial City, igniting fire. Partial evacuations. CAUSEWAY: King Fahd Causeway (Saudi-Bahrain) temporarily closed over Iranian air raid threats. US shelter-in-place for Americans in Bahrain. KHAMENEI LEAK: US/Israeli intel memo leaked — Mojtaba Khamenei unconscious in Qom, unable to govern. Wounded Feb 28 same strike that killed his father. Only 2 statements read on state TV + 1 AI-generated video since war began. PEACE PLAN: Iran presented 10-point proposal — permanent end to war, security guarantees, Hormuz toll (~$2M/ship), sanctions lifting. Trump: 'significant' but 'not good enough.' IDF STRIKES: Khorramshahr shipbuilding (80% of IRGC Navy vessels), Natanz IRGC/police HQ. HUMAN CHAINS: Iranians forming shields around power plants ahead of Trump deadline. KITTLESON: American journalist freed by Kataib Hezbollah in Baghdad. QATAR: FM Al Thani condemned Iran's 'reckless' Gulf attacks to Araghchi. UAE: Intercepted 1 BM + 11 drones. DEADLINE: 8PM ET approaches. Brent $113.40. Gas $4.14.

title: Day 39 Late Evening: Trump agrees to 2-week ceasefire; Iran fires 6 BM salvos with cluster munitions at Israel; IDF destroys Shiraz sonar + petrochemical facilities; Ali Al Salem drone strike wounds 15 Americans; Pope Leo condemns Trump threat
description: CEASEFIRE: After 8PM ET deadline passed, Trump posted on Truth Social agreeing to 'suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks,' conditional on Iran reopening Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan PM Sharif mediated. Bloomberg: 'two-week ceasefire to finalize talks.' CLUSTER BARRAGE: Iran fired 6 separate BM salvos at central/southern Israel on last day of Passover. ~10 missiles per salvo, most with cluster munitions. ~50 submunition impact sites across Petah Tikva, Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak. 6 wounded including 1 seriously. SHIRAZ STRIKES: Joint IAF/Navy operation struck Iran's central sonar production facility + last remaining petrochemical plant producing nitric acid for BM explosives. IDF claims 85%+ of Iran's petrochemical export capacity now inoperable. Also struck large-scale BM system site in NW Iran. ALI AL SALEM: Iranian drone strike hit Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, wounding 15 Americans (IRGC Wave 98 of True Promise 4). Most returned to duty. POPE LEO: Called Trump's civilization threat 'truly unacceptable.' VANCE: Iran faces 'two pathways: normalcy or more pain.' War will end 'very shortly.' HEGSETH: Claims Tehran 'embarrassed and humiliated' — but officials say claims 'contradict reality.' Brent $113.40. Gas $4.14. Hormuz blockade Day 36.
factCheckNote: US News, CNBC, Bloomberg, Times of Israel, FDD Long War Journal, ANI News, APA, Vatican News, Washington Times confirmed.

China gave Khamenei regime 'last-minute nudge.' OIL CRASH: Brent plunged ~16% to ~$93, WTI dropped ~19% to ~$92 in after-hours — biggest drop since 1991 Gulf War. By morning, prices rebounded to Brent ~$108-110, WTI ~$112-115 on ceasefire skepticism. S&P futures +2.4%, Dow futures +1,000 pts. PEACE TALKS: Pakistan PM Sharif confirmed Islamabad talks for Friday April 10. VP Vance to lead US delegation. KHAMENEI HEALTH: US-Israeli intel reports Mojtaba Khamenei unconscious and incapacitated in Qom, 'unable to be involved in any decision making.' IRGC effectively managing country. Gas $4.119 (AAA). Hormuz blockade Day 36 — conditional reopening under ceasefire. Conflict Day 40.

title: Day 40 Update: Iran violates ceasefire with missile barrage minutes after announcement, then compliance begins; Netanyahu excludes Lebanon; oil stabilizes ~$96; Pope condemns Trump threat; journalist freed
description: CEASEFIRE VIOLATION: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel and Gulf states ~10 minutes after Trump's announcement. Sirens across Israel; 4 injured Tel Sheva; Jubail (Saudi) struck; Habshan gas fire (UAE); Bahrain sirens. Despite violation, ceasefire holding by morning — US suspended all strikes, Khamenei ordered military to cease fire ('not the end of the war'). LEBANON EXCLUDED: Netanyahu formally stated ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon' — contradicts Pakistan PM Sharif. Israel set 3 conditions for Lebanon talks. IDF operations continue. OIL STABILIZES: After 16% crash, Brent settled ~$95.80 (+20.0%); WTI ~$97.28 (+28.8%). Physical dated Brent hit record $144.42. HEZBOLLAH: 24 attack waves, 40+ rockets at northern Israel; 6 injured Nahariya. POPE LEO: Condemned Trump's 'whole civilization will die' threat as 'truly unacceptable.' JOURNALIST FREED: Shelly Kittleson released from Kataib Hezbollah in Iraq via prisoner swap. WORLD REACTS: Guterres, Egypt, Oman welcome ceasefire; UAE rejects as insufficient. Kallas: Iran 'willing to talk.' Gas $4.14 (AAA). Hormuz blockade Day 37 — conditional reopening. Conflict Day 40.
factCheckNote: Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera, Axios, PBS, Washington Post, The Hill confirmed ceasefire violation, Netanyahu Lebanon exclusion, and oil stabilization.

Iran requires armed forces coordination. LEBANON: Israel renewed south Lebanon strikes. Netanyahu: ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon.' Lebanese army warns against returns. Hezbollah quiet since 01:00 Apr 8. Fars: tanker traffic 'suspended.' IRGC warns 'heavy response.' White House denied closure. IRAN MISSILES: Iran fired barrages at Israel despite ceasefire — 3 children injured Negev. GULF: UAE intercepted 17 ballistic missiles + 35 drones; Habshan gas complex suspended. PROXIES: Iraqi Islamic Resistance suspended operations 2 weeks. title: Ceasefire Day 2: Fragile hold; Iran cries 3 violations; Vance talks Saturday; Hormuz standoff
description: CEASEFIRE: US-Iran ceasefire holding into Day 2 — no new strikes on Iranian soil. But Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf accuses US of 3 violations: (1) Lebanon not included, (2) US drone entered Iranian airspace, (3) denial of enrichment rights. FM Araghchi warns ceasefire may collapse if Lebanon attacks continue. TALKS: VP Vance confirmed to lead US delegation at Islamabad peace talks Saturday April 12 (moved from Friday). Delegation includes Witkoff and Kushner. Highest-level US-Iran meeting since 1979. HORMUZ: Still effectively closed despite ceasefire. ~2,000 ships stranded. Only 3 ships exited April 8, all bulk carriers — zero oil tankers. Iran demanding crypto tolls. White House insists 'open without tolls.' MARKETS: Brent settled $94.22 (+18.1%), WTI $94.25 (+24.8%). Gas $4.164. LEBANON: 254 dead from Operation Eternal Darkness confirmed by multiple sources. UN Human Rights Chief: 'nothing short of horrific.'

All public institutions closed, flags at half-staff. IRGC: Shot down Israeli-made Hermes 900 drone over Lar, Fars Province. Called it 'flagrant ceasefire violation.' Warned any aircraft entering Iranian airspace 'will be met with decisive response.' Also threatened 'regret-inducing response' if Lebanon attacks continue. BAGHDAD: Iraqi militia drone attacks hit near Baghdad airport and Diplomatic Support Center despite ceasefire. US Embassy issued Level 4: Do Not Travel. description: HORMUZ: IRGC publishes map showing 'danger zone' over standard shipping routes — mines identified as Maham-3 and Maham-7 types. Directs ships to alternative routes north/south of Larak Island, requiring coordination with IRGC. Press TV: Strait 'fully closed' — tanker AUROURA made 180° turn. 800+ vessels still trapped. Only 2-3 ships transited since ceasefire. White House: closure 'completely unacceptable.' HEZBOLLAH: Rockets escalate — sirens 7+ times in northern Israel. 4 injured (3 Nahariya, 1 Kabri). Dozens of launches since midnight. Statement: 'Response continues until aggression ceases.' LEBANON: IDF strikes Abbassiyeh (7 killed), plus Kafra, Jmaijmeh, Safad al-Battikh. Division 162 deploying to expand buffer zone in south Lebanon. HOUTHIS: Anti-ship missile targets Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted by USS Mason/Laboon escort. TEHRAN: Massive crowds mark 40th day since Khamenei assassination. Some normalcy returns with first ceasefire day. POLITICS: House Democrats attempt War Powers Resolution at 11:30am pro forma session — GOP expected to block. OIL: Brent $97.80 (+3.1%), WTI $97.53 (+3.1%). AAA gas $4.17/gal.

description: LEBANON: IDF confirmed killing Ali Yusuf Harshi — Hezbollah chief Qassem's personal secretary and nephew — in Tallet Khayat, Beirut airstrike (April 8 evening). Initial Reuters flash claiming Qassem killed was retracted. Also killed LRB commander Hamdan in Sidon. Lebanon Health Ministry updated April 8 toll to 303 killed, 1,150 wounded; total war toll now 1,888 killed, 6,092 wounded. HEZBOLLAH: ~70 rockets fired at northern Israel throughout Day 41 — Kiryat Shmona, Galilee, Taibe, Manara hit. One rocket struck home in Kiryat Shmona (no injuries). DIPLOMACY: Netanyahu orders direct Lebanon negotiations 'ASAP' after Trump/Witkoff pressure — first meeting at State Dept next week. HORMUZ: 3 Chinese VLCCs test transit (Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu) carrying Iraqi/Saudi crude. China FM confirms. Iraq says Iranian exemption allows transits. Iran still demands coordination. Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge, pledges 'new phase' for Hormuz. POLITICS: Trump slams NATO at Rutte meeting, revives Greenland threat. Kallas arrives Riyadh for diplomatic push. Iran's Qalibaf cites 3 US ceasefire violations. OIL: Brent $98.26 (+4%), WTI $100.27 (+6%). Goldman flags $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Axios, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, CNBC, CNN, NPR all confirming. Oil prices via CNBC/Bloomberg. Lebanon toll via Health Ministry/CNN.

Key sticking points: enrichment, Lebanon, Hormuz, sanctions. Trump tells NBC he's 'very optimistic.' MILITARY: IRGC shoots down Israeli Hermes 900 drone over Fars Province — warns any airspace entry is ceasefire violation. IDF 98th Division near-full control of Bint Jbeil; IDF Chief Zamir visits troops, says 'Iran is much weaker.' Hezbollah fires missile at Ashdod (deepest attempt) — intercepted. Houthis target Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted by USS Mason. Israel strikes continue in Lebanon: al-Abbassieh (7 killed), other locations (17+ killed early April 9). description: IRAN: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declares 'decisive victory' on state TV, announces 'new phase' in Hormuz management. Says 'Iran will not forfeit its rights.' Chinese state media amplifies message. LEBANON: National mourning day declared after 303 killed April 8 — deadliest day of Lebanon front. HRW condemns Israeli strikes, reports vital Qasmiyeh Bridge near Tyre destroyed, severing southern Lebanon from humanitarian aid. IDF continues hitting residential areas in Hanawya and Aita al-Shaab (7+ killed). Pezeshkian: Israeli strikes 'violate temporary truce, imperil peace talks.' UN: Russia and China VETO Security Council resolution on Hormuz freedom of navigation — UK expresses 'deep regret.' Veto protects Iran's leverage during ceasefire talks. TRUMP: Says Iran 'doing a very poor job' of letting oil through Hormuz. Zero oil tankers have transited since ceasefire. KALLAS: Warns of 'slippery slope' if Iran controls Strait of Hormuz (from Riyadh). FACT-CHECK: PolitiFact rates Trump's 'total victory' claim as false — Hormuz still closed, Lebanon escalating, enrichment unresolved. Hegseth's 'decisive victory' claim contradicted by officials (WaPo). OIL: Brent ~$98, WTI ~$97. Ceasefire doubt premium persists.

Total Lebanon toll reaches 1,953 killed, 6,303 wounded. Hezbollah fires missile at Ashdod naval base (~90mi from border) — deepest strike yet, intercepted. ~30 rockets hit northern Israel (Misgav Am house, Deir al-Asad school, Metula packaging facility). Hezbollah missile toward central Israel/Tel Aviv — intercepted. IDF ground clashes in Bint Jbeil, 2 soldiers wounded by anti-tank missile. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir visits Lebanon, approves continuation of operations. IRAN: Former FM Kharazi (81) dies from April 1 strike wounds. ECONOMY: US CPI surges to 3.3% — gasoline +21.2% in one month. Brent $96.69 (-$1.58 from AM), WTI $99.03 (+$2.40). Gas $4.17. POLITICS: House GOP blocks war powers vote. Saudi FM holds first call with Iran FM Araghchi since war began. Israel excludes France from Lebanon talks (Tuesday at State Dept). HORMUZ: 7 vessels transit (vs 140/day pre-war). Blockade day 39. HORMUZ: Only 6 vessels transited Apr 10 (down from 7). 600+ vessels stranded incl. 325 tankers. Russian-flagged tanker made it through. UAE oil CEO: 'Hormuz is not open.' OIL: Brent $96.76 (+21.2%); WTI $99.03 (+31.2%). Saudi capacity down 600k bpd from attacks + 700k bpd East-West Pipeline. JPMorgan warns $150 if Hormuz shut into mid-May. GAS: $4.166 (flat). Iran's 71-member team led by Ghalibaf and Araghchi. Talks shifted from proximity to direct format at Serena Hotel. Iran red lines: Hormuz sovereignty, Lebanon ceasefire, frozen assets, enrichment. Iran calls it ceasefire violation, threatens attack 'within 30 minutes.' LEBANON: IDF kills 10+ in southern Lebanon including 3 emergency workers. Lebanon toll now 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded. Hezbollah fires missiles at Ashdod naval base (intercepted). Netanyahu formally rejects Lebanon ceasefire ahead of Washington talks Tuesday. Corruption trial resumes Sunday. GUTERRES: Welcomed talks, warned Lebanon fighting endangers truce. HORMUZ: Iran cannot locate many of its 2,000-6,000 deployed mines — have drifted from original positions. Mine clearance estimated 6-10 weeks. Only 9 ships transited Thursday. LEBANON: 18+ killed in Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon. Hezbollah fired 30+ rockets at northern Israel (Safed, Nahariya, Shlomi). IDF 98th Division near-complete control of Bint Jbeil. CHINA: US intel — Beijing preparing MANPAD delivery to Iran via third countries. OIL: Brent ~$96.66, WTI ~$95.50 (weekend — no trading). Gas $4.166/gal.
factCheckNote: CNBC, WaPo, NPR, Al Jazeera, AP, USNI News, JPost, Times of Israel, CNN, ANI all confirming.

title: Day 44 AM: ISLAMABAD TALKS COLLAPSE after 21 hours — Vance departs with 'final and best offer'; Iran: 'ball in America's court'; IRGC 'last warning' to US warships in Hormuz; Israel approves energy strike target list; Hezbollah 105 attack waves (record day); Netanyahu trial resumes — court rejects delay twice
description: ISLAMABAD COLLAPSE: VP Vance announced NO DEAL after 21 hours of marathon negotiations across 2 days. Left a 'final and best offer' for Iran. Key sticking points: Iran's refusal to commit long-term to no nuclear weapons, Hormuz control, Lebanon operations. Vance: 'That's bad news for Iran much more than for the US.' Departed aboard Air Force Two at 07:08 local. IRAN RESPONSE: Foreign Ministry says 'ball in America's court,' claims agreement on 'several points' but 'two important issues' remain. Ghalibaf calls talks 'unreasonable.' Tasnim: Iran in no rush to return. TRUMP: White House gaggle — 'Whether we make a deal or not makes no difference to me. Regardless what happens, we win.' HORMUZ: IRGC issues 'last warning,' 'strongly denies' US warship transit, vows 'strong response.' USS Murphy turned ON AIS beacon — deliberate transparency signal. Mine clearance continues. ISRAEL: Approves updated target list for energy/infrastructure strikes on Iran if diplomacy fails. Iran warns reciprocal attacks on Orot Rabin power plant (25% of Israel's electricity). LEBANON: Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves April 11 — most intense day since war began. Drone struck Shlomi apartment building. IDF 98th Division in direct clashes at Bint Jbeil center. NETANYAHU: Corruption trial resumes — court rejected delay request TWICE. PEZESHKIAN: Phone call with Macron — Iran entered talks with 'determination and seriousness.' OIL: Brent $95.20 (+19.3%), WTI $95.50 (+26.5%). Weekend — Friday close. Gas $4.166/gal.
factCheckNote: NPR, Newsweek, AP, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Fortune, ANI, Times of Israel, YNet, JPost, Deccan Chronicle all confirming.

title: Day 44 PM: Israeli overnight strikes kill 24 in Lebanon (Teffahta 13, Qana 5, Maaroub 6); 3 VLCCs exit Hormuz — biggest oil exit since war; 2 supertankers U-turn after talks collapse; Iran: 'no plan for next round'; Consumer sentiment record low 47.6
description: LEBANON: Israeli overnight airstrikes killed at least 24 across southern Lebanon — Teffahta (13 killed), Qana al-Khashna (5 killed), Maaroub (6 killed). Dawn strikes also hit Aaitat, Samaya, Mahrouna, Maarakeh, and Khiam — 3 wounded. Strikes intensifying after talks collapse. Lebanon death toll now 2,044+. HORMUZ: 3 VLCCs (Serifos, Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai) exited Hormuz — BIGGEST day of oil movement since war began (~6M barrels). Cospearl Lake heading to China with Iraqi oil. BUT 2 empty supertankers (Agios Fanourios I, Shalamar) U-turned immediately after talks collapsed — signal renewed market panic. Monday oil spike expected. TALKS AFTERMATH: Fars News reports Iran has 'no plan for next round of negotiations.' Ghalibaf blamed US for failure. Iran FM: 'ball in America's court.' Ceasefire expires ~April 22. ECONOMY: Consumer sentiment 47.6 — ALL-TIME RECORD LOW (U. Michigan). March CPI 3.3% (gasoline +21.2% MoM — largest since 1967). Goldman: $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month. JPMorgan: $150 if shut into mid-May. OIL: Brent $95.20, WTI $95.50 (Friday close). Gas $4.166/gal.
factCheckNote: Xinhua, The National, TRT World, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NPR, CNBC confirmed.

title: Day 44 EVE: TRUMP ANNOUNCES US NAVAL BLOCKADE OF HORMUZ — 'effective immediately'; Iran: 'Hormuz will not be opened,' warns blockade would 'backfire'; Netanyahu: campaign 'not over,' claims 12 nuclear scientists killed; Ghalibaf: 'we don't trust you'; Pope Leo vigil denounces 'delusion of omnipotence'; Russia gave Iran 55 Israeli energy targets
description: CRITICAL ESCALATION: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.' Ordered Navy to 'interdict every vessel that has paid a toll to Iran' and 'destroy the mines.' Added: 'we are fully LOCKED AND LOADED, and our Military will finish up the little that is left of Iran.' IRAN RESPONSE: Lawmaker on negotiating team: 'The Strait of Hormuz will not be opened.' IRGC vowed to 'deprive Americans and allies of regional oil and gas for years.' Official: blockade would 'disastrously backfire.' No plan for next round of talks. NETANYAHU: Video statement — campaign 'is not over' but 'historic achievements.' Claims 12 nuclear scientists killed. 'They wanted to strangle us, and we are strangling them.' GHALIBAF: Official post-talks statement — 'we have no trust in the opposing side' due to 'experiences of two previous wars.' POPE LEO XIV: Peace vigil at St. Peter's — 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling war. Archbishop of Tehran present — first time during conflict. RUSSIA-IRAN: Ukrainian intel reports Russia provided Iran list of 55 Israeli energy targets including Orot Rabin (25% of Israel's power). PEZESHKIAN: Phone call with Macron — Iran entered talks with 'determination and seriousness,' called Lebanon civilian deaths 'war crimes.' MACRON: Condemned war 'since first day,' urged ceasefire extension to Lebanon. OIL: Brent $95.20, WTI $95.50 (Friday close). Monday spike expected after blockade announcement.
factCheckNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, CBS News, Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera, NPR, Times of Israel, PBS, BusinessToday all confirming.

description: CENTCOM BLOCKADE: US Navy begins enforcing blockade of ALL maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10:00 AM ET. USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln CSGs with 6 destroyers. Will NOT impede non-Iranian port traffic. IRGC warns of 'deadly vortex' — military vessels approaching = ceasefire violation. OIL SURGE: Brent $102.29 (+7.4%), WTI $104.56 (+9.5%) — first time above $100 since blockade escalation. Wholesale gas +6%, European gas +18%. Goldman $100+ call vindicated. BINT JBEIL: IDF 98th Division + Golani enter eastern districts. Givati Brigade raids hospital — ~20 killed inside, ~20 in vicinity. Weapons cache seized. All roads cut off. Netanyahu visits troops: 'The war continues.' Operation officially named 'Silver Plow.' HUMANITARIAN: Red Cross paramedic Hassan Badawi killed by Israeli drone in Beit Yahoun. UNIFIL: IDF tank rams UN vehicles twice near Bayada, spray-paints HQ windows. Qana airstrike kills 5 (3 women), 25 wounded. HORMUZ: 3 VLCCs exit (~6M barrels) — biggest oil movement since war. 2 tankers U-turn on talks collapse. 2,800 ships stranded, 10 crew dead, 20K seafarers stuck. IRAN: Forensic agency identifies 3,375 dead (2,875 men, 496 women). Intel leak: Iran still holds 1,000+ missiles vs Pentagon 'depleted' claims. DIPLOMACY: Israel-Lebanon direct talks set Tuesday at State Dept. Rubio dismisses DNI Gabbard intel on nuclear issue as 'irrelevant.' Lebanon PM Salam cancels US/UN trip.

description: LEBANON FRONT ESCALATES: Hezbollah fired 30+ rockets at Kiryat Shmona, Doviv, Safed — kindergarten hit (empty, 'a miracle'), 2 lightly injured. IDF 98th Division + Givati raid Bint Jbeil hospital: ~40 Hezbollah killed, weapons cache seized, stadium captured. IDF expects full control of Bint Jbeil within days. IDF claims 1,400 total Hezbollah killed since March 2. Lebanon death toll: 2,055 (+35 in past 24h). DIPLOMACY: Israel-Lebanon peace talks set for Tuesday April 15 at State Dept in Washington — first direct engagement. Amb. Yechiel Leiter leads Israeli delegation. TRUMP FOX NEWS: Outlines 5-point demands: (1) dismantle nuclear facilities, (2) hand over 400+ kg enriched uranium, (3) accept security framework, (4) stop funding proxies, (5) open Hormuz with no tolls. 'I predict they come back and give us everything we want. They have no cards.' Confirms minesweepers heading to Hormuz. ALLIES SPLIT: France-UK announce conference 'in coming days' for 'peaceful multinational mission' to restore Hormuz transit — separate from warring parties. Starmer explicitly rejects US blockade. IRAN RESPONSE: Navy chief Shahram Irani calls blockade 'ridiculous and funny.' Army: blockade is 'piracy' — if Iran's ports threatened, no Gulf port will be safe. POPE LEO: Responds to Trump — 'no fear of Trump administration,' peace rooted in Gospel. GERMANY: Merz announces fuel tax cut as EUR 2/litre prices hit households. Growth forecast cut to 0.6%. ISRAEL: New Mossad chief Roman Gofman approved (3-1). INFRASTRUCTURE: Lavan refinery to regain 80% capacity in 1-2 months.

IDF READINESS: Chief of Staff Zamir ordered 'heightened state of readiness' — intelligence expanding target banks, air force preparing strike packages. Pezeshkian condemns Trump's insult to Pope Leo. UN: Guterres warns world 'on the edge of a wider war.' EUROPE: France/UK planning conference for 'strictly defensive' Hormuz mission. title: Day 46 AM: US CENTCOM BLOCKADE TAKES EFFECT; Chinese tanker tests it; Trump 'eliminate' ships; IDF encircles Bint Jbeil (100+ Hezbollah killed); Sgt. Maj. Bianco KIA; 8 IDF wounded by drone; Netanyahu Holocaust speech backs blockade; Vance 'economic terrorism'; Rubio hosts Israel-Lebanon talks; Qassem rejects; Pope Leo 'no fear'; Brent $98.05; Oil pulls back
description: BLOCKADE: US CENTCOM naval blockade of all Iranian ports takes effect 10:00 AM ET (14:00 GMT). At least 15 ships including USS Abraham Lincoln enforcing. ~2,800 ships stranded, 230 oil tankers waiting, 40,000+ seafarers affected. FIRST TEST: Chinese tanker Rich Starry (~250K bbl methanol from UAE) attempts transit. Ostria (Botswana-flagged) reverses course 41 min after deadline. TRUMP: 'Ships will be immediately ELIMINATED.' Claims Iran called wanting deal — Iran hasn't confirmed. BINT JBEIL: IDF 98th Division (Paratroopers, Commando, Givati) completes encirclement of historic Hezbollah stronghold. 100+ Hezbollah killed in CQC and airstrikes. Dozens of infrastructure sites dismantled. Only 'several dozen' fighters remain. IDF CASUALTIES: Sgt. Maj. (Res.) Ayal Uriel Bianco (30, Katzrin, 188th Armored) KIA in southern Lebanon — 13th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since Feb 28, 937th since Oct 7. 3 reservists wounded same incident. 8 more IDF soldiers wounded by explosive Hezbollah drone (2 moderate, 6 light). Hezbollah claims 11 attacks; sirens 15 times across northern Israel. NETANYAHU: Yad Vashem Holocaust speech compares Iran nuclear sites to Nazi camps: 'Had we not acted, Natanz, Fordow might have become synonymous with horror — like Auschwitz.' Rebukes Europe: 'deep moral weakness.' Cabinet meeting: backs Trump blockade, 'constant coordination,' ceasefire 'could turn on a dime.' VANCE: Accuses Iran of 'economic terrorism' for Hormuz closure. 'Two can play that game.' DIPLOMACY: Rubio hosts first direct Israel-Lebanon ambassador talks at State Dept — highest-level since 1993. Lebanon PM Salam cancels trip under Hezbollah pressure. Qassem (Hezbollah): rejects talks, warns of soldier abductions, says group 'quietly rebuilding.' IRGC: warns of 'new capabilities enemy has no idea about,' calls blockade 'piracy.' Pakistan pushing Round 2 US-Iran talks for April 16. POPE LEO: 'No fear of Trump administration.' Begins Africa tour. GUTERRES: 'No military solution,' demands ceasefire preservation. PROTESTS: Thousands rally in Tehran against blockade. IRAN ECONOMY: Central bank warns of 180% inflation, 2M newly unemployed, 12-year recovery. MARKETS: Oil pulls back from April 13 highs — Brent $98.05, WTI $96.57. Gas $4.125 steady. OPEC March production plunged 27% (historic). CPI March 3.3%. S&P 500 erased all war losses.

title: Day 46 PM: Oil plunges Brent $94.87 WTI $91.42 on talk hopes; Italy suspends defense pact; Hezbollah jam-proof drone hits 20+ targets; IDF 'Operation Silver Plough'; Russia evacuates Bushehr; IEA/IMF bombshells; Kallas 'gravest violation since WWII'; Lavrov-China; Gulf states demand blockade end
description: OIL CRASH: Brent plunges to $94.87 (-4.4% from $99.36), WTI to $91.42 (-7.7% from $99.08) — sharpest single-day drop since ceasefire on renewed US-Iran talk hopes. Pakistan pushing Islamabad Round 2 as early as Thursday. Ceasefire expires April 22. ITALY: PM Meloni suspends two-decade military defense cooperation agreement with Israel over 'unacceptable attacks' on Lebanon civilians. Israel summons Italian ambassador. HEZBOLLAH: Deploys new jam-proof optical-guidance drone for first time — resistant to electronic warfare. 40+ drones hit 20+ targets across northern Israel including Karmiel paratrooper base, Golani Division HQ at Shraga, Tefen base. 'Heavy damage' reported. IDF: Defense Minister Katz announces 'Operation Silver Plough' — demolishing all homes in Lebanese border villages for buffer zone. Bulldozing Naqoura. Nabatieh drone strike kills 2. BUSHEHR: Russia evacuates 108 of 128 staff from Iran's nuclear plant. Rosatom: attack could cause 'Chernobyl-level disaster.' Kremlin renews offer to take Iran's enriched uranium. IRAN: UN envoy Iravani sends formal letter calling blockade 'gross violation of sovereignty' under Article 2(4) of UN Charter. Reserves 'inherent right' to self-defense. GULF: Saudi Arabia + 5 Gulf states jointly demand US end Hormuz blockade. 30 tankers near Yanbu within Houthi range. Houthi al-Bukhaiti: considering own Bab al-Mandeb counter-blockade. IEA: Global oil demand forecast slashed — now projects DECLINE of 80K bpd (was +640K growth). March: 10.1M bpd lost = largest oil supply disruption in history. IMF: Cuts global growth to 3.1% (from 3.3%), raises inflation to 4.4%. Adverse scenario: 2.0% growth. KALLAS: 'Gravest violation of international law since WWII.' EU approves new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials. LAVROV: Arrives Beijing for coordination with Wang Yi on Middle East + Ukraine. MOSSAD: Chief Barnea — 'mission not complete until regime falls.' First public call for regime change. SWITZERLAND: Offers Geneva for next round of talks. PROTESTS: ~90 arrested in NYC including Chelsea Manning outside Schumer/Gillibrand offices. CANADA: First Canadian citizen (Mohamad Haidar, 38) killed by Israeli drone in southern Lebanon. GAS: $4.118 (AAA, flat). S&P 500: 6,886 — erased all war losses.
factCheckNote: CNBC, Trading Economics, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Middle East Monitor, Kurdistan24, Moscow Times, PressTV, PBS, Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post, CBC, Newsweek confirmed all items.

title: Day 47 PM: Oil settles Brent $94.79 WTI $91.28; Israel-Lebanon historic DC talks agree to further negotiations; Trump hints Round 2 Iran talks 'in next two days'; US sanctions waiver expires April 19; Xi Jinping first personal statement; S&P 500 erases all war losses; ICRC first aid to Iran; CENTCOM blockade holds; 17-nation coalition backs Lebanon talks
description: OIL: Brent settles at $94.79 (from $94.87 AM), WTI at $91.28 (from $91.42 AM) — afternoon session extends decline on renewed talk hopes. ISRAEL-LEBANON: Historic first direct Israel-Lebanon meeting since 1993, hosted by Secretary Rubio. Israeli Ambassador Leiter and Lebanese Ambassador Moawad agree to begin direct peace negotiations. Hezbollah's Qassem rejects talks as 'futile,' threatens to capture Israeli soldiers. TRUMP: Tells NY Post Round 2 US-Iran talks 'could happen in next two days' in Islamabad. Pakistan proposing second round. Ceasefire expires April 21. SANCTIONS: US Treasury confirms 30-day Iran oil waiver (covering ~140M stranded barrels) will NOT be renewed when it expires April 19. Russia oil waiver also expired April 11. India faces double hit losing Hormuz + Russian crude. CHINA: Xi Jinping makes first personal statement since war began — 'world must not revert to law of the jungle.' Presents 4 proposals for Middle East peace. FM Guo calls blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible.' Lavrov arrives Beijing for talks with Wang Yi. MARKETS: S&P 500 +1.18% to 6,967 — erases ALL Iran war losses. Nasdaq +1.96%. 10Y yield 4.248%. Rally driven by Iran talk optimism despite bad PPI data. HUMANITARIAN: First ICRC aid delivery to Iran since war began — truck convoy from Turkey with trauma kits, tents, medical supplies. 14 trucks from Jordan for 25,000 people. BLOCKADE: CENTCOM Day 2 — Pentagon confirms no ships reached Iranian ports in first 24 hours. 6 merchant vessels U-turned. Chinese tanker 'Rich Starry' transited strait but carried UAE cargo, not Iranian. 17-NATION COALITION: UK-led joint statement from 17 countries urging Israel-Lebanon to 'seize this opportunity.' Notable absences: Germany, Austria, Hungary, Italy. VANCE: Accuses Iran of 'economic terrorism.' Says 'ball is in Iran's court.' US CASUALTIES: 399 WIA confirmed (up from 381). IRGC: Navy warns any military vessel approaching Hormuz 'will be regarded as ceasefire violation.' GAS: $4.118 flat.
factCheckNote: CNBC, NPR, State Department, Bloomberg, Foreign Policy, Euronews, ICRC, Al Jazeera, Motley Fool confirmed all items.
