description: OIL: Brent rebounds to $95.72 intraday (+1.0% from $94.79 settlement), WTI $91.50 (+0.2% from $91.28). Modest uptick on mixed signals — Round 2 talk hopes vs blockade escalation. API reports US crude inventories +6.1M barrels (8th straight build). TRUMP: Fox Business interview aired April 15 — 'I think it's close to over, yeah.' Also: 'If I pulled up stakes right now, it would take them 20 years to rebuild.' Says he is 'not thinking about extending' ceasefire (expires April 21). Hints at Round 2 Pakistan talks 'in next two days.' BLOCKADE DAY 2: US destroyer intercepts 2 tankers attempting to leave Chabahar port (Gulf of Oman). CENTCOM confirms 6 ships turned back in first 24 hours. No shots fired. 5 of 6 were oil tankers. IRAN: Demands $270B war compensation from US, Israel, and 5 Arab states. Spokesman calls figure 'very crude' preliminary calculation. Pezeshkian: blockade 'doomed to failure,' Iran 'not seeking war' but 'will not be forced to surrender.' Interior Minister Momeni: instructs governors to 'neutralize the threat.' ARAGHCHI: Says Tehran came to talks 'in good faith' but was met with 'maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade.' Thanks Oman for 'principled stance.' GUTERRES: 'There is no military solution to this crisis. Serious negotiations must resume.' Calls for ceasefire preservation. XI-LAVROV: Confirmed meeting in Beijing. Lavrov: Russia could 'compensate' for China's energy shortages. Xi vows closer coordination. HEZBOLLAH: 30+ rockets fired at northern Israel (Times of Israel liveblog). 1 man injured. SWITZERLAND: Reiterates offer to host talks in Geneva. MARKETS: S&P futures flat, holding near ATH. GAS: $4.118 flat (AAA).

title: Day 47 PM: Iran threatens Red Sea/Gulf/Oman total shipping blockade; Trump 'permanently opening' Hormuz, claims China no-weapons deal; Pakistan army chief Munir in Tehran; CENTCOM: blockade 'fully implemented' 8 tankers intercepted; Israel strikes kill 13 in Lebanon; Netanyahu 'Greater Israel' vision; Hezbollah 70 rockets; IMF warns global recession; Oil Brent $96.80; S&P 500 breaks 7,000; EIA: Q2 peak $115/bbl
description: IRAN THREAT: Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya commander) threatens to block ALL shipping in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea if US blockade continues: 'The powerful armed forces will not allow any exports or imports to continue.' First time Iran explicitly threatens Red Sea. TRUMP: Posts on Truth Social: 'I am permanently opening the Strait of Hormuz... China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran... President Xi will give me a big, fat, hug.' Tells NY Post Round 2 talks could happen 'over next two days' — praises Asim Munir. MEDIATION: Pakistan army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir + Interior Minister Naqvi land in Tehran with Washington message for mediation push. BLOCKADE DAY 2: CENTCOM announces blockade 'fully implemented' — 8 tankers intercepted (up from 6). Iran's sea trade 'completely halted.' 10,000+ US personnel enforcing. Iranian tanker 2M bbl arrived unannounced near Gujarat, India. LEBANON: Israeli airstrikes kill 13+ in southern Lebanon (Jbaa 4, Ansariyeh 5, Qadmus 4) — one day after historic DC talks. IDF struck 200+ Hezbollah targets. Total: 2,124 killed, 6,921 wounded since March 2. HEZBOLLAH: 70 rockets at northern Israel (Nahariya targeted, 2 injured). 24 separate waves. NETANYAHU: i24 interview endorses 'Greater Israel' vision — 'historic and spiritual mission.' Gofman approved as new Mossad chief. HEZBOLLAH CHIEF: Qassem calls Washington talks 'free concession.' IMF: Cuts global growth to 3.1%, warns severe scenario = 2% ('close call for global recession'). Inflation raised to 4.4%. MENA growth 1.1%. EIA STEO: Brent avg $96/bbl for 2026, Q2 peak $115/bbl. OIL: Brent $96.80 (+2.13% intraday). WTI ~$91-93. S&P 500 breaks 7,000 — erases all war losses. Harvard: $1T long-term cost. RUBIO meets Egyptian FM Abdelatty. Ali Nikzad (Iran parliament): revenge vow for Khamenei. Gas $4.118 flat.

title: Day 47 EVE: Pentagon reveals 150 Iranian ships sunk, 13K targets; Hegseth impeachment filed; Ceasefire extension 'in-principle'; UAE-Iran first call since Feb; 10K+ US troops deploying; S&P 500 record 7,022; Lebanon death toll 2,167; Gas $4.108
description: PENTAGON BRIEFING: SecDef Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Caine reveal Operation Epic Fury statistics — 90%+ of Iran's regular naval fleet sunk (~150 ships at bottom of ocean), 13,000+ targets struck, 80% air defense destroyed, 90% of weapons factories attacked. Hegseth: Iran 'combat ineffective for years to come,' troops 'prepared to restart at a moment's notice.' HEGSETH IMPEACHMENT: Rep. Yassamin Ansari (D-AZ), first Iranian-American Democrat in Congress, files 6 articles of impeachment against Hegseth with 8 co-sponsors. Charges: unauthorized war, targeting civilians, Signalgate, obstruction, abuse of power. Pentagon: 'another charade.' CEASEFIRE: Bloomberg/Fortune report 'in-principle agreement' to extend ceasefire (expires April 21) by 2 weeks. US has NOT formally agreed. 3 sticking points: nuclear program, Hormuz, $270B compensation. Leavitt: 'We feel good about the prospects of a deal.' Vance: 'I feel very good.' UAE-IRAN: First high-level contact since February — VP Mansour bin Zayed calls Parliament Speaker Qalibaf on de-escalation. IRAN rejects UAE-IMO maritime corridor proposal at Legal Committee meeting. Baghaei: 'Iran is not blockadable.' TROOP DEPLOYMENT: WaPo exclusive — US deploying 10,000+ additional troops: USS Bush (CVN-77) CSG (~6,000) + Boxer ARG/11th MEU (~4,200), arriving late April. BLOCKADE DAY 2 UPDATE: 9 tankers now turned back (up from 8). USS Spruance intercepts Iranian-flagged vessel fleeing Bandar Abbas. LEBANON: Death toll rises to 2,167 (WAFA). 3 paramedics killed in 'triple-tap' strike at Mayfadoun. 4 family members killed in Jbaa. Total 20+ killed today. Hezbollah fires 40+ rockets at northern Israel — 1 injured in Tamra. SATELLITE: Imagery shows Iran clearing missile tunnels at Khomein + Tabriz South. US intel: ~50% of Iran's missile launchers remain intact. NETANYAHU: Video — 'too early to say how this will end... prepared for any scenario.' TRUMP-POPE: Trump attacks Pope Leo on Truth Social re: 42,000 protesters. Pope: 'no fear' of speaking out. MARKETS: S&P 500 closes at ALL-TIME record 7,022.95 (+0.8%) — above 7,000 first time ever. Nasdaq record above 24,000 (+1.6%). All war losses erased. OIL: Brent settles ~$95.72, WTI ~$91.50. GAS: $4.108 (down 1¢ from $4.118).

description: HISTORIC DIPLOMACY: Trump announces PM Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun will speak by phone Thursday April 17 — first Israel-Lebanon leader-to-leader call in 34 years. Trump: 'trying to get a little breathing room between Israel and Lebanon.' PAKISTAN NUCLEAR BREAKTHROUGH: Pakistani sources report 'major breakthrough' tied to Iran's nuclear programme. Army Chief FM Asim Munir arrived in Tehran with Interior Minister Naqvi carrying message from Washington. Met FM Araghchi. HEZBOLLAH STRIKES: Drone attacks hit IDF 146th Division logistics base near Nahariya + settlement near Liman barracks. Hezbollah claims 39 military operations in 24 hours. Rocket attack in Bint Jbeil wounds 5 IDF soldiers (1 serious). READINESS: IDF Chief Zamir orders heightened readiness; IAF preparing updated strike packages including Iran. Netanyahu expands Lebanon buffer zone with 5 divisions — 'solid, deeper security zone.' Nuclear demands: enriched material removed, enrichment capability annulled, Straits reopened. BLOCKADE DAY 3: 10 ships turned back, zero breaches. MARKETS: S&P 500 hits 7,033 (+0.14%), extending record. SANCTIONS: Waiver expires April 19 — will NOT be renewed. OIL: Brent $94.89; WTI $91.50. GAS: $4.09 (down 2¢).

title: Day 48 EVE: Trump announces 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire (5 PM ET); US blockade goes global — 13 ships turned back; Hegseth threatens Iran's electrical grid; Qasmiyeh Bridge destroyed; 4 paramedics killed; Iran's Rezaei threatens to sink US warships; Oil $94.89; Gas $4.093
description: CEASEFIRE: Trump announces Israel and Lebanon agreed to 10-day ceasefire starting 5 PM ET (21:00 GMT). First direct Israel-Lebanon diplomatic engagement since 1983. Aoun and Netanyahu invited to White House. Israel says IDF will NOT withdraw from southern Lebanon — will maintain freedom of action against Hezbollah. No confirmation from Hezbollah. BLOCKADE GOES GLOBAL: Gen. Caine (CJCS) announces US Navy will intercept ANY Iranian-flagged vessel anywhere in the world, expanding beyond Hormuz to all oceans. 13 ships turned back rather than challenge blockade. Hegseth: US 'locked and loaded' to attack Iran's energy and electrical power infrastructure if negotiations fail. QASMIYEH BRIDGE: Israeli airstrikes completely destroy last bridge over Litani River — southern Lebanon (~10% of country) fully severed. Lebanese army evacuated checkpoint. PARAMEDICS KILLED: Triple-tap Israeli strikes kill 4 Lebanese paramedics in Mayfadoun near Nabatiyeh — 3 ambulance teams hit in succession. IRAN THREATS: Mohsen Rezaei warns US warships in Hormuz are 'under our missile launchers' and threatens to 'sink them all.' PAKISTAN: Munir meets Iranian Parliament Speaker — 'significantly narrowing differences.' Ceasefire expires April 22. MARKETS: S&P 500 pulls back 0.3% from record. Oil: Brent $94.89 (+18.9%), WTI $91.29 (+20.9%). Gas: $4.093 (+30.4% from pre-war). Sanctions waiver expires April 19.
factCheckNote: Axios, PBS, Bloomberg, Breaking Defense, Stars and Stripes, Al Jazeera, Xinhua, CBC, Newsweek, NBC News confirmed all items.

Israel will NOT withdraw troops — maintains expanded security zone. No Hezbollah confirmation. LAST STRIKES: 8 killed in Nabatieh minutes after Trump's announcement. Hezbollah fired 10 rockets at northern Israel 2 hours before ceasefire — 3 wounded, 2 severely, IDF intercepted 8. WAR POWERS: House resolution to withdraw US forces from Iran failed 214-213. Third failed attempt. Only Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) crossed party lines. War Powers Act 60-day deadline approaching end of April. IMPEACHMENT: House Democrats filed 6 articles of impeachment against Defense Secretary Hegseth — charges include unauthorized war, civilian casualties (Minab girls school), classified info disclosure. IRAN THREATS: IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi threatens to block ALL shipping in Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea. Houthis threaten Bab al-Mandeb closure. DIPLOMACY: Pakistan army chief Munir in Tehran delivering US message. No date set for Islamabad Round 2. Ceasefire expires April 22. Extension 'in principle' but not formal. SANCTIONS: Treasury 'Economic Fury' sanctions 24+ entities in Iranian oil smuggling network. Waiver expires April 19. MARKETS: S&P 500 above 7,000 record. Nasdaq 10-day win streak. Oil: Brent $95.86 (+20.1%), WTI $91.62 (+21.3%). Gas: $4.093 (flat). US crude inventories -9.13M bbl (massive draw). Israeli shekel 30-year high 2.9954/USD. Iran rial all-time low 1.75M/USD. IMF cuts global growth to 3.1%, Middle East to 1.1%. Iran GDP forecast -6.1%.

IRGC gunboats fired on tanker 20 miles NE of Oman (UKMTO confirmed, no injuries). Two more merchant vessels hit by gunfire. Armed Forces spokesperson accused US of 'piracy and maritime robbery.' TRUMP: At Arizona Turning Point rally, claimed Hormuz 'fully open' (already reversed). On Air Force One: 'some pretty good news 20 minutes ago' — declined to elaborate. Warned ceasefire may not extend past Wednesday without deal. Blockade 'remains in full force.' NUCLEAR STALEMATE: Iran FM rejected Trump's enriched uranium handover claim — 'will not be transferred anywhere.' US wants 20-year moratorium, Iran offers 5. ~450kg at 60% enrichment at issue. SANCTIONS: Treasury designated 7 Iran-backed Iraqi militia commanders (Kata'ib Hizballah, Nujaba, AAH, KSS) under EO 13224. Next talks Monday Islamabad.

Lidor Porat, 31, 7106th Battalion, 769th Regional Brigade, killed in combat in southern Lebanon — 9 more soldiers injured (1 severe, 4 moderate, 4 light). title: Day 51 Afternoon: Trump 'NO MORE MR. NICE GUY' — threatens every power plant and bridge; Vance-led delegation to Islamabad Monday; Pezeshkian 'Trump has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights'; Netanyahu-Milei announce 'Isaac Accords'

NICE GUY!' — threatening to 'knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran' if deal rejected, accusing Iran of 'total violation' of ceasefire. Confirmed VP JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner will travel Monday April 20 to Islamabad for Round 2 Iran talks with Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir mediating. Iran's Tasnim reported 'no date set' as US blockade of Iranian ports continues. title: Day 52: USS Spruance seizes Iranian ship Touska — Iran vows retaliation; IRNA declines Pakistan Round 2 talks; Netanyahu demands all enriched uranium leave Iran; ceasefire expires in 2 days
description: NAVAL/ESCALATION: USS Spruance (DDG-111) fired rounds into engine room of Iranian-flagged cargo ship Touska (~900 ft, sanctions-listed for IRGC links) after 6-hour standoff in Gulf of Oman. Marines from 31st MEU boarded and seized vessel — first ship physically taken under US blockade (25 total intercepted). Trump announced on Truth Social, said blockade costs Iran '$500M/day.' Iran Armed Forces (Khatam al-Anbiya) officially confirmed seizure, calling it 'armed piracy' and vowing 'the armed forces will soon respond and retaliate.' DIPLOMACY COLLAPSE: IRNA officially declined Pakistan Round 2 talks, citing 'excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts, repeated contradictions, and ongoing naval blockade.' Yet Ghalibaf touted 'progress' and CNN sources say Iranian delegation may still arrive Tuesday. Witkoff/Vance/Kushner heading to Islamabad. ISRAEL: Netanyahu declared 'all enriched material has to leave Iran — by agreement or through a renewal of the fighting.' Said war 'not over' and Israel ready for resumption. Signed 'Isaac Accords' with Argentine President Milei. OIL: Brent surges to ~$97-98 (+22%), WTI ~$92-94. Gas holds $4.076 (AAA). Ceasefire expires April 22 — 2 days.
factCheckNote: NPR, CNBC, Al Jazeera, ANI News, Axios, NBC News, Times of Israel, Haaretz confirmed all items.

Pentagon FY2027: $1.5T budget — drones tripled to $74B, Tomahawks 14x to 785. EU agrees Hormuz navigation sanctions. OFAC designated 7 individuals, 4 entities, 2 Mahan Air aircraft. MARKETS: Brent $99.81 (+1.4%, +25.0% from pre-war). WTI $90.86 (+1.3%). Gas $4.022 (flat). Gold $4,769.93. IEA: 10.1M bpd supply disruption — largest in history.

Netanyahu: 'Hezbollah is dismantling the ceasefire.' Israel issued evacuation orders for 7 towns north of buffer zone — significant expansion beyond occupied zone. Hezbollah fired 2 rockets + drone at northern Israel (1 rocket intercepted, drone intercepted). description: DIPLOMACY: Iran transmitted new proposal to US through Pakistani mediators — offering to reopen Hormuz and end war while postponing nuclear program talks (Axios scoop, multiple US officials cited). System intercepted dozens of Iranian missiles. Iran fired ~550 BMs/CMs and ~2,200 drones at UAE. NAVAL: CENTCOM reports 38 ships now redirected (up from 37). US mine-sweeping operations continuing in Hormuz with littoral combat ships and Avenger-class minesweepers. UK-France initiative on freedom of navigation. King Charles III arrives Washington for 4-day state visit — Iran on agenda. Bennett-Lapid announce 'Together' party. AVIATION: Iran resumed commercial flights from Tehran (April 25) — first since Feb 28. Flights to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina. MARKETS: Brent $107.97 (+35.2%). WTI ~$96. Gas $4.031 AAA. War Powers May 1 — 3 days.

title: Day 60 — Iran's Hormuz offer rejected; Guterres warns of food emergency; Brent surges to $110
description: Iran offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and halt the war, deferring nuclear talks — Secretary of State Rubio rejected the proposal, insisting any deal must 'definitively prevent' Iran from 'sprinting towards a nuclear weapon.' Trump reviewed the offer with national security advisers but is reportedly 'unhappy.' UN Secretary-General Guterres warned the Hormuz blockade risks a global food emergency, urging parties to 'let ships pass, no tolls, no discrimination.' Brent crude surged to $110.44 (+38.3% from pre-war). Second Hezbollah drone attack wounded IDF soldiers in southern Lebanon (Day 60). German Chancellor Merz said Iran was 'humiliating' the US. Senate Republicans defeated War Powers resolution for 5th time (May 1 deadline in 3 days). Veterans anti-war protest on Capitol Hill — 62 arrested. Pezeshkian: Iran 'will not negotiate under pressure.' Araghchi briefed Putin on Pakistan-mediated process; Putin pledged to 'do everything for peace.' Hezbollah's Qassem rejected direct talks with Israel, set 5 conditions. IEA described Hormuz disruption as 'largest in history.' Goldman Sachs raised Q4 Brent forecast to $90. WTI $97.32. Gas ~$4.06. Hormuz Day 57 effectively closed.

description: MILITARY: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 28 across at least 23 villages in southern Lebanon ('Black Wednesday') — Sultanieh, Qalaouiyeh, Yohmor, Zawtar al-Sharqiya, Meifdoun, Biyyout al-Saiyad, Kunin. Caine briefed Trump on three military options — (1) 'short and powerful' wave of strikes on Iranian infrastructure, (2) ground/naval seizure of part of Strait of Hormuz, (3) special-forces mission to secure ~200 kg HEU at Isfahan. UAE formally exits OPEC effective May 1 (12% of OPEC output, 60-year membership ends). Iranian rial: 1.787M IRR/USD record-low. S&P 500 hit fresh all-time intraday high (best month since 2020). description: CONGRESS: Senate rejected 6th War Powers Resolution 47-50. Sen. Susan Collins (R-ME) broke with GOP for first time: 'The President's authority as Commander-in-Chief is not without limits.' Rand Paul (R-KY) also voted for. War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline arrives May 1. Hegseth claimed ceasefire 'pauses or stops' the clock — legal experts and Sen. Kaine disagree. Sen. Murkowski (R-AK) plans AUMF if no 'credible plan' from White House. MILITARY: IDF Sgt. Liem Ben Hamo (19, Herzliya), Golani Brigade Battalion 13, killed by Hezbollah explosive drone in Qantara, S. Lebanon (2nd drone intercepted). IRGC Aerospace Commander Mousavi threatened strikes on US bases and warships: 'We've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships.' IAEA Chief Grossi: 2015 nuclear deal 'no basis' for new agreement; Iran's enriched uranium 'likely' still at Isfahan; Iran's nuclear program is a 'completely different ballgame' now. Putin warned Trump against resuming attacks, saying ceasefire extension was 'the right' decision. Netanyahu pushed Trump for 2-3 week deadline on Lebanon talks; Israel braces for war resumption 'as early as next week.' DIPLOMATIC: US launched Maritime Freedom Construct (MFC) — joint State Dept/CENTCOM initiative to build international coalition to reopen Hormuz. UK/France leading parallel 30-nation effort. US embassies instructed to approach partner nations by May 1. ECONOMIC: Brent crude pulling back to ~$109 in overnight/May 1 morning trading from $114.01 close. WTI ~$105. UAE OPEC exit now officially in effect (May 1). CBS News: Pentagon true war cost closer to $50B vs official $25B. Hormuz blockade Day 60.
factCheckNote: Time, Maine Public (War Powers 47-50, Collins breaks). JPost, Haaretz, JNS (Sgt. Ben Hamo killed Qantara). Siasat (IRGC Mousavi threat). FP, WaPo (Grossi nuclear). Al Jazeera (Putin warning). JPost, Head Post (Netanyahu Lebanon deadline). Euronews, ABC News (Maritime Freedom Construct). CNBC, CNN (oil pullback). CBS News (true cost $50B). All cross-verified.

Euronews — US seeks maritime freedom coalition

title: Day 63 Late — Trump admin declares war 'terminated' for War Powers; Brent July $111.63; UAE OPEC exit takes effect; Murkowski plans AUMF; Iran excavating buried missiles
description: WAR POWERS: Trump administration declared its Iran war 'terminated' for War Powers Resolution purposes as the 60-day deadline arrived May 1. Senior official: 'The hostilities that began on Saturday, February 28, have terminated.' Legal experts call this 'a sizeable extension of previous legal gamesmanship.' Hegseth previously claimed ceasefire 'pauses or stops' the WPA clock — Sen. Kaine disagreed. CONGRESS: Sen. Murkowski (R-AK) announced she will introduce an AUMF when Senate returns week of May 11 unless White House presents 'credible plan' — working with Sen. Curtis (R-UT). First Republican push for formal authorization. MILITARY: Iran accelerating excavation of buried missiles and munitions during ceasefire — seeking to rebuild drone/missile capability. US officials warn 30-40% of buried stock may be recoverable. Hezbollah fiber-optic FPV drones now daily threat — immune to jamming. Washington Post investigation details new drone warfare tactics adapted from Ukraine. IDF now using netting on vehicles. ECONOMIC: Brent July contract $111.63 (+39.9%), WTI $105.54 (+39.8%). UAE OPEC exit officially takes effect May 1 — frees 1.6M bpd spare capacity but Hormuz limits actual exports. Gas $4.30 national avg (wartime high). California topped $6.01. Hormuz Day 60. CENTCOM: 42 vessels redirected, 41 tankers with 69M barrels ($6B+) blocked.
factCheckNote: KSAT, Business Standard (war 'terminated'). Alaska Public (Murkowski AUMF). Kurdistan24, Iran International (missile excavation). WaPo, Al Jazeera (fiber-optic drones). CNBC (oil prices May 1). AAA (gas $4.30). All cross-verified.

sources.name: KSAT — Trump admin says war 'terminated' before deadline
CNBC — Oil prices May 1 Brent WTI
Kurdistan24 — Iran digging for buried missile launchers

title: Day 63 AM — Hezbollah record 105 attack waves; Israeli strikes kill 16 in Nabatieh; Katz warns Israel may 'act again'; Trump briefed on new military options; WaPo poll: Iraq-era disapproval; Brent $114.01; Gas $4.39
description: MILITARY: Hezbollah launched record 105 attack waves on May 1 — most intense day since war began. Rockets hit Nahariya (1 killed, 14 injured), 10 rockets at Karmiel, 30+ at other northern Israel targets. 2 IDF soldiers lightly wounded by explosive drone. Total attack waves from Lebanon: 1,084. Israeli strikes kill 15-16 in Nabatieh district despite ceasefire — 4 from one family, 6 near village cemetery, Lebanese army soldier killed in Kfar Rumman. DIPLOMATIC: Defense Minister Katz warns Israel may 'act again soon' against Iran — 'set it back years in every field.' Trump briefed on new CENTCOM options: ground deployments to Hormuz islands, SF operation to remove Isfahan enriched uranium, rapid heavy strikes. Trump: 'I don't know that we need it. We might need it.' USS Gerald R. Ford departing after record 309-day deployment — Bush and Lincoln remain. POLITICS: WaPo/ABC/Ipsos poll: war at Iraq-era disapproval. Trump overall approval 34%. 61% disapprove Iran handling. DHS shutdown ends after 75 days. Democrats exploring lawsuit if war continues past May 1 without authorization. ECONOMIC: Brent June settled $114.01 (+42.9%), WTI ~$106 (+40.4%). AAA gas $4.39/gallon (+40.3% from $3.13). Hormuz Day 60. 10B meals/week at risk from fertilizer disruption.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Voice of Emirates (105 attack waves). Manila Times, CNN (Nabatieh 16 killed). ToI, Fox News (Katz 'act again'). Al Jazeera, CNN (Trump military options). WaPo (Ford departing). WaPo/ABC/Ipsos (poll). CBS, PBS (DHS shutdown). CNBC (oil). AAA (gas $4.39). All cross-verified.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 63 Trump signals possible attacks
Washington Post — Poll: Iran war reaches Iraq-era disapproval

title: Day 63 Noon — War Powers deadline expires, Trump declares 'hostilities terminated'; Iran rejects via Pakistan; 14 IRGC killed in Zanjan UXO blast; IDF destroys 40+ Hezbollah sites; USS Ford departing; UAE exits OPEC; Brent retreats to $108.10 on Pakistan talks
description: MILITARY: 60-day War Powers deadline reached without congressional authorization; Trump notifies Congress 'hostilities have terminated.' Sen. Kaine: 'A ceasefire means bombs aren't dropping. It doesn't mean there are no hostilities.' Sen. Collins to introduce AUMF May 11. CENTCOM has now directed 44 commercial vessels to turn around. IDF announced it dismantled 40+ Hezbollah sites in S. Lebanon over 24 hours, seized 7,500 weapons. Israeli airstrikes killed 10+ in S. Lebanon May 1 (4 killed in single vehicle strike). Habboush evacuation order issued near Nabatiyeh. Hezbollah explosive drone wounded 2 IDF lightly at Margaliot; Hezbollah claims strike on IDF vehicle at Al-Bayyada (IDF says intercepted). Lebanon war toll 2,618 killed / 8,094 wounded. 14 IRGC killed and 2 wounded in unexploded ordnance demining accident in Zanjan — largest IRGC death toll since April 7 ceasefire. Iran briefly halted Strait of Hormuz traffic again, citing Israeli Lebanon strikes. USS Gerald R. Ford departing Middle East after 300+ day deployment (longest since Vietnam); USS Lincoln + USS Bush remain. Israel deployed Iron Beam laser, Iron Dome battery (first ever outside US/Israel), and Spectro drone-detection to UAE during war (FT/Haaretz report). DIPLOMATIC: Trump rejects Iran's Pakistan-mediated proposal: 'Either we blast the hell out of them and finish them forever, or we want to try and make a deal.' Calls Iran leadership 'very disjointed.' Briefed by CENTCOM Adm. Bradley Cooper. Pezeshkian Persian Gulf Day speech: blockade 'extension of military operations,' 'intolerable.' Araghchi held FM phone calls with Turkey, Egypt, Qatar, Saudi, Iraq, Azerbaijan. Mojtaba Khamenei written message: foreigners 'have no place except at the bottom of [Gulf] waters.' US Treasury warns shippers facing sanctions if they pay Iran any tolls. State Dept sanctions Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal (12th round under NSPM-2). UAE exits OPEC and OPEC+ effective today. POLITICS: WaPo/ABC/Ipsos poll: 61% say war was a mistake (Vietnam-era disapproval). Hegseth Senate testimony: ceasefire 'pauses' War Powers clock; legal scholars dispute. ECONOMIC: Brent retreats to $108.10/bbl (+35.4%, -$5.91 from $114.01 morning) on Pakistan-mediated talks news; WTI $101.05/bbl (+33.8%). AAA gas $4.392/gallon (+43.1%, slightly off wartime high). Diesel $5.351. Iran exports collapsed to 567K bpd from 1.85M bpd in March. SPR at ~409M; IEA emergency 400M-bbl release ongoing. War risk premiums $10-14M/voyage; Cape of Good Hope reroute adding ~$8B/month industry-wide.
factCheckNote: WaPo, CNN (War Powers deadline). NPR (Republicans defer). Times of Israel (40+ Hezbollah sites, USS Ford, Iron Beam UAE, Pezeshkian, IDF Zanjan). Al Arabiya, Dawn (14 IRGC Zanjan). Press Democrat/AP, WaPo (Lebanon strikes). WSLS (Margaliot drone). Tribune India, Fox News (CENTCOM 44 vessels). State Dept (Qingdao sanctions). WAM, CNBC (UAE OPEC exit). Haaretz (Mojtaba). Mehr News (Araghchi). TradingEconomics, FXLeaders (oil). AAA (gas). 1News (Iran exports). All cross-verified.

title: Trump calls war criticism 'treasonous' at Florida rally
description: At The Villages in Florida, President Trump told supporters it is 'actually, I believe it's treasonous' to say the US is not winning the war with Iran. The remark came hours after his War Powers letter declaring hostilities 'terminated.'
factCheckNote: CNN, The Independent confirmed Trump's 'treasonous' remark at The Villages rally May 1 evening.

title: Tehran air defenses activated against reconnaissance drones
description: Air defense systems fired across Tehran's western, central, and southeastern districts for ~20 minutes against 'small aircraft and reconnaissance drones' per Tasnim and Fars. Situation returned to normal. No attribution. Israeli officials did not comment.
factCheckNote: Newsmax, Arab News, Times of Israel liveblog all confirmed Tehran air defense activation May 1 evening.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Air defenses shooting at drones over Tehran

title: Pentagon orders 5,000 US troops withdrawn from Germany
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth signed an order to withdraw ~5,000 US troops from Germany over 6-12 months, retaliating against Chancellor Merz's criticism that Iran is 'humiliating' the US. The move escalates transatlantic tensions as European allies distance themselves from the war.
factCheckNote: Breaking Defense, Washington Post confirmed Hegseth signed withdrawal order May 1.

sources.name: Breaking Defense — Hegseth orders 5,000 troops out of Germany

title: CNN: 16 US military sites in 8 countries damaged and 'unusable'
description: CNN investigation revealed at least 16 American military sites across 8 Middle Eastern countries were 'destroyed and unusable' from Iranian strikes. Al-Udeid base aviation control center hit twice. Critical radar and comm lines damaged beyond repair. Iran targeted E-3 Sentry AWACS with 'precise, fast strikes.' This represents the majority of US positions in the region.
factCheckNote: CNN exclusive investigation with satellite imagery and official sources. Corroborates earlier NBC News reporting on base damage.

sources.name: CNN — US military bases damaged investigation

title: Day 64 AM: $8.6B arms bypass Congress; blockade costs Iran $4.8B; Hezbollah 4 drones; Trump 'better off' without deal; Pezeshkian-Araghchi rift
description: ARMS SALES: State Dept bypasses congressional review — $8.6B emergency military sales to Israel ($992.4M APKWS), Qatar ($4.01B Patriot + $992.4M APKWS), Kuwait, UAE. BLOCKADE: Pentagon estimates US blockade has cost Iran $4.8B since April 13; 31 tankers with 53M barrels stuck; 40+ vessels redirected. LEBANON: Hezbollah fires 4 drones at northern Israel (Galilee Panhandle, Golan Heights) — all shot down by IAF, did not cross into Israel. May 1 toll expanded: 13 killed across Habboush (8), Zrariyeh (4 inc. 2 women), Ain Baal near Tyre (1 killed, 7 wounded). IRAN INTERNAL: Pezeshkian and Speaker Ghalibaf seek to fire FM Araghchi over IRGC commander Vahidi allegiance — deepening civilian-IRGC power struggle. DIPLOMACY: Trump says US 'better off not making a deal at all' — negotiations 'been going on too long.' Options: 'blast the hell out of them' or deal. MARKETS: Brent ~$108.17 (+35.5%), WTI ~$101.05 (+33.8%). AAA gas $4.39. S&P 500 at all-time high 7,230. Hormuz Day 61.
factCheckNote: Al-Monitor, Axios, Business Standard, Times of Israel, CNN, Washington Examiner, Iran International, Jerusalem Post — all cross-verified.

title: Trump launches 'Project Freedom' — US Navy escorts ships through Hormuz under fire
description: US Central Command launched 'Project Freedom' to escort commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. Two American-flagged ships successfully transited with Navy guided-missile destroyer escorts. Over 100 aircraft, unmanned platforms, and 15,000 service members deployed. Iran immediately fired cruise missiles, drones, and deployed fast boats against the convoy.
factCheckNote: NPR, Navy Times, PBS, Axios all confirmed Project Freedom launch and 2 ships transiting May 4.

title: US Navy sinks 6 Iranian fast boats — first direct US-Iran naval engagement
description: US military used Sea Hawk and Apache attack helicopters to destroy 6 Iranian fast boats targeting civilian ships during Project Freedom. Iran claims the US attacked passenger boats from Khasab (Oman), killing 5 civilians — CENTCOM denies this. Marks first confirmed US-Iran direct combat engagement since the April 8 ceasefire.
factCheckNote: CBS News, Jerusalem Post, CNN, Pentagon briefing (Hegseth/Caine) all confirmed 6 boats destroyed. Iran's civilian claim unverified.

UAE air defenses intercepted most but one drone hit Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (FOIZ), causing major fire. 3 Indian nationals injured. First attack on UAE since April 8 ceasefire. description: South Korean-flagged cargo ship HMM Namu suffered explosion and fire in engine room near Strait of Hormuz. 24 crew (6 Korean nationals), no casualties. Ship towed to Dubai. Cause disputed — Trump says Iran attacked; Seoul investigating sea drone/mine. South Korea subsequently rebuffed Trump's call to join Project Freedom, saying a UN mandate is required.
factCheckNote: Jerusalem Post, Al Arabiya, SCMP confirmed explosion. Attribution to Iran unverified — under investigation by South Korea.

DIPLOMACY: Araghchi heads to Beijing for first China visit since war — calls Project Freedom 'Project Deadlock.' Pakistan mediating talks 'making progress.' MILITARY: Israel and US preparing potential 'short campaign' of new strikes on Iran to pressure negotiations — targets include missile launchers and energy sites. Hormuz Day 63 effectively closed.

IDF disclosed it struck approximately 100 Hezbollah targets across southern Lebanon over the May 16-17 weekend, including the Golani Brigade's weeklong Litani River raid discovering warheads and weapons caches. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 18 killed and 124 wounded in Israeli strikes in the past 24 hours despite the 45-day ceasefire extension — ceasefire effectively a 'ceasefire in name only.' title: Day 81 Late AM: IAEA Confirms Barakah Power Restored; 48–72h Gulf Diplomacy Window Active; Araghchi Admits Enrichment 'Deadlock' at BRICS
description: IAEA confirmed May 19 that external power has been fully restored to Unit 3 of the UAE's Barakah Nuclear Power Plant following the May 17 drone strike — the first confirmed kinetic incident at a nuclear power facility of the war. No radiation release; nuclear safety systems intact. IAEA DG Grossi renewed calls for 'no attacks near nuclear infrastructure.' Diplomatically, the 48–72 hour Gulf-mediated pause window (secured by Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) remains active, with US-Iran talks ongoing through Pakistan. Iranian FM Araghchi acknowledged a 'deadlock' on enrichment duration at the BRICS Foreign Ministers meeting in New Delhi (May 15) — signaling flexibility by suggesting the issue be 'postponed to later stages.' Qatar's FM al-Ansari confirmed negotiations 'need more time.' No new major missile strikes reported since early morning. Oil steady at ~$107–108/bbl; gas $4.515/gal; Hormuz Blockade Day 78.
factCheckNote: IAEA Barakah restoration per Eastern Herald/IAEA May 19. Araghchi enrichment deadlock per Al Jazeera May 15, Tribune India. Qatar FM al-Ansari statement per Al Jazeera liveblog May 19. Oil prices per Reuters/TradingEconomics. Hormuz Day 78 calculated from March 3 blockade start.

title: Day 81 PM: Hezbollah 200-Rocket Nahariya Barrage (1 Killed); IDF Strikes 30+ Hezbollah Sites; Oil Drops to $107.78 After False Sanctions Report
description: Hezbollah fired approximately 200 rockets at northern Israel, killing 1 man and injuring 14 in Nahariya (one seriously). The IDF said it blocked an additional 400 rockets from launching. The Lebanese Health Ministry reported 687 killed and 1,774 wounded across Lebanon in the prior 24-hour period from Israeli strikes — including IDF's 30+ Hezbollah infrastructure strikes in southern Lebanon targeting weapons depots, observation posts, and command nodes. Oil markets whipsawed: Brent briefly spiked to $112/bbl after Iranian semi-official Tasnim News claimed the US had agreed to lift Iran oil sanctions — the US immediately denied the report as 'false,' and Brent settled back to $107.78 (-$3.41 vs morning). WTI held at $107.35. Gas $4.515/gal (AAA, May 19). Trump's earlier 'Clock is Ticking' Truth Social post preceded the Gulf states' intervention to pause the strike. Pezeshkian declared: 'Dialogue does not mean surrender' — Iran will negotiate from 'dignity and authority.' Hormuz Blockade Day 78.
factCheckNote: Hezbollah 200-rocket barrage confirmed by JNS, IDF. Nahariya 1 killed/14 injured per JNS. Oil spike/denial confirmed by Reuters/Investing.com (Brent $112 high, dropped to $107.78). IDF 30+ Hezbollah strikes per JNS, All Israel News, Times of Israel. Pezeshkian quote per PressTV May 18.

title: Day 81: Iran Fires 4 Missile Salvos at Israel; Houthis Declare Haifa Blockade; NSC Meeting Held; Trump Strike on Hold
description: Day 81: Iran launched four separate missile salvos at Israel in early morning hours — all intercepted or landed harmlessly. Houthi spokesman Yahya Saree formally declared a maritime blockade of Haifa port, making all vessels bound for or linked to Haifa 'legitimate military targets.' Trump's NSC Situation Room meeting (Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Caine, Ratcliffe, Witkoff) was held but no new strike orders announced publicly. US-Iran negotiations via Pakistan continued; key sticking point remains enrichment moratorium duration. Oil prices rebounded: Brent $111.19 (+1.77%), WTI $107.35 (+1.52%) after Monday's –1.42%/–1.80% session. Gas $4.515/gal (+42.0% from pre-war). Hormuz Blockade Day 78.
factCheckNote: Iran missile salvos confirmed by IDF/CBS liveblog. Houthi Haifa blockade confirmed by Maritime Executive, Supply Chain Brain, Ship & Bunker. NSC meeting held per Axios, ABC. Oil prices via Fortune/Reuters/TradingEconomics May 19.

title: Day 81 Afternoon: US Seizes Skywave Tanker; Lebanon 3,000 Deaths; Vance 'Locked and Loaded'; IRGC Warns of Retaliation
description: Day 81 afternoon developments: (1) US military boarded and seized the sanctioned Iranian oil tanker Skywave in the Indian Ocean (est. 1M+ barrels of Iranian crude), prompting Iranian FM Araghchi to call it 'a ceasefire breach.' (2) Lebanon's death toll crossed 3,000 since March 2 — 3,042 confirmed killed, 9,273 wounded, ~1M displaced. (3) VP Vance said the US remains 'locked and loaded' to restart the Iran military campaign if nuclear talks fail; Trump warned he was 'an hour away from striking.' (4) An IRGC commander vowed any new US 'aggression will be resolutely thwarted' — Iran 'more prepared and stronger than before.' Gulf-mediated 48-72h diplomatic window active. Oil settled: Brent $111.05 (-0.93%), WTI $103.54 (-1.10%). Hormuz Blockade Day 78.
factCheckNote: Skywave seizure confirmed by PBS NewsHour, Pentagon. Lebanon 3,000 toll confirmed by The National, PBS, NBC. Vance confirmed by Fox News. Oil prices from TradingEconomics May 19.

title: Day 82 Morning: Trump Formally Cancels Iran Strike; Israel Kills 19 in Lebanon; IDF Officer KIA; Hezbollah Drones Hit Israel
description: Day 82 key developments: (1) President Trump formally announced on Truth Social he called off a 'scheduled attack of Iran' after Gulf allies Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE intervened — he had been 'an hour away' from launching a 'full, large scale assault.' Military ordered to remain ready 'on a moment's notice.' A 2–3 day diplomatic window was granted. (2) Israeli airstrikes killed at least 19 in southern Lebanon (May 19), including 3 children and 4 women — strikes hit Deir Qanun al-Nahr (10 killed), Nabatieh (4 killed), and Kfar Sir (5 killed). Israel struck 25+ Hezbollah sites despite the 45-day ceasefire extension. (3) Maj. (res.) Itamar Sapir, 27, of the 551st Brigade was killed in Qouzah when Hezbollah fired from inside a church — the 10th IDF death since the Lebanon ceasefire April 16. (4) A Hezbollah explosive UAV detonated in Israeli territory; a second was intercepted. Hezbollah also fired rockets at IDF troops in violation of the truce. Hormuz Blockade Day 79.
factCheckNote: Trump's Truth Social statement confirmed by NPR, Washington Post, PBS. Lebanon 19 deaths confirmed by Washington Post, Washington Times. Sapir KIA confirmed by Jerusalem Post, JNS. Hezbollah drone confirmed by Times of Israel.

title: Day 82: Xi-Putin Ceasefire Call; CNN: Israel Readying Nuclear Strike Plans; NYT: Ahmadinejad Post-War Plan Leaked; Araghchi Warns of Surprises
description: Day 82 diplomatic and intelligence developments: (1) Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin met in Beijing — Xi said a 'comprehensive ceasefire is of utmost urgency' and that 'resuming hostilities is even more inadvisable.' Trump confirmed Xi personally pledged China would not send weapons to Iran. (2) CNN reported US intelligence shows Israel is readying plans to strike Iranian nuclear facilities (Natanz, Fordow, Arak) if US-Iran diplomacy fails — intercepted communications and munitions movements observed; no final decision made. (3) The New York Times revealed that Israeli-developed post-war plans included a scenario where former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad would be installed as Iran's leader after Khamenei's assassination. (4) Iranian FM Araghchi warned 'a return to war will feature many more surprises,' signaling Iran's readiness to escalate beyond Phase 1. Iran had submitted a new 14-point revised proposal to the US via Pakistan mediator. Oil: Brent $111.15 (+39.2%), WTI $103.58 (+37.2%). Gas $4.533. Hormuz Blockade Day 79.
factCheckNote: Xi-Putin summit confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog, Gulf News, Al Jazeera. CNN nuclear strike report confirmed by Times of Israel. NYT Ahmadinejad report referenced in Times of Israel May 20 liveblog. Araghchi warning confirmed by Al Jazeera May 20 liveblog.

title: Day 82 (Late): Trump Sets Friday-Sunday Deadline; Araghchi: 'Cannot Trust Americans'; Netanyahu Calls Security Cabinet; Iran Releases US Prisoner
description: Day 82 late-morning developments: (1) Trump spoke to reporters outside the White House, saying the war 'is going to end very quickly' and Iran is 'desperate' for a deal — predicting oil prices will 'plummet' after an agreement. He specified his 2–3 day deadline as 'maybe Friday, Saturday, Sunday — maybe early next week.' (2) Iranian FM Araghchi issued his most explicit trust-breakdown statement of the conflict: 'We cannot trust the Americans at all. Every day brings a different message, sometimes even two different messages in a single day, which deepens mistrust. The lack of trust in the US is the main obstacle to any diplomatic effort.' (3) PM Netanyahu convened Israel's security cabinet and spoke with Trump, maintaining that the war is 'not over' until Iran's enrichment sites are dismantled. (4) Iran released US permanent resident Shahab Dalili from 10-year Evin Prison sentence — widely seen as goodwill gesture. (5) G7 Finance Ministers in Paris pledged fiscal restraint; US Treasury Secretary Bessent pushed G7 allies to 'disrupt Iran's global financing networks.' Brent $111.22 (+39.3%), WTI $103.58 (+37.2%). Gas $4.555. Hormuz Blockade Day 79.
factCheckNote: Trump 'very quickly' quote confirmed by CNBC. Araghchi distrust quote confirmed by ABC News live updates. Netanyahu security cabinet confirmed by Times of Israel. Dalili release confirmed by HRANA and Iran International. G7 Bessent push confirmed by Bloomberg.

title: Day 83 AM: Trump 'Hour Away' from Strike; Netanyahu Clash Over Iran Deal; IRGC 'War Beyond Region'; PGSA Controlled Maritime Zone; Pakistan Mediation Active
description: Day 83 opens with the conflict at peak diplomatic tension. (1) Trump told reporters on May 20 evening he was 'an hour away from making the decision to go' before Gulf states intervened — Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and UAE requested 2-3 more days for negotiations. Trump confirmed: 'He's fine, he'll do whatever I want him to do' after a 'lengthy and dramatic' call with Netanyahu in which the two clashed sharply — Netanyahu wanted to resume the war while Trump pursued a 30-day letter of intent via Qatar-Pakistan mediation. (2) IRGC issued a formal statement threatening to extend conflict 'far beyond the region' and crush the US/Israel with 'devastating blows' if attacks resume. The IRGC claimed it was repositioning military assets abroad. Iran's army warned of 'new fronts with new equipment.' (3) Iran's PGSA formally declared a 'controlled maritime zone' at the Strait of Hormuz — all vessels must register, pay, and receive permits; US and Israel permanently excluded. Iran claims 26 vessels transited in coordination with IRGC Navy in the past 24 hours. (4) Iranian FM spokesman Baghaei confirmed on May 21 that message exchanges with the US via Pakistan 'are continuing' — Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi in Tehran for two-day mediation with Pezeshkian, Momeni, and Ghalibaf. Iran demands frozen asset release and end to 'maritime piracy.' (5) Oil markets: Brent fell further to ~$105.54, WTI ~$101.85 on diplomatic optimism from the Gulf-mediated pause. Gas steady at $4.555/gal. Hormuz Blockade Day 80.
factCheckNote: Trump 'hour away' confirmed by CBS News, Time, Arab News. Netanyahu-Trump clash confirmed by Axios, ABC17, Jerusalem Post. IRGC 'beyond region' confirmed by IranWire, CNBC, Times of Israel. PGSA 'controlled maritime zone' confirmed by Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, GMA News. Pakistan mediation confirmed by Xinhua May 21, Arab News. Oil prices from TradingEconomics May 20-21.

title: Day 82 (PM): Israel Kills 8 in Lebanon; Senate Advances War Powers 50-47; US Boards Iranian Tanker; Trump at Coast Guard; Oil Falls 6-7%
description: Day 82 afternoon developments: (1) Israel launched airstrikes across southern Lebanon killing at least 8 people — 5 in Doueir (Barakeh neighborhood), 2 in Tibnin near a hospital, and 1 in Burj Shemali by drone. Hezbollah claimed 26 counterattacks. (2) The Senate advanced a war powers resolution 50-47, with four Republican senators (Collins, Murkowski, Paul, Cassidy) breaking with Trump — the first successful procedural advance in eight attempts, representing a significant domestic constraint signal. (3) US forces boarded the Iranian oil tanker M/T Celestial Sea in the Gulf of Oman, the fifth such boarding since the blockade began. (4) Trump spoke at the Coast Guard Academy commencement: 'The only question is, do we go ahead and finish it up or are they going to be signing a document?' (5) Oil markets fell sharply — Brent to $110.34 (–$2.59) and WTI to $97.33 (–6.55%) — as Trump's confirmed strike cancellation fueled diplomatic optimism; three supertankers exited Hormuz for the first time since May 4. (6) Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf accused the US of carrying out 'overt and clandestine movements showing it has not abandoned its military objectives and is seeking to start a new war.'
factCheckNote: Lebanon strikes confirmed by Al Jazeera. Senate 50-47 vote confirmed by CBS/NBC/WaPo. M/T Celestial Sea boarding confirmed by WSLS. Trump CGA speech confirmed by TurnTo10/C-SPAN. Oil price drop confirmed by Fortune/fxdailyreport. Ghalibaf statement from Fox News Day 82 liveblog.

title: Day 83 (Morning): Pezeshkian 'Dialogue Is Not Surrender'; Pakistan Mediation Ongoing; Oil Brent $105.54; Hormuz Day 80
description: Day 83 morning update: Iranian President Pezeshkian addressed the public with defiant-yet-diplomatic language, declaring 'Forcing Iran to surrender through coercion is nothing but an illusion' and 'We will never bow our heads before the enemy — if talk of dialogue arises, it does not mean surrender or retreat.' He affirmed 'All paths to a diplomatic solution remain open from our side.' Iran's Foreign Ministry confirmed message exchanges with the US via Pakistan are 'continuing' — Pakistani Interior Minister Naqvi is in Tehran for a two-day mediation mission meeting with Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf, and Momeni. Oil continues its diplomatic-optimism slide: Brent ~$105.54/bbl (-32.2% from war highs, +32.2% vs pre-war), WTI ~$101.85. Gas steady at $4.555/gal. Hormuz Blockade Day 80. No new strikes confirmed overnight.
factCheckNote: Pezeshkian statements confirmed by Al Jazeera May 21 liveblog. Pakistan mediation confirmed by Xinhua. Oil prices from TradingEconomics.
